<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144927 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00578: Import tool

- page_id: `189144927`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144927/Import+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:21.742+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1175109811

1175109813

1175109812

Import tool is a module of functions that allows importing documents to be in generated reports. Import tool enables you to dynamically import whole or parts of documents into reports, giving you greater flexibility when generating reports that require dynamic resources. You can now include documents whose location is only known at the actual translation time.

The Import tool allows you to:

- Parse and import RTF , DOCX, HTML , and text templates from any location in the file system
- Reuse the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard custom language' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard custom language' space=''] syntax to import any part of a document. The Import tool uses the predefined syntaxes #sectionBegin and #sectionEnd , introduced with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard custom language' space=''] .

1175109810

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Import tool enables you to dynamically import whole or parts of documents into reports, giving you greater flexibility when generating reports that require dynamic resources. You can now include documents whose location is only known at the actual translation time.</p><p>The Import tool allows you to:</p><ul><li>Parse and import <em>RTF</em>, <em>DOCX, HTML</em>, and text templates from any location in the file system</li><li>Reuse the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#sectionBegin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> and <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#sectionEnd]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> syntax to import any part of a document. The Import tool uses the predefined syntaxes <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> and <strong>#sectionEnd</strong>, introduced with the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#includeSection Directive]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c24317f-6306-485b-83a6-79ca6155bab8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109810</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="02c30e2f-93e2-4da8-9cd7-9b065513f43f" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00579: Import usage

- page_id: `189144929`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144929/Import+usage
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:21.829+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Import tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains the examples of usage of the main and child templates. These examples allow you to create documents as the main template and reuse the content of child templates into it.

#### Preparatory Step

Define a child template that you will use as in Examples 1, 2, and 3. This child template contains the header line and named sections (**Section A** and **Section B**). Type the following lines in the document that you want to define the child template:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 1

This example shows how to statically include the complete child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include it, call the **$import.include('child.docx')** method. Note that there is no specified path. This path to the child template shows you that the main and child templates reside in the same directory. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include all of its content to the report:

```text

```

The output right below will include the entire text from the main template (right above) and from the child template minus the template directives. The included content will be then parsed and stripped of all velocity directives. The output is as follows:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 2

This example shows how to statically include a section of the child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include the section named **Section A** from the child template, call the **$import.includeSection('templates/child.rtf', 'Section A')** method. In this example, the main and child templates reside in different directories, which means that the child template will reside in a subdirectory called *templates*. This *templates* directory will thus reside in the same directory as the main template. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in **Section A** to the report:

```text

```

The output will be:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 3

This example shows how to dynamically include the section of a child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. Before dynamically including the section of the child template named **Section B**, set a variable for the file location of the child template **[#set ($child = "***C:/ImportTool/child.rtf***")]** and another one for the section name to be included**[#set ($section = "Section B")]**. To include the **Section B** section from the child template, call the **$import.includeSection($child, $section)** method. In this example, the child's template location is provided by an absolute path. Note that dynamic values must not be specified in a pair of quote marks, otherwise the section will not be included. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in **Section B** to the report:

```text

```

The output will be:

```text

```

The Import tool supports *DOCX, RTF, HTML, XML*, and*text* files only.

#### Usage in Example 4

This example shows how to include a document as an Attached File element in a project. First, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files' space=''] element in a project. The **File** property will then be used as a parameter to make a reference to the Attached File element. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include the content of the Attached File element to the report:

```text

```

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Including a document as an Attached File element in a project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This child template contains the header line and named sections (<strong>Section A</strong> and <strong>Section B</strong>). Type the following lines in the document that you want to define the child template:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5fc47c3-5cca-4eaa-b170-f0b958c676e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the child template.

#sectionEnd

This is Section B.
#sectionEnd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 1</h2><p>This example shows how to statically include the complete child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. This is Section B.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 2</h2><p>This example shows how to statically include a section of the child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. In this example, the main and child templates reside in different directories, which means that the child template will reside in a subdirectory called <em>templates</em>. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in <strong>Section A</strong> to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ce50327-1838-4616-b129-4f4d56003298"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
This is the 2nd main template
Include Section A
$import.includeSection('templates/child.rtf', 'Section A')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output will be:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32e6166f-cb4b-4901-a003-982722bd78e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the 2nd  main template
Include Section A
This is Section A.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 3</h2><p>This example shows how to dynamically include the section of a child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. Before dynamically including the section of the child template named <strong>Section B</strong>, set a variable for the file location of the child template <strong>[#set ($child = &quot;</strong><em>C:/ImportTool/child.rtf</em><strong>&quot;)]</strong> and another one for the section name to be included<strong> [#set ($section = &quot;Section B&quot;)]</strong>. To include the <strong>Section B</strong> section from the child template, call the <strong>$import.includeSection($child, $section)</strong> method. In this example, the child's template location is provided by an absolute path. Note that dynamic values must not be specified in a pair of quote marks, otherwise the section will not be included. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in <strong>Section B</strong> to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d10683bc-cf02-44d9-ab7a-35406d92e52a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
This is the 3rd main template
Include Section B
#set ($child = "C:/ImportTool/child.rtf")
#set ($section = "Section B")
$import.includeSection($child, $section)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output will be:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be15115f-a695-4e24-9cb6-10022f943c67"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[This is the 3rd main template
Include Section B
This is Section B.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e9e5e3b-600a-4d8a-b607-5ba5ccd20fdc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Import tool supports <em>DOCX, RTF, HTML, XML</em>, and<em> text</em> files only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Usage in Example 4</h2><p>This example shows how to include a document as an Attached File element in a project. First, you need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create an Attached File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> element in a project. The <strong>File</strong> property will then be used as a parameter to make a reference to the Attached File element. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include the content of the Attached File element to the report:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eade2b34-65c8-4540-8454-0bc694a8a8ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')
#foreach($included in $AttachedFile)
	#if($included.file == "included.docx")
		$import.include($included)
	#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Attach File Property.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Including a document as an Attached File element in a project.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145037 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00580: Importing a template to modeling tool using Report Wizard

- page_id: `189145037`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145037/Importing+a+template+to+modeling+tool+using+Report+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:46:08.376+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Creating Your First Template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1356305390

1356305392

1356305389

**On this page**

3

1356305392

#### Importing a template to the modeling tool

Once you have created a template, you will need to import it into the modeling tool by using Report Wizard.

To import a template into a modeling tool using Report Wizard

1. Click Tools > Report Wizard on the main menu.
2. Click the **New** button in the **Select a report template** wizard. The **New Template** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Opening the Report Wizard dialog and creating a new custom template by clicking the New button.
3. In the **New Template** dialog, type the name and description. Select a category, and then click**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Entering the new template name and description, and then selecting a template file in the New Template dialog.
4. Find the template file that you have created, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Select_button.png'] .
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_button.png'] to import the template.

To generate a report based on a template, select the template (in this example, **My template)**from the template list, and follow the steps as instructed by the wizard. See [Report Wizard User Guide](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Report+Wizard) for more information.

#### Glossary

- Report template Ready-made report configurations that you can use to quickly create a specific type of report in Report Wizard.

#### More information about working with templates and reports

For more information about working with templates and reports, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Template Management Pane' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default templates' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Data Management Pane' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85e293df-3081-4921-82d9-af55eb14dc98"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1356305390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25cdf189-a96c-4bef-9288-014b54a8324b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1356305392</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fae8f6e1-8872-46b1-9d01-4b935e03e17a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1356305389</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cdabee79-46ea-4ddc-9bdb-2a2d0d8b5898"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8bdf3b9-0ed7-4a04-8a67-ad1fc80f9a78"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1356305392</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>Importing a template to the modeling tool</h2><p>Once you have created a template, you will need to import it into the modeling tool by using Report Wizard.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import a template into a modeling tool using Report Wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong> on the main menu.</li><li><p>Click the <strong>New</strong> button in the <strong>Select a report template</strong> wizard. The <strong>New Template</strong> dialog will open.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="newTemplate.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Opening the Report Wizard dialog and creating a new custom template by clicking the New button.</h6></li><li><p>In the <strong>New Template</strong> dialog, type the name and description. Select a category, and then click<strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="button-1.png" /></ac:image></strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="New Template Dialog" ac:alt="New Template Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-template-a.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Entering the new template name and description, and then selecting a template file in the New Template dialog.</h6></li><li>Find the template file that you have created, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select_button.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image><strong> </strong>to import the template.</li></ol><p>To generate a report based on a template, select the template (in this example, <strong>My template) </strong>from the template list, and follow the steps as instructed by the wizard. See <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Report+Wizard">Report Wizard User Guide</a> for more information.</p><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Report template</strong><br />Ready-made report configurations that you can use to quickly create a specific type of report in Report Wizard.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with templates and reports</h2><p>For more information about working with templates and reports, visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Template Management Pane" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Default templates" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Data Management Pane" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating Reports from Report Wizard" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141941 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00581: Importing Blocks and their properties

- page_id: `189141941`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141941/Importing+Blocks+and+their+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:32.971+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets say, we need to import the engine structure including Blocks, their Part and Value Properties. Firstly, open an existing or create new project. In this case, we create new project. Next, do the following:

1. 
2. 
3. 
4. 

###### Importing Blocks

To import Block properties, the an *Engine* and *Engine Type* Blocks must be created in the model. In this case, we import those blocks. For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify the following options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Block as Element Type option value.
3. Specify the location of imported elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space=''] by selecting the Target Scope option value. In this case, the Structure Package is selected.
4. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 1.
5. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
6. Click Import . The Engine and Engine Type Blocks are created in the model. [ATTACHMENT filename='importing_engine_blocks.png']

###### Saving properties Import Maps

After the Blocks are imported, their all properties can be imported at once by creating a Map Group which consists of 3 Import Maps: *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties*. Follow the procedures below to save Import Maps.

The *Engine* Block is decomposed to *Engine Type* Part Property. Let's create an Import Map for the Part Property. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify the following options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Part Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select the Engine Block as the Target Scope option value.
4. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 2.
5. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
6. Click Save Map .
7. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
8. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Part . Click OK > Close . The Engine Part Import Map is saved. [ATTACHMENT filename='saving_engine_parts_import_map.png']

The *Engine* Block contains *serialNum* and *year*Value Properties. To save this Import Map, in the already opened [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Value Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select Name and Type properties as Properties to Map option value.
4. Select the Engine Block as the Target Scope option value.
5. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 3.
6. Create mapping between element property Name, Type and excel table cell Name, Type.
7. Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.
8. Click Save Map .
9. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
10. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Value Properties . Click OK > Close . The Engine Value Properties Import Map is saved.

Also, the E*ngine Type* Block contains *make*, *model*, *hp*, and *fuel***Value Properties. To save this Import Map, in the already opened [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Value Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select Name and Type properties as Properties to Map option value.
4. Select the Engine Type Block as the Target Scope option value.
5. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.
6. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
7. Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.
8. Click Save Map .
9. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
10. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Type Value Properties . Click OK > Close . The Engine Type Value Properties Import Map is saved.

###### Creating Map Group

After, the properties Import Maps are prepared, let's create the *Engine Properties Import* Map Group and add the following Import Maps: *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties.* For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups.**In the **Manage Map Groups** dialog, do the following:

1. In the **Tree** tab, select the *Excel/CSV imports* Package as the owner for the new Map Group.
2. Click the **Create** button.
3. In the Specification window, type thename *Engine Properties Import* for the new Map Group and click **Close**. The newly created Map Group is selected as the Select Map Group for Editing option value.
4. Press Ctrl and in the Tree tab, select the *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties* Import Maps.
5. C lick [ATTACHMENT filename='add_import_map.png'] to add them to the *Engine Properties Import* Import Map.
6. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

###### Importing Map Group

Finally, import an *Engine Properties Import* Map Group. For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map Group.**In the **Select Map Group** dialog, select the *Engine Properties Import* Map Group and click **OK**. All properties are created for the *Engine* and *Engine Type* Blocks at once.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145968 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00582: Importing custom Jython libraries

- page_id: `189145968`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145968/Importing+custom+Jython+libraries
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:42:07.657+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Macro Engine Documentation > Supported scripting languages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to import custom Python libraries.

To import and use a custom *.py* file

1. Create a Lib directory in the <tool_installation_directory>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib\engines\jython directory.
2. Add the custom .py file to the Lib directory.
3. To use the custom *.py* script, execute the following (e.g. *myscript.py* is the name of the *.py* file):

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to import custom Python libraries.</p><p>To import and use a custom <em>.py</em> file</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <em>Lib</em> directory in the <em>&lt;tool_installation_directory&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib\engines\jython</em> directory.</li><li>Add the custom <em>.py</em> file to the <em>Lib</em> directory.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To use the custom <em>.py</em> script, execute the following (e.g. <em>myscript.py</em> is the name of the <em>.py</em> file):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="114a7d2c-f334-462d-a92c-d86e3afd2bb9"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import myscript
myscript.calculate(1, 5)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145125 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00583: Importing custom tool to template

- page_id: `189145125`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145125/Importing+custom+tool+to+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:28.741+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard uses the import directive for importing a Custom Tool to a template.

An example of a directive:

```text

```

The import directive declares that the template uses the custom tool, names the tool that defines it, and specifies its tag prefix before the custom tool is used in a template page. You can use more than one import directive in a template, but the prefix defined in each template page must be unique.

#### Attributes

**name** (type:String) 
 The uniform or full qualified class name that locates the Tool class, for example, mytool.HelloTool

**prefix** (type:String) 
 The prefix that precedes the custom tool name such as a string in $hello.getHello(). Empty prefixes are not allowed. When developing or using custom tools, do not use tag prefixes such as bookmark, sorter, template, file, array, group, map, iterator, list, date, report, exporter, profiling, and project, as they are reserved by Report Wizard.

The code fragment indicated below declares a custom tool.

```text

```

The output from the above template is:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard uses the import directive for importing a Custom Tool to a template.</p><p>An example of a directive:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd50b953-d474-470e-a26b-45c8eefbac29"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('prefix', 'name')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The import directive declares that the template uses the custom tool, names the tool that defines it, and specifies its tag prefix before the custom tool is used in a template page. You can use more than one import directive in a template, but the prefix defined in each template page must be unique.</p><h2>Attributes</h2><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>name</strong> (type:String)<br />            The uniform or full qualified class name that locates the Tool class, for example, mytool.HelloTool</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>prefix</strong> (type:String)<br />            The prefix that precedes the custom tool name such as a string in $hello.getHello(). Empty prefixes are not allowed. When developing or using custom tools, do              not use tag prefixes such as bookmark, sorter, template, file, array, group, map, iterator, list, date, report, exporter, profiling, and project, as they are reserved                by Report Wizard.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The code fragment indicated below declares a custom tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a6f2762-f7a5-40ea-9382-07cadf641971"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('hello', 'mytool.HelloTool')
Get Hello
$hello.getHello()
Get Hello Foo
$hello.getHello('foo')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output from the above template is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfe379ac-2d2c-4a4a-98ff-bd3a4fbdaf5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Get Hello
Hello World
Get Hello Foo
Hello foo]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141902 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00584: Importing data from Excel or CSV files

- page_id: `189141902`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141902/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:31.474+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

43

INLINE

##### Introduction

You can import data from Excel (*.xls*and*.xlsx*) or*CSV*(*.csv*and*.txt*) format files to your model. You are able to specify the import mapping options between file columns and element properties of the model. Additionally, you can save your maps, collect them in groups, manage those groups, and use an entire group instead of a single map for data import. Before importing data from Excel or CSV files to model, create new or open an existing project.

You can choose the following import commands:

- 
  - Import Using New Map - to import data from Excel or CSV file by specifying new mapping options and/or save an Import Map. >]]> [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
  - Import Using Existing Map - to import data from an Excel or CSV file by selecting a previously saved map. >]]> [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
  - **Import Using Existing Map Group** - to import data from Excel or CSV file by selecting the map group that collects as many Import Maps as you added. 
>]]>
  - Manage Map Groups - to create, delete or change the order of the m ap groups. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Map Groups' space='']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All Excel/CSV file import commands from the File menu.

##### Preparing Excel or CSV file to import

To import data from Excel or CSV files, the files must met the following prerequisites:

- Heading is located in the first row.
- When creating relationships and connectors, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contains the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are:
  - the client and supplier of the relationship.
  - the roles of the connector ends A and B.
- When creating connectors, the spreadsheet may optionally contain the owner column. The owner of the connector is determined in the following ways:
  - If the owner property is mapped, the connector is imported to the mapped owner.
  - If there is no owner property, the connector is imported to the element that owns the source and target elements indicated in the file.
    - If no such element exists in the model, the connector is imported to the owner of the element that is indicated as the source in the file.
  - If no qualified owner is found, the element selection dialog opens to select the owner for the connector.

Recommended prerequisites:

- Eliminate merged cells.
- Every column has a unique column name.
- Every row has the same number and name of columns.
- Every cell has the element name or qualified element name.

##### Importing data by using a new map

To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using a new map

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure: 
 - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. How to >>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
4. Select the file and specify mapping options. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea) You must select the file in the **Select Excel/CSV file** areaand specify the**Element Type**,**Properties to Map,**and **Target Scope** mapping option values to import data.
5. In the Mapping area, create mapping between the Excel/CSV file data and modeling tool properties. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
6. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
7. Click the Import button. The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.

##### Importing data by using an existing map

To import data from Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure: 
 - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. How to >>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map .
4. In the **Select Import Map** dialog, select existing Import Map. If you cannot see any Import Maps, you need to save it first. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
5. Click OK . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
6. (Optional) Select the file, change mapping options. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea)
7. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to override the selected Import Map or create a new one. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
8. Click the Import button. The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.

##### Importing data by using an existing map group

To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map group

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure: 
 - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. How to >>- the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map Group .
4. In the **Select Map Group** dialog, choose the map group. If you cannot see any map group, you need to create it first. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Map Groups' space='']>]]>
5. Click **OK**. 
The data from Excel or CSV files are imported to the model according to Import Maps and their order in the Map Group. The import of a map group can fail because of the following reasons:the Excel or CSV file is missing;the element type does not exist in the model;properties do not exist in the selected element;the target scope does not exist in the model.

**Related pages**

****

- [Sync with Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139740 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00585: Importing data from other UML tools and formats

- page_id: `189139740`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139740/Importing+data+from+other+UML+tools+and+formats
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:14.976+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: ['importing-uml', 'importing-data']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you need to open a model of a different format or created with another UML tool, you have to import it to the modeling tool instead of simply opening it. The modeling tool allows importing projects from the following file formats:

- Another .mdzip project
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']
- UML 2.1/2.5 XMI File
- MagicDraw Native XML File
- MOF XMI File
- CA ERwin Data Modeler v7.x
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing ReqIF files' space='CRMP2024xR2']
- OWL Ontology File
- Eclipse UML2 (v2.x, v3.x, v4.x, v5.x) XMI file
- XPDL File
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FMU import' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulink import' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']
- Modelica Library

- Enterprise Architect UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 File. Data can be imported from an Enterprise Architect 7.1, 7.5, or 8.0 file. Please note that Enterprise Architect does not export 100% standard UML 2.1 XMI, and this causes some data loss during the import.

- Use[Sodius](https://www.sodiuswillert.com/en/home)to import data from the following products:
  - Publisher for Rational Software Architect
  - Publisher for System Architect
  - Publisher for Rhapsody

- UML diagrams from Microsoft Visio using XMI files. Microsoft Visio 2007 and earlier requires extra add-ons for exporting UML model to XMI files.

To import an external model

1. Select File > **Import** From .
2. Select a file format you want to import a model from.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you need to open a model of a different format or created with another UML tool, you have to import it to the modeling tool instead of simply opening it. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The modeling tool allows importing projects from the following file formats:</span></p><p><br /></p><ul><li>Another .mdzip project</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>UML 2.1/2.5 XMI File</li><li>MagicDraw Native XML File</li><li>MOF XMI File</li><li>CA ERwin Data Modeler v7.x</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CRMP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Importing ReqIF files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Requirements Interchange Format (ReqIF) File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>OWL Ontology File</li><li>Eclipse UML2 (v2.x, v3.x, v4.x, v5.x) XMI file</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">XPDL File</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="FMU import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FMU File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Simulink import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Simulink File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Modelica Library</span></li></ul><ul><li><p>Enterprise Architect UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 File. Data can be imported from an Enterprise Architect 7.1, 7.5, or 8.0 file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e76de8f-6f1d-46d0-a283-b9757cf03d8c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Please note that Enterprise Architect does not export 100% standard UML 2.1 XMI, and this causes some data loss during the import.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use </span><a href="https://www.sodiuswillert.com/en/home">Sodius</a><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> to import data from the following products:</span><br /></span><ul><li>Publisher for Rational Software Architect</li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Publisher for System Architect</span></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Publisher for Rhapsody</span></p></li></ul></li></ul><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">UML diagrams from Microsoft Visio using XMI files.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55b087f9-d2c1-461d-b087-fd7b5f19607d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Microsoft Visio 2007 and earlier requires extra add-ons for exporting UML model to XMI files.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To import an external model</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">File</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><strong>Import</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">From</strong>.</li><li>Select a file format you want to import a model from. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139755 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00586: Importing Diagrams

- page_id: `189139755`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139755/Importing+Diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:24.454+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Enterprise Architect Import allows you to import diagrams. The imported diagram information includes:

33

##### Geometry Properties

The geometry properties imported to a UML model are:

- Positions on a diagram (for shape elements)
- Width and height (for shape elements)
- Path break points (for link elements)

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Other display properties can override geometry information. For example, if an imported element width is shorter than the required width to display text on the element, the width will be adjusted automatically.

##### Color Properties

Color properties will be imported along with the diagrams to a modeling tool developed by NoMagic. Each color property has a different name in a modeling tool. The following table shows color properties mapping.

| EA | Modeling tool by NoMagic |
| --- | --- |
| Background | Fill color |
| Border color (for shape element) | Pen color |
| Font color | Text color |
| Line color (for link element) | Pen color |

##### Display Properties

Display properties in EA can be categorized into three groups: shape, link, and diagram. Only those properties corresponding to the modeling tool will be imported. For example, the **Show Diagram Details**property in EA will be imported as the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table with diagram information' space='']**Show Diagram Info**property in a modeling tool developed by NoMagic.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Each color property has a different name in a modeling tool. The following table shows color properties mapping.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 32.1145%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 64.0199%;" /><col style="width: 35.9801%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>EA</th><th><span>Modeling tool by NoMagic</span></th></tr><tr><td>Background</td><td><p>Fill color</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Border color (for shape element)</p></td><td><p>Pen color</p></td></tr><tr><td><span>Font<span> </span>color</span></td><td><p>Text color</p></td></tr><tr><td><span>Line color (for link element)</span></td><td><p>Pen color</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Display Properties</h3><p>Display properties in EA can be categorized into three groups: shape, link, and diagram. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141951 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00587: Importing instances

- page_id: `189141951`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141951/Importing+instances
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:33.435+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets say, we need to import the nested instances with slots in the already prepared Engine model structure where the *Engine* is decomposed to *Engine Type*, as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The *Engine* Block will be a classifier for three engine instances. Each engine instance will contain *serialNum*, *year*slots with defined values and nested *engine Type* instances*.* Each nested *engine Type* instances will contain four *fuel*, *hp*, *make* and *model* slots with defined values. In the following figure, the Excel spreadsheet is prepared for the instances import. [Learn more how to prepare the Excel or CSV file for import >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files#ImportingdatafromExcelorCSVfiles-PreparingExcelorCSVfiletoimport)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Next, begin the import: in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with instances. In this case, the Vehicle model import Excel file is selected.
2. Select Instances as Import Type option value.
3. Select Engine Block as Instance Classifier option value.
4. Select Value Properties of the Engine Block and engine Type Part Property as the Nested Properties To Map option values. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_nested_properties.png']
5. Specify the location of imported elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space=''] by selecting the Target Scope option value. In this case, the Instances Package is selected.
6. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.
7. Create mapping between model properties and excel table cells.
8. Click Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='instances_import_dialog_options.png']

As a result, three nested engine instances and their slots are created with defined values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Each engine instance will contain <em>serialNum</em>, <em>year </em>slots with defined values and nested <em>engine Type</em> instances<em>.</em> Each nested <em>engine Type</em> instances will contain four <em>fuel</em>, <em>hp</em>, <em>make</em> and <em>model</em> slots with defined values. In this case, the <em>Instances </em>Package is selected.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.</li><li>Create mapping between model properties and excel table cells.</li><li>Click <strong>Import</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instances_import_dialog_options.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p>As a result, three nested engine instances and their slots are created with defined values. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="imported_instances.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139993 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00588: Importing models

- page_id: `189139993`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139993/Importing+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:39.713+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Composing model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Importing models from used projects

If you no longer need to have the set of elements in a separate project, you can import these elements into the main project.

To import a used project into the main project

1. In the Model Browser, select the package of this used project and open the shortcut menu .
2. Select Project Usages > Import .

All the elements of this used project are copied into the main project, and links to this used project are removed from the main project. The unlinked project and its elements continue to exist in the file system or server after the import.

- If the used project you want to import uses other projects, after the import these projects become directly used in the main project.
- The Standard/System Profiles cannot be imported according to our tool policy.

##### Importing models from other projects

Another way of composing your model, is to import a model from another project.

To import a model from another project

1. In the main menu, select File > Import From > Another Project .
2. Select a project you want to import.
3. Click **Import**. All elements from another project will be imported. If the model that you are importing also uses projects, these projects will be reused directly in your project after the model import.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Importing models from used projects</h3><p>If you no longer need to have the set of elements in a separate project, you can import these elements into the main project.</p><p> </p><p>To import a used project into the main project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, select the package of this used project and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cd108d97-84da-4865-8ccf-44b486b6e0c6">open the shortcut menu</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Import</strong>.</li></ol><p>All the elements of this used project are copied into the main project, and links to this used project are removed from the main project. The unlinked project and its elements continue to exist in the file system or server after the import.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4790239a-deb3-4f59-ba25-1ce2e1cdf086"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the used project you want to import uses other projects, after the import these projects become directly used in the main project.</li><li>The Standard/System Profiles cannot be imported according to our tool policy.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Importing models from other projects</h3><p>Another way of composing your model, is to import a model from another project.</p><p> </p><p>To import a model from another project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Another Project</strong>.</li><li>Select a project you want to import.</li><li><p>Click <strong>Import</strong>. All elements from another project will be imported.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8f81a64-5f1e-4400-a66f-28ac9ff8134f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the model that you are importing also uses projects, these projects will be reused directly in your project after the model import.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141938 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00589: Importing nested Requirements

- page_id: `189141938`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141938/Importing+nested+Requirements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:32.736+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets' say we need to import nested structure of requirements from the Excel file. For this, you must define the Owner property in the Excel spreadsheet with the name or id of each requirement. As example, see the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Requirements in the Excel spreadsheet with defined Owner property.

To import nested Requirements

1. Open the project in the modeling tool.
2. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the **File** > **Import From** > **Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. For element import via tables, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space=''] functionality.
3. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog), select the Excel file. Make sure the Excel file contains Owner column. See the example above.
4. Specify the mapping options:
  - Import Type - select the Element value.
  - Element Type - select the Requirement type you want to create.
  - Properties to Map - select Name, Text, id and Owner properties you want to fill in with data from Excel file.
  - **Target Scope**- select the Package you want to contain imported Requirements. If the Owner property is selected as the **Properties to Map** option value, the data is imported according the owner. You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. [Learn more about mapping****options >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea)
5. Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the column on the right to the property on the left. [Learn more about mapping area >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
6. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map. Learn more how to save Import Map >>
7. Click the Import button. The Requirements are imported. If any of the imported Requirement already exist in the model t hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify **Identification Property** value or **I****dentify Relationship (End) Source by** and **Identify Relationship (End) Target by** values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

For this, you must define the Owner property in the Excel spreadsheet with the name or id of each requirement. As example, see the following figure. </span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_req_excel_spreadsheet.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Requirements in the Excel spreadsheet with defined Owner property.</h6><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To import nested Requirements</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Open the project in the modeling tool.</span></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="550f0d57-9ef5-4e47-be80-3ea69ef5a3be"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For element import via tables, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exce/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> functionality.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a>, select the Excel file. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d12b70c5-f63a-40ff-b71b-6c5c7968ddbf"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure the Excel file contains Owner column. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140070 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00590: Importing projects from Ecore files

- page_id: `189140070`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140070/Importing+projects+from+Ecore+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:16.537+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Ecore support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is no possibility to import Ecore files directly. Ecore files should be imported using the EMOF import feature. 
 
To import an Ecore file

1. In the Eclipse environment, open the *.ecore file and save it as *.emof.
2. Use the Importing projects from MOF files procedure.

This way of importing Ecore files may cause some Ecore-specific model details that don't exist in EMOF to be lost.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is no possibility to import Ecore files directly. Ecore files should be imported using the EMOF import feature. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> To import an Ecore file</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Eclipse environment, open the *.ecore file and save it as *.emof.</li><li>Use the <a href="http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Importing+projects+from+MOF+files">Importing projects from MOF files</a> procedure.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32276341-4f32-43be-be16-e7105068a796"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This way of importing Ecore files may cause some Ecore-specific model details that don't exist in EMOF to be lost.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140048 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00591: Importing projects from MOF files

- page_id: `189140048`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140048/Importing+projects+from+MOF+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:41.961+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > MOF support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To import a project from a MOF file

1. From the main menu, select File > Import From > MOF XMI File .
2. In the dialog, select the EMOF or CMOF file you want to import, and click Open . The file is imported as a separate project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To import a project from a MOF file</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>MOF XMI File</strong>.</li><li>In the dialog, select the EMOF or CMOF file you want to import, and click <strong>Open</strong>. The file is imported as a separate project.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 00592: Importing relationships

- page_id: `189141947`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141947/Importing+relationships
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:33.228+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files > Import examples
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

73869989

100.00002%

73870000

73869990

**On this page**

5

73869987

##### Main conditions

You can create relationships between existing model elements by importing them from Excel/CSV files. For thisthe following conditions must be met:

- 
  - The source/client and target/supplierelements you want to create relationships between must exist in the project.
  - The Excel/CSV source and target columns must be mapped with the Client/Source and Supplier/Target properties in the mapping area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space=''].
  - The source and target elements must be legal in terms of Metamodel, DSL and code rules, e.g., the satisfy relationship client must be a requirement.

##### Preparing the Excel file to import relationships

When creating relationships in the model by importing them, you need to make sure the Excel file contains the source and target columns. The source column contains the names of elements that are the client of the relationship. The target column contains the names of the elements that are the suppliers of the relationship. In the example below, the Source column contains the names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the names of Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Excel file content prepared with element names for the relationships import.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='']>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing nested Requirements' space='']>]]>

If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, the elements are not under the same target scope, or you want to increase the import speed, use the following:

- Select to search elements and references in the Target Scope Only in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog wh en importing data.
- Define your source and target columns in the Excel file with qualified names of elements. In the example below, the Source column contains the qualified names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the qualified names of Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The The Excel file content prepared with qualified element names for the relationships import.

##### Relationships import procedure

To import relationships

1. Prepare the Excel file for relationships import. >]]>
2. Open the project in the modeling tool. Make sure the elements you want to create relationships between exist in the project. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing Blocks and their properties' space='']>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing nested Requirements' space='']>]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the **File** > **Import From** > **Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. For element import via tables, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space=''] functionality.
4. In the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog , select the Excel file. How to >>
5. Specify the mapping options:
  - Import Type - select the Element value.
  - Element Type - select the relationship type you want to create.
  - Properties to Map - the Client and Supplier properties are selected by default.
  - **Target Scope**- select the Package you want to contain imported relationships. If the Owner property is selected as the **Properties to Map** option value, the data is imported according the owner. You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. [Learn more about mapping****options >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea)
6. Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the Source column on the right to the Client property on the left. Do the same with the Target and Supplier or other columns/properties.[Learn more about mapping area >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
7. (Optional) Cli ck the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map. Learn more how to save Import Map >>
8. Click the Import button. The relationships are imported. If any of the imported relationships already exist in the model t hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify **Identification Property** value or **I****dentify Relationship (End) Source by** and **Identify Relationship (End) Target by** values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']>]]>

The relationship import can fail due to the following reasons:

- The relationship end is empty in the data file.
- The source or the target elements do not exist in the model or the mechanism didn't find any.

In the following example, you can see specified import options in the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog before the import. After the import the Satisfy relationships are created between the defined Source and Target elements: Blocks and Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The import of Satisfy relationship.

73874634

## PAGE 00593: Importing server project file

- page_id: `189143540`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143540/Importing+server+project+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:16.064+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Collaboration between disconnected teams > Using server project file format
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a project as a new server project, import the project*.szip*file.

To import a server project file

1. Select File > Import From > Server Project File.
2. Select the .szip file you want to use and click **Open**. This opens the **Import Project to the Server**dialog: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the Action column, you see the action that is performed with the project once you click Import . Possible actions include:
  - Add to Server – the project is added to the server.
    - If the action is Add to Server , you can modify the **Name on Server**column to choose a new name for the imported project.
    - If you want to skip certain projects, you can change the Add to Server action to Skip .
  - Skip – the server project is skipped.
  - Skip (Project Removed) – the project is removed. You must use Data Manager to either delete it completely from the server or restore the deleted version.
  - Skip (Project Exists) – the project already exists on the server and cannot be imported . If you still need to upload the .szip version of the project to Teamwork Cloud, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Updating a server project with local changes' space='']**Update from Local Project**functionality.
4. To choose the category for the newly added project, select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the dialog. This opens the **Select Category**dialog : 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Choose the category and click **OK**to return to the **Import Project to the Server**dialog.
6. Click **Import**.****The project will be added as a new server project.

**Please note:**

- If the project you are importing was saved together with used projects, all of the used projects will be visible in the import dialog. You can then decide if you want to include them in the import.
- If a main or used project you are importing already exists in the server, it will be skipped and not imported. You can see the results of the import action in the Notification Window: [ATTACHMENT filename='server_projects_notification.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This opens the <strong>Import Project to the Server </strong>dialog:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_project_to_server.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li>In the <strong>Action </strong>column, you see the action that is performed with the project once you click <strong>Import</strong>. Possible actions include:<ul><li><strong>Add to Server</strong> – the project is added to the server.<ul><li>If the action is <strong>Add to Server</strong>, you can <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">modify the <strong>Name on Server </strong>column to choose a new name for the imported project.</span> </li><li>If you want to skip certain projects, you can change the <strong>Add to Server </strong>action to <strong>Skip</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Skip </strong>– the server project is skipped.</li><li><strong>Skip (Project Removed)</strong> – the project is removed. You must use Data Manager to either delete it completely from the server or restore the deleted version.</li><li><strong>Skip (Project Exists)</strong> – the project already exists on the server and cannot be <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e5075efb-8b1f-459b-8bfd-e58eb1442197">imported</ac:inline-comment-marker>. If you still need to upload the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.szip version of the project to Teamwork Cloud, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Updating a server project with local changes" /><ac:link-body><strong>Update from Local Project </strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>functionality.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To choose the category for the newly added project, select <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR1" ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> at the bottom of the dialog. This opens the <strong>Select Category </strong>dialog</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="461"><ri:attachment ri:filename="server_project_category_selection.jpg" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Choose the category and click <strong>OK </strong>to return to the <strong>Import Project to the Server </strong>dialog.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Click <strong>Import</strong>.<strong> </strong>The project will be added as a new server project.<br /><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4fdaf3a5-e1fc-4b1c-aac0-0eb146562935"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Please note:</strong></p><ul><li>If the project you are importing was saved together with used projects, all of the used projects will be visible in the import dialog. You can then decide if you want to include them in the import.</li><li>If a main or used project you are importing already exists in the server, it will be skipped and not imported.<br />You can see the results of the import action in the Notification Window:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="235"><ri:attachment ri:filename="server_projects_notification.png" /></ac:image> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=216268955 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00594: Importing server project files to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `216268955`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/216268955/Importing+server+project+files+to+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-03-04T15:14:34.589+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Disconnected team collaboration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting projects to server project files' space=''], you can import itto the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform. You can alsouse .szip files to update existing projects on the platform.

To import/update projects from a server project file to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. In the main menu, select File > Import From > Server Project File .
2. In the open dialog, select the .szip file you want to import and click the **Open** button. The **Import Project to the Server** dialog opens. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Import Project to the Server** dialog lists the main project and its usages that will be imported to or updated on the server. The **Action** column indicated the action that will be performed on each project:**Add to Server** - The project does not exist on the server and will be added as a new project.**Update** - An earlier project version exists on the server, and the project will be updated. During the update, a new project version is created, and the project data from the server project file overwrites the project data stored on the target server. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select which project revision or branch should be updated.**Skip** - The same version of the project you're trying to import exists on the server. The existing project version on the server will be used in the project that requires it. No new data will be imported or updated.
3. Click the Import button.

When the import is complete, the **Notification Window** opens saying that the projects were imported successfully.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>After <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exporting projects to server project files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exporting a project to the server project file (.szip)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can import it</span><span> to the </span><strong>3D</strong><span>EXPERIENCE platform. You can also<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> use .szip files to update existing projects on the platform.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p>To import/update projects from a server project file to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">File</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> &gt; </span><strong style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Import From</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> &gt; </span><strong style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Server Project File</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">In the open dialog, select the .szip file you want to import and click the <strong>Open</strong> button. The <strong>Import Project to the Server</strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_project_to_server.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8cc84f47-ecc2-457a-b418-452ed36aeee3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> <strong>Import Project to the Server</strong> dialog lists the main project and its usages that will be imported to or updated on the server. The <strong>Action</strong> column indicated the action that will be performed on each project:<br /></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Add to Server</strong> - The project does not exist on the server and will be added as a new project.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Update</strong> - An earlier project version exists on the server, and the project will be updated. During the update, a new project version is created, and the project data from the server project file overwrites the project data stored on the target server. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_revision.png" /></ac:image> to select which project <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="04c25ee8-212a-484d-90af-4b2f98c49629">revision or branch</ac:inline-comment-marker> should be <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f73966ba-649e-460b-8962-aa81f4aa7985">updated</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Skip</strong> - The same version of the project you're trying to import exists on the server. The existing project version on the server will be used in the project that requires it. No new data will be imported or updated.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />When the import is complete, the <strong>Notification Window</strong> opens saying that the projects were imported successfully.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143786 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00595: Importing the content of a used project into your project

- page_id: `189143786`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143786/Importing+the+content+of+a+used+project+into+your+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:23.516+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To import the content of a used project into your project, you must have an **Administer Resources** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud185'].

If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.

Standard/system profiles, such as *UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip*, cannot be imported.

To import the contents of a used project into your project

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space=''] .
2. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select the used project and click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select **Import**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Import dialog, choose how to import the elements of the used project: with new IDs or with old.
5. If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click **Yes** and wait while the import completes. This may take some time. NoteThe imported project no longer appears in the **Project Usages** dialog.
6. Click OK .

The imported project also disappears from the package *Project Usages* and a new package named after the imported project appears under the root package in the Model Browser. If the imported project uses other projects, these projects will be directly used in your project.

After it is imported to the contents of your project, a used project will still exist in the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) repository.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
