After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a3d4aa3-1caa-4487-8226-47573b3c0e12"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Standard/system profiles, such as <em>UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip</em>, cannot be imported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To import the contents of a used project into your project</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt; Project Usages &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the used project and click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac0e76a5-8ae3-4829-8bdb-a3a08b2944bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Shortcut menu</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select <strong>Import</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_usages_import.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>In the <strong>Import</strong> dialog, choose how to import the elements of the used project: with new IDs or with old.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click <strong>Yes</strong> and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17f6ab1e-9cfb-4f41-9fdf-79440af6a9a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The imported project no longer appears in the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The imported project also disappears from the package <em>Project Usages</em> and a new package named after the imported project appears under the root package in the Model Browser. If the imported project uses other projects, these projects will be directly used in your project.</p><p>After it is imported to the contents of your project, a used project will still exist in the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) repository.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=209257502 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00596: Importing the content of used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `209257502`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/209257502/Importing+the+content+of+used+projects+in+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-02-04T14:54:09.758+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.

Standard/system profiles, such as *UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip*, cannot be imported.

To import the contents of a used project into your project

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking models in 3DEXPERIENCE' space=''] .
2. In the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. In the **Project Usages** dialog, do one of the following:
  - Select a used project and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - Right-click a used project and select Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='importing_used_projects.png']
4. In the Import dialog, do one of the following:
  - Click the **Import with New IDs** button to create new IDs for the imported elements in the main project. This way, element IDs are not duplicated, ensuring the main project remains intact. However, if the elements of the used project are used in other projects, choosing **Import with****New IDs** may break those references. Importing elements with new IDs is highly recommended as it is the safest option regarding project consistency.
  - Click the **Import with Old IDs** button to import the elements with their existing IDs, which can be useful if the elements are used in multiple other projects. However, this may cause duplicate element IDs, resulting in the main project being corrupted. To proceed with the import using the old IDs, click **Yes**in the question****dialog, acknowledging that the procedure might corrupt the data of the main project.
5. If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click **Yes** and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.
6. Click OK to close the dialog .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a3d4aa3-1caa-4487-8226-47573b3c0e12"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Standard/system profiles, such as <em>UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip</em>, cannot be imported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To import the contents of a used project into your project</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Locking models in 3DEXPERIENCE" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a used project and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li>Right-click a used project and select <strong>Import</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_used_projects.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Import</strong> dialog, do one of the following: <ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import with New IDs</strong> button to create new IDs for the imported elements in the main project. This way, element IDs are not duplicated, ensuring the main project remains intact. However, if the elements of the used project are used in other projects, choosing <strong>Import with </strong><strong>New IDs</strong> may break those references.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f73d9e1f-6144-49a9-b69c-3c28a0861585"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Importing elements with new IDs is highly recommended as it is the safest option regarding project consistency.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import with Old IDs</strong> button to import the elements with their existing IDs, which can be useful if the elements are used in multiple other projects. However, this may cause duplicate element IDs, resulting in the main project being corrupted. To proceed with the import using the old IDs, click <strong>Yes </strong>in the question<strong> </strong>dialog, acknowledging that the procedure might corrupt the data of the main project.</p></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click <strong>Yes</strong> and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.</p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialog<strong>.</strong></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139684 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00597: Improving performance

- page_id: `189139684`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139684/Improving+performance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:17.623+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1427324068

1427324070

1427324069

INLINE

When working with very large models or a number of diagrams at a time, the modeling tool performance may decrease. To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory allocation' space=''] (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space=''] to track memory usage.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation period' space=''] and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.
- Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project partitioning' space=''].
- Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space=''] to free unused memory.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Background Task Manager' space=''] that run too long.
- Set the limit of open diagrams in the Environment Options dialog ( Options > Environment > General > General > Diagram Tabs Limit ).

1427324067

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Getting started' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing and Selecting Perspective' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Disabling inbound network activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a61e022-7559-4d3f-b56c-3dee6baa15de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324068</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="163095b8-9d1f-4648-bcc3-550ea595c28e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324070</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d690d15-ffd8-4de9-b158-88970fb6091a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324069</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dea99695-1d3c-4cd0-93b9-438f4b087bb8"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When working with very large models or a number of diagrams at a time, the modeling tool performance may decrease. To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:</p><ul><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory allocation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Increase memory allocation value]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory monitor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[memory monitor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to track memory usage.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Active validation period" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Increase an active validation period]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.</p></li><li><p>Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project partitioning" /></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Memory monitor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Garbage Collector]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to free unused memory.</p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Background Task Manager" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cancel the background tasks]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that run too long.</li><li>Set the limit of open diagrams in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog (<strong>Options</strong> &gt;<strong> Environment</strong> &gt; <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Diagram Tabs Limit</strong>). </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bee057bf-13ac-4c87-a9c6-d0ef312493f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324067</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Getting started" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing and Selecting Perspective" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Configuration files" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Disabling inbound network activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141486 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00598: Include

- page_id: `189141486`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141486/Include
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:17.768+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Include relationship is used when there are common parts of the behavior among two or more Use Cases. Each common part is then extracted to a separate Use Case, to be included by all base Use Cases having this part in common. The Include relationship is represented by a base Use Case to an inclusion Use Case.

An Include relationship between Use Cases indicates that an instance of the including Use Case also contains the behavior as specified by the included Use Case.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Include relationship

In the example above, the*Make Item Reservation*Use Case is a base Use Case. It includes the*Find Item*Use Case. The*Make Item Reservation*Use Case is the including Use Case and the*Find Item*Use Case is the included Use Case.

To use the Include relationship

1. Create two or more Use Cases by dragging and dropping the Use Case element [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.28.57 PM.png'] onto your diagram pane.
2. Click on the Include relationship [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.30.51 PM.png'] and connect it between two Use Cases.
3. The result should look like the aforementioned example.

You can format comment symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about symbol representation properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space=''].

You can specify a comment in the Include Specification Window. In the same window, you can find the description of each Include property in the description area of the Specification Window.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Include relationship is used when there are common parts of the behavior among two or more Use Cases. Each common part is then extracted to a separate Use Case, to be included by all base Use Cases having this part in common. The Include relationship is represented by a base Use Case to an inclusion Use Case.</p><p>An Include relationship between Use Cases indicates that an instance of the including Use Case also contains the behavior as specified by the included Use Case.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 11.35.50 AM.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Example of Include relationship</h6><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;">In the example above, the </span><em>Make Item Reservation</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case is a base Use Case. It includes the </span><em>Find Item</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case. The </span><em>Make Item Reservation</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case is the including Use Case and the </span><em>Find Item</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case is the included Use Case.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To use the Include relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Create two or more Use Cases by dragging and dropping the Use Case element <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="90"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.28.57 PM.png" /></ac:image>onto your diagram pane.</li><li>Click on the Include relationship <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="80"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.30.51 PM.png" /></ac:image> and connect it between two Use Cases.</li><li>The result should look like the aforementioned example.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can format comment symbol properties in the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19aa51b2-b4f6-4ae5-b87a-0d31db6145b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about symbol representation properties, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can specify a comment in the Include Specification Window. In the same window, you can find the description of each Include property in the description area of the Specification Window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02202a08-01b8-4664-b9d1-3104c6e4ecc5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about the Specification window usage, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about specifying property values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing property values" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff1aef2b-4429-4784-bb8f-aa49ccc1bf16"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144562 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00599: Including external images

- page_id: `189144562`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144562/Including+external+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:58.190+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050285233

2050285245

2050285231

**On this page**

43

2050285234

You can include images from an external source in a report. The supported image types are *BMP*, *JPG*, *WBMP*, *GIF*, and *PNG*.

###### **$image.include(location)**

Include an external image from a local file or *URL* in a report.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | location | java.lang.String | An image’s location. The location format can be either a URL or a local file. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image included in a report |

For example:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adcb2062-dfa2-4b21-9dcd-3e6517f349c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285233</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2aaa8a70-e325-4c59-8f8d-45f736106588"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285245</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="897d6481-21c5-4b5d-8590-c2a4a1b7ae7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285231</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd7876af-2c40-481d-9c44-715984bb4606"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285234</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can include images from an external source in a report. The supported image types are <em>BMP</em>, <em>JPG</em>, <em>WBMP</em>, <em>GIF</em>, and <em>PNG</em>.</p><h4><strong>$image.include(location)</strong></h4><p>Include an external image from a local file or <em>URL</em> in a report.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>location</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An image’s location. <br />The location format <br />can be either a URL <br />or a local file.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image included <br />in a report</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd86fd2e-acc9-4364-82e6-34794a984140"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$image.include("C:\Users\username\images\logo.gif")
$image.include("http://www.magicdraw.com/images/product_boxes/MD.gif")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142015 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00600: Incompatible properties and related elements

- page_id: `189142015`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142015/Incompatible+properties+and+related+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:47.203+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Refactoring > Converting Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the conversion, a converted element owns the same compatible specification properties and is connected with the same related elements as the original element was connected. The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.

Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.

For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.

To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.

##### Changes after conversion

###### Converting a property to a port

If the property visibility is *private*, *protected,* or *package* , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to *public*.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>After the conversion, a converted element owns the same compatible specification properties and is connected with the same related elements as the original element was connected. The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.</p><p>Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.</p><p>For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)" ac:title="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_elements_incompatible_elements.png" /></ac:image></p><p>As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.</p><p>To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.</p><h3>Changes after conversion</h3><h4>Converting a property to a port</h4><p>If the property visibility is <em>private</em>, <em>protected,</em> or <em>package</em> , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to <em>public</em>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67b51616-1257-45d8-b179-df6555185bfc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fc359cb-dee4-4291-9163-fcd677e10dd5" /> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139944 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00601: Indexing

- page_id: `189139944`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139944/Indexing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:34.533+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model elements representation text indexing allows users to find elements in the model more quickly. It starts automatically when a Teamwork Cloud project is opened for the first time; you can track the progress in the status bar (see the image below). The index is automatically updated when the model changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The indexing progress shown in the status bar.

Please note that indexing may take some time depending on the project size. The more elements the project contains, the longer it takes to index the model elements representation text.

If representation text building is in progress, searches performed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space=''] dialog may be slower. In this case, a warning appears next to the search progress bar (see an image below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If a search does not return expected results, we recommend that you rebuild the model elements representation text index (see below).

The**Rebuild Text Index** action is available in the Expert mode only.

instructionsTo rebuild the text index

- In the main menu , click Tools > Rebuild Text Index . [ATTACHMENT filename='rebuild_text_index_dialog.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139986 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00602: Indexing of unloaded projects

- page_id: `189139986`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139986/Indexing+of+unloaded+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:38.362+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Decomposing model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Proxy

628699399

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Proxy

628699401

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Proxy

628699400

If you have a large project and, due to performance issues, some used projects are not loaded, you may use indexing to work with the project smoothly.Indexing can be considered as an intermediate form of work, falling between working with a fully loaded used project and working with a not-loaded used project.

#### INFO: Proxy

Proxy

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']true

When a used project is unloaded/not loaded in the project, only proxies of necessary elements are shown in place of the used project. Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources. If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.

A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:

A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.

To specify elements for indexing

1. Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.
2. From the Options menu, select Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
3. Select the Indexing property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_indexing.png']
4. Select the **Common Indexing** option. This enables indexing of the used project and determines what information will be indexed.
5. When common indexing is chosen, classifiers and their inheritance relationships will be indexed. If you want more elements to be indexed, select the **Custom Indexing** option and fine-tune which element types (properties, methods, etc) should be indexed. The more elements you select, the more elements will be accessible in the project using them as proxies. However, your gains in performance from the used project unloading will also diminish. Thus, a balance is needed when customizing the index. It is usually better to use the common indexing variant. Click **OK**.
6. In the project using the indexed project, go to the options of the used indexed project (from the main menu, select **Options** > **Project Usages**) and select the **Use Index** check box.

628699398

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b67429c1-fe33-441f-95d4-891a75370dfe"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699399</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5323c54-ea67-42f4-b33b-56df6d2842e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699401</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a7a20e1-4dc1-4bd9-bb11-8b409706345d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699400</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="291b13b2-a9df-4d8c-9830-9a083f16b245">If you have a large project and, due to performance issues, some used projects are not loaded, you may use indexing to work with the project smoothly. </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="291b13b2-a9df-4d8c-9830-9a083f16b245">Indexing</ac:inline-comment-marker> can be considered as an intermediate form of work, falling between working with a fully loaded used project and working with a not-loaded used project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1c1ae84-92a9-410a-9e4a-0cda821f843b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Proxy</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e66c221-e641-4588-b01e-0fca83433f19"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Maintaining decomposed model integrity" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>When a used project is unloaded/not loaded in the project, only proxies of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c07af6fa-3fd0-4554-a664-3e5b51394736">necessary element</ac:inline-comment-marker>s are shown in place of the used project. Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources.  If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.</p><p>A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:</p><p>A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify elements for <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="108124cd-4ada-4792-92e5-e3120682817a">indexing</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="77374505-fd83-4671-b796-37de755e139c">Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the <strong>Indexing</strong> property group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_indexing.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>Select the <strong>Common Indexing</strong> option. This enables indexing of the used project and determines what information will be indexed.</p></li><li><p>When common indexing is chosen, classifiers and their inheritance relationships will be indexed. If you want more elements to be indexed, select the <strong>Custom Indexing</strong> option and fine-tune which element types (properties, methods, etc) should be indexed. The more elements you select, the more elements will be accessible in the project using them as proxies. However, your gains in performance from the used project unloading will also diminish. Thus, a balance is needed when customizing the index. It is usually better to use the common indexing variant.</p><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li><p>In the project using the indexed project, go to the options of the used indexed project (from the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>) and select the <strong>Use Index</strong> check box. </p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d3bdc33-2e2d-4238-8f19-7a0e4fd3b85c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699398</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Maintaining decomposed model integrity" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141490 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00603: Information Flow

- page_id: `189141490`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141490/Information+Flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:27.664+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Information Flow specifies that one or more information items circulate from its sources to its targets. Information flows require an “information channel” for transmitting information items from the source to the destination.

An information channel is represented in various ways, depending on the nature of its sources and targets. It can be represented by connectors, links, associations, or dependencies. For example, if the source and destination are parts in composite structure diagrams such as a collaboration, the information channel is likely to be represented by a connector between them. Or, if the source and target are objects (which are a kind of Instance Specification), they can be represented by a link that joins the two, and so on. The Information Flow and the information item notation are added. You can draw them using the Information Flows toolbar in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''].

##### Setting an Information Flow

Information Flows can be set on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']in the Class Diagram and on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='']in the Composite Structure Diagrams.

To set an Information Flow

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space=''] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='']) relationship between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='']). Make sure the properties for displaying a Conveyed Information on relationships are selected.To display Conveyed Information:Right-click the Association (Connector) and choose the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']** command.In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property group toolbar' space=''] to **Expert** or **All**.Set the **Show Conveyed Information A** and **Show Conveyed Information B** check boxes to *true*.
2. Specify a Conveyed Information in the following ways: 
- Drag the Class or Information Item on an Association (Connector) or Information Flow and select the Set as Conveyed Information command. 
- Select an Association (Connector) relationship and from the smart manipulator toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
- In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Information (e.g., Class) and drag it onto the Information Flow on the diagram pane.
3. In the **New Conveyed Information** dialog, specify **Conveyed Information**, **Information Flow**, **Direction** and click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Conveyed informationOne Information Flow may have several Conveyed Information items. If a new Information Flow is set on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space=''] that is represented in other diagrams, the new Conveyed Information is displayed only on the diagram on which you set it.You can reverse the direction of the [Information Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Information+Flow) on [Connectors](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Connector). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reversing Information Flow direction' space='']>]]> An Information Flow is set.

##### Changing the arrow notation visibility

You can choose whether you want to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements. The **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space=''] controls the arrow visibility.

To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Conveyed Information is hidden or unspecified

1. From the modeling tool main menu, choose Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, go to General > Diagrams .
3. In the Diagrams property group, find the Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing option and set it to true.
4. Click OK when you are done.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reversing Information Flow direction' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Item' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>An Information Flow specifies that one or more information items circulate from its sources to its targets. Information flows require an “information channel” for transmitting information items from the source to the destination.</p><p>An information channel is represented in various ways, depending on the nature of its sources and targets. It can be represented by connectors, links, associations, or dependencies. For example, if the source and destination are parts in composite structure diagrams such as a collaboration, the information channel is likely to be represented by a connector between them. Or, if the source and target are objects (which are a kind of Instance Specification), they can be represented by a link that joins the two, and so on. The Information Flow and the information item notation are added. You can draw them using the Information Flows toolbar in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Setting an Information Flow</h3><p>Information Flows can be set on <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Associations ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in the Class Diagram and on <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connectors ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in the Composite Structure Diagrams.</p><p>To set an Information Flow</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Connector" /></ac:link>) relationship between <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe1498c7-4dbd-4f5d-8345-c6d7b7f7a012"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure the properties for displaying a Conveyed Information on relationships are selected.</p><p>To display Conveyed Information:</p><ol><li>Right-click the Association (Connector) and choose the <strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong> command.</li><li>In the open dialog, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Property group toolbar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[change the properties display mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to <strong>Expert</strong> or <strong>All</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Conveyed Information A</strong> and <strong>Show Conveyed Information B</strong>  check boxes to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Specify a Conveyed Information in the following ways:<br />- Drag the Class or Information Item on an Association (Connector) <span>or Information Flow</span> and select the Set as Conveyed Information command.<br />- Select an Association (Connector) relationship and from the smart manipulator toolbar click <ac:image ac:title="New Conveyed Information" ac:alt="New Conveyed Information"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_conveyed_information.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br />- In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Information (e.g., Class) and drag it onto the Information Flow on the diagram pane.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>New Conveyed Information</strong> dialog, specify <strong>Conveyed Information</strong>, <strong>Information Flow</strong>, <strong>Direction</strong> and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_conveyed_information_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb7a8d5-00d7-4e0c-8b73-96aed9eb4dd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Conveyed information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>One Information Flow may have several Conveyed Information items.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e02e60f-9ce9-4041-a097-219a8ccdba28"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If a new Information Flow is set on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Connector" /></ac:link> that is represented in other diagrams, the new Conveyed Information is displayed only on the diagram on which you set it.</li><li>You can reverse the direction of the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Information+Flow">Information Flow</a> on <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Connector">Connectors</a>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Reversing Information Flow direction" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to reverse direction >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">An Information Flow is set.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><h3><span>Changing the arrow notation visibility</span></h3><p>You can choose whether you want to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements. The <strong>Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> controls the arrow visibility.</p><p>To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Conveyed Information is hidden or unspecified</p><hr /><ol><li>From the modeling tool main menu, choose <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, go to <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Diagrams</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagrams </strong>property group, find the <strong>Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing</strong> option and set it to true.</li><li>Click <strong>OK </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Reversing Information Flow direction" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Information Item" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143265 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00604: Information Flow has no Conveyed Information

- page_id: `189143265`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143265/Information+Flow+has+no+Conveyed+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:56.035+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

IFHNCI[1]

**Description**

This validation rule checks if there are any Information Flows without Conveyed Information.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Information Flow

**Solvers**

- **Select Conveyed Information** - opens the Select Conveyed Information dialog where you can choose the Conveyed Information for the validated Information Flow.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Information Flow has no Conveyed Information. The error is resolved by specifying Conveyed Information via the **Select Conveyed Information**solver.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">IFHNCI</span><span class="error" style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">[1]</span></p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if there are any <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Information Flows without Conveyed Information.</span></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">warning</span></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Information Flow</span></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Select Conveyed Information</strong> - <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">opens the Select Conveyed Information dialog where you can choose the Conveyed Information for the validated Information Flow.</span></span></li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Information_Flow_has_no_Conveyed_Information.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An Information Flow has no Conveyed Information. The error is resolved by specifying Conveyed Information via the <strong>Select Conveyed Information </strong>solver.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141494 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00605: Information Item

- page_id: `189141494`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141494/Information+Item
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:18.381+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Quick search

633879781

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Quick search

633879783

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Quick search

633879782

INLINE

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space=''] package provides mechanisms for specifying the exchange of information between entities in a system at a high level of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigating between different levels of abstraction' space='']. Information Flows describe the circulation of information in a system in a general manner. They do not specify the nature of the information or the mechanisms by which this information is conveyed (message passing, signal, common data store, parameter of operation, etc.). Also, they do not specify sequences or any control conditions. It is intended that representation and realization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Link' space=''] will be able to specify which model element implements the specified information flow, and how the information will be conveyed while modeling in detail.

An Information Item is an abstraction of all information that can be exchanged between objects. It is a kind of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying internal structure on structured classifiers' space=''] intended to represent information in a very abstract way, one which cannot be instantiated.

In a classifier, the Information Item can be represented as a name inside a rectangle. The black triangle icon at the top of the rectangle indicates that it is an Information Item.

Information Items, or any conveyed classifiers, can be displayed on any relationships.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of an Information Item named Wage

One purpose of Information Items is to enable you to define preliminary models before making detailed modeling decisions on types or structures. Another purpose of Information Items and Information Flows is to abstract complex models by using a less specific more general representation of the information exchanged between entities of a system.

To create an Information Item

1. In a model or diagram, select a relationship or create a new one.
2. Set a relationship as an Information Flow, using one of the following methods:
  - Click a relationship. The smart manipulator opens. Select the New Conveyed Information command. [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_new_conveyed_information.png']
  - Open the relationship [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] . Select the Conveyed Information property group and click the Add button.
3. The New Conveyed Information dialog opens. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='open_dialog_button.png'] next to the Conveyed Information text box.
4. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space=''] dialog opens. Click the Creation Mode button to enable the creation of new elements.
5. Select the package where you want to create a new element.
6. Click the **Create** button and select **Information Item**element in the list. Quick searchStart typing the element name to find the element quicker.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. The Specification window of the newly created element opens. Type the element name and specify other properties according to your needs.
8. After you finish, close all the dialogs one by one. The Information Item element is created.

633879780

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139597 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00606: Inheritance tab

- page_id: `189139597`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139597/Inheritance+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:12.776+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425598369

1425598371

1425598370

INLINE

The Inheritance tab represents classifiers (e.g., packages, data types, and stereotypes) hierarchy within your project. Inheritance according to the UML Specification is shown using the generalization relationship.

If a model element has no generalization relationship, it will not be represented in the Inheritance tab.

To open a new Inheritance tree

1. Open the Containment , Structure , or Inheritance tab.
2. In the open tab toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='open_new_tab_button.png'] and then select Open in New Inheritance Tree .

You can change some Inheritance tab properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog in the **Browser** options group.

The Inheritance tab consists of the following two components:

- Inheritance tab toolbar
- Inheritance tree

##### Inheritance tab toolbar

The Inheritance tab toolbar contains the following buttons

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all currently expanded elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Open in New Tree | Opens the contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.You can choose to open elements in:New Containment TreeNew Structure TreeNew Inheritance Tree |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favorites Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |
|  | Options | Click to open the Options menu and then select:Invert Tree - inverts the tree hierarchy - shows the childelement at the top of the hierarchy.Show Applied Stereotypes - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree. |

##### Working with model elements in the Inheritance tree

You can work with elements in the Inheritance tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Select in Structure Tree | Opens the Structure tab and the structured classifier is selected.This command is available for structured classifiers (SysML). |

1425598368

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']

ri:filename="Collapse_all.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Collapse All</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">C</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ollapses all currently expanded elements in the Containment tree.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Collapse_selected_recursively.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Collapse Selected Recursively</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="18" ac:width="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_new_tab_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><p>Open in New Tree</p></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Opens the contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.</p><p>You can choose to open elements in:</p><ul><li>New Containment Tree</li><li>New Structure Tree</li><li>New Inheritance Tree</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_tab.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="17" ac:width="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorite_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Favorites</p></td><td><p>Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Add a selected element to favorites</li><li>Open the dialog for managing favorites<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li>Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="19" ac:width="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Quick Find</p></td><td><p>Opens the <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialog.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1">Options</td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to open the Options menu and then select:</p><ul><li><strong><strong>Invert Tree</strong> </strong>- inverts the tree hierarchy - shows the child<br />element at the top of the hierarchy.<strong><br /></strong></li><li><strong>Show </strong><strong>Applied Stereotypes</strong> - shows or hides the applied <br />stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Working with model elements in the Inheritance tree</h3><p><br /></p><p>You can work with elements in the Inheritance tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 39.6427%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 28.0677%;" /><col style="width: 71.9323%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Command</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Function</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Select in Containment</strong> <strong>Tree</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the Containment tab and selects the <br />current element in the Containment tree.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Select in Structure Tree</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Opens the Structure tab and the structured classifier is selected.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af49f33a-5b79-474a-834d-d8ee5e6a182b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command is available for structured classifiers (SysML).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b778001b-c783-474f-92d7-aaa6e02f945c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425598368</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141499 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00607: Initial Node

- page_id: `189141499`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141499/Initial+Node
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:27.900+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Initial node is a control node where flow starts when the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity' space=''] is invoked. An Activity may have more than one Initial node.

You can specify an Initial node by changing its property values in the Initial node[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

You can format Initial Node symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Initial node is a control node where flow starts when the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity" /></ac:link> is invoked. An Activity may have more than one Initial node.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can specify an Initial node by changing its property values in the Initial node </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</span></p><p>You can format Initial Node symbol properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /></ac:link>.    </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="initial_node.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141742 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00608: Initial state

- page_id: `189141742`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141742/Initial+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:27.465+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every object belongs to a particular state as soon as it is created. So, it is useful to explicitly show that particular state. A solid filled circle represents the initial state of an object. There can only be one initial state for an object. The initial state denotes the starting place for a transition, the target of which is a composite state.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every object belongs to a particular state as soon as it is created. So, it is useful to explicitly show that particular state. A solid filled circle represents the initial state of an object. There can only be one initial state for an object. The initial state denotes the starting place for a transition, the target of which is a composite state.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a91c5c61-63c8-4aaa-bfa5-b56b64a56477"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145492 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00609: Initializing a Custom Model

- page_id: `189145492`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145492/Initializing+a+Custom+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:43.657+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With DSL customization, you can modify a model element to act according to your needs depending on the stereotype you apply.

###### Default values

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space=''] have default values. Use the default values when you create the tag, making sure its definition has multiplicity greater than zero. In this case it is automatically created when applying a stereotype.

###### Stereotypes on relationship ends

At times, DSL requires applying [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']to some elements after DSL relationship connection to those elements.

Use the following properties for this purpose:

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Apply to Source | Specifies stereotypes that must be applied to the source element of this relationship after connection. |
| Apply to Target | Specifies stereotypes that must be applied to the target element of this relationship after connection. |

#### INFO: Source and Target in an example

Source and Target in an example

“Source” and “Target” are respectively the “source” and “target” of a directed relationship.

The first connected element acts as a“source” for a non-directed relationship.

Example:

1. Create a stereotype «serve» and select the Dependency metaclass.
2. Create two stereotypes «main» and «servant» and select the Element metaclass.
3. Create a customization element.
4. In the customization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , specify the following property values:
  1. for the Customization Target property, select the «serve» stereotype,
  2. for the Apply To Source property, select the «main» stereotype,
  3. for the Apply To Target property, select the «servant» stereotype.
5. Reopen the project. Draw the «serve» dependency from one element to another. The «main» and «servant» stereotypes are applied to these elements.

###### Required Generalization or Interface Realization

Sometimes DSL requires elements to be subtypes of a general abstract [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='']. Use the **Super Types**property for this purpose. The **Super Types** property specifies types to be super types of the DSL element. The*Generalization*or*InterfaceRealization*(if using an interface) will be created in the model after applying the customized stereotype.

#### INFO: Example

Example

The following is an example of customization. Every JAVA class will be a subclass of the *Object*class. In other words, every Class marked with the «JAVAClass» stereotype is inherited from the Object.

1. Create a customization element.
2. In the customization Specification window, specify the **Customization Target**property as the «JAVAClass» stereotype.
3. In the customization Specification window, specify the **Super Types**property as the *Object* class.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

44

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='']
