<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145347 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00610: Initializing opposite association end

- page_id: `189145347`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145347/Initializing+opposite+association+end
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:36.158+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Working with Profiles > Creating Profiles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If two stereotypes are connected with an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']and there are roles at both ends, the role at one end will be automatically initialized when the opposite role is specified.

#### INFO: Example:

Example:

When «dog» Rex is set as a pet for «human» Tom, Tom will be automatically set as “main” for Rex:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association End' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>If two stereotypes are connected with an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Association ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and there are roles at both ends, the role at one end will be <span>automatically </span> initialized when the opposite role is specified. </span></p><p><span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="079e92f2-14af-4600-9586-9e0aabb85b18"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example:</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>When «dog» Rex is set as a pet for «human» Tom, Tom will be automatically set as “main” for Rex:</span></p><p><span>  <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Initializing Opposite Association End.png" /></ac:image><br /> </span></p></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> <br /> </span></p></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c94b9e3-fe28-468c-a38a-773ee720d4ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association End" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141075 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00611: Inserting a shape on the path

- page_id: `189141075`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141075/Inserting+a+shape+on+the+path
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:46.795+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['split-transition', 'insert-shape-on-path', 'split-path']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='']StateMachine and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''] diagrams.

In the State and Activity diagrams, you can split a path into two paths by dropping a shape on it. This is valid for Transition/ Control Flow/ Object Flow relationships and allows you to connect the element shape with the elements connected via the path. 
 
To insert a new shape by splitting the path on the diagram pane

1. Select the shape you want to insert or create a new one.
2. Drag the shape on the path (the path is highlighted in blue) and drop it.
3. In the **Split Transition** dialog, click the appropriate button according to where you want to insert the shape on the path:

- **Before Transition**button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means that a new path is created, then the dropped shape is drawn and then the existing path is drawn. For example, *Password read* transition is drawn from *Read Name* State to*Verification* State. If you want to insert the*Read password* State before the *Password read* Transition, drop the *Read password* State on the Transition, and in the **Split Transition** dialog, choose the **Before Transition** button.[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **After Transition**button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means the existing path is created, the dropped element symbol is drawn, and then a new path is drawn. For example, *Name**read* Transition is drawn from *Read Name* State to *Verification* State. If you want to insert the *Read**password* State after the *Name read* Transition, drop the *Read password* State on the Transition, and in the **Split Transition** dialog, choose the **After Transition** button.[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **Do Not Insert**button. Action is canceled and the dialog is closed.

In the **Split Transition** dialog, select the **Remember my choice** check box and the next time the shape will be inserted before or after the path, depending on your selection.

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be0eba18-1df8-4cbc-aa4e-4458ff1a6bfb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">State </span>Machine</ac:link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the State and Activity diagrams, you can split a path into two paths by dropping a shape on it. This is valid for Transition/ Control Flow/ Object Flow relationships and allows you to connect the element shape with the elements connected via the path. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To insert a new shape by splitting the path on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the shape you want to insert or create a new one.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Drag the shape on the path (the path is highlighted in blue) and drop it.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> In the <strong>Split Transition</strong> dialog, click the appropriate button according to where you want to insert the shape on the path:</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></li></ol><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Before Transition </strong>button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means that a new path is created, then the dropped shape is drawn and then the existing path is drawn.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b22b0b0c-fd35-4399-84a2-38e5a213de0b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, <em>Password read</em> transition is drawn from <em>Read Name</em> State to<em> Verification</em> State. If you want to insert the<em> Read password</em> State before the <em>Password read</em> Transition, drop the <em>Read password</em> State on the Transition, and in the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog, choose the <strong>Before Transition</strong> button.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="200" ac:width="160"><ri:attachment ri:filename="state_machine_diagram_2.png" /></ac:image><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="242" ac:width="163"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Insert_symbol_before_path_with_state.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>After <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Transition </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">button</span>. The shape is inserted before the path. It means the existing path is created, the dropped element symbol is drawn, and then a new path is drawn.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="125c74a4-a702-4fa7-af1e-ad6533f7bf0f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, <em>Name </em><em>read</em> Transition is drawn from <em>Read Name</em> State to <em>Verification</em> State. If you want to insert the <em>Read</em><em> password</em> State after the <em>Name read</em> Transition, drop the <em>Read password</em> State on the Transition, and in the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog, choose the <strong>After Transition</strong> button. </span></p><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="222" ac:width="159"><ri:attachment ri:filename="state_machine_diagram_1.png" /></ac:image><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="252" ac:width="154"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Insert_symbol_after_path_with_state.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Do Not Insert </strong>button. Action is canceled and the dialog is closed.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69da0fcd-52d9-42db-9ab9-6f589edb6390"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog</span>, select the <strong>Remember my choice</strong> check box and the next time the shape will be inserted before or after the path, depending on your selection.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Split Transition dialog" ac:alt="Split Transition dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="split_transition_dialog.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong><br /></strong></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142268 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00612: Inserting images into HTML text

- page_id: `189142268`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142268/Inserting+images+into+HTML+text
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:07.601+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > HTML editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

INLINE

To insert an image into HTML text

#### EXPAND: From the Containment tree

From the Containment tree

- When the image is already an AttachedFile, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with attached files' space=''] .

#### EXPAND: By using the Advanced HTML Editor

By using the Advanced HTML Editor

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Advanced HTML Editor dialog' space=''] .
2. On the toolbar, click Insert Image button [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_image_button.png'] , then click one of the following:
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_image_button.png'] From File – to browse the image in your file system or paste the attached image file URL into the Picture source field.
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_attached_image.png'] From Attached File – to open the Select Elements dialog and select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached.
  - [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **From Image Library** – to use images from a bundled image collection or your own added sets of images.
3. Click OK twice to close both dialogs.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging the image file from file system

By pasting or dragging the image file from file system

1. Open the image file location, select the file(s) you want to insert, then do one of the following:
  - copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the **Paste Special**menu, select **Image**to paste image and attach it to the project or **Image (without attaching)** to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag them to the HTML Editor. In the **Select Image Owner**, select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click **Cancel**, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.
2. Click**OK**to close the dialogs. If you chose to not attach the image, it is displayed in the HTML Editor, but when you delete the file from your file system, it is no longer displayed. That also means other users you want to share the project with won't be able to see the image if they open the project from another device.This option is very useful when the file you want to insert is large and you don't want to increase the project size. This feature works on both the HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser

By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser

1. Do either:
  - copy/paste the image to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select Image to paste image and attach it to the project or HTML Text to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag the image to the HTML Editor.
2. Click **OK** to close the dialogs. This feature works only on the HTML Editor. This feature works only with Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

1. Do either:
  - copy/paste content to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select HTML Text to choose whether paste it only for referencing in the diagram or attach it to the project, or select Image to paste and attach it to the project, or select Plain Text to paste only text without an image.
  - drag the image to the HTML Editor. In the Select Image Owner , select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click**Cancel**, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.
2. Click **OK** to close the dialogs. This feature works only on HTML Editor. Only rich text from MS Office files is supported. If the image from the rich text document is not stored in your file system, you are not asked to choose whether to attach the file or not.

To insert multiple image files

1. Open the image files location, select the files you want to insert, then do either:
  - copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select Image to paste images and attach them to the project or Image (without attaching) to paste them only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag them to the HTML Editor. In the Select Image Owner , select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile images should be attached.
2. Click OK to close the dialogs.

This feature works on both HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142387 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00613: Inspecting changes in the Change details panel

- page_id: `189142387`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142387/Inspecting+changes+in+the+Change+details+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:11.561+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['change-details', 'merge-result']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1991256206

INLINE

1991256208

INLINE

1991256207

INLINE

The **Change details** panel displays a tree structure reflecting the changes that occurred either on the element selected in the **Merged** **Result** tree/list or on the property selected in the **Specification** panel. The panel is located under the **Specification** panel on the **Merge** window.

The panel contains a tree structure that has two top-level branches – Source changes and Target changes – in 3-way merge, and 1 top-level branch – Source changes – in 2-way merge. In this panel, you can accept or reject changes using the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the **Dependent changes** branch under an element with dependent changes.

For the dependent changes description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space=''].

The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed to numerical/text types accordingly.

1991256204

INLINE

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Getting merge summary information and changes legend' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding content of the Merged Result panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying and navigating through changes' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quickly navigating through conflicting changes' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accepting and rejecting changes using shortcut menu' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing and analyzing changes of modified diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating a differences report' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143218 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00614: Inspecting differences in the Change details panel

- page_id: `189143218`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143218/Inspecting+differences+in+the+Change+details+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:48.608+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['change-details', 'differences', 'difference-viewer']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Change details** panel displays a tree structure reflecting the differences between the two versions of either the element selected in the **Differences** tree or the property selected in the **Specification** panel. If several elements or properties are selected, then all changes that have been detected in these elements or properties accordingly will be displayed in the **Change** **details** panel. The panel is located above the **Specification** panel on the **Difference Viewer** window.

The panel contains a tree structure that has 1 top-level branch – Changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the **Dependent changes** branch under an element with dependent changes.

For the dependent changes description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change types' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Change details</strong> panel displays a tree structure reflecting the differences between the two versions of either the element selected in the <strong>Differences</strong> tree or the property selected in the <strong>Specification</strong> panel. If several elements or properties are selected, then all changes that have been detected in these elements or properties accordingly will be displayed in the <strong>Change</strong> <strong>details</strong> panel. The panel is located above the <strong>Specification</strong> panel on the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> window.</p><p>The panel contains a tree structure that has 1 top-level branch <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="edfa489e-d703-4e81-84d4-4e561ff6619a">– Changes.</ac:inline-comment-marker>   <span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> <br /> </span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Change details dialog" ac:alt="Change details dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_details_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the <strong>Dependent changes</strong> branch under an element with dependent changes.</p><p>For the dependent changes description, r<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="319a5311-84d4-4531-8f99-20cc6ed2e97e">efer t</ac:inline-comment-marker>o <ac:link ac:anchor="dependentchange"><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding change types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Dependent changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143214 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00615: Inspecting element property changes in the differences Specification panel

- page_id: `189143214`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143214/Inspecting+element+property+changes+in+the+differences+Specification+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:48.290+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['property-changes', 'compare-specification-panel']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see all the details of the element property changes in the **Specification** panel that is located at the bottom of the **Difference Viewer** window. All you need to do is selecting the appropriate element in the **Differences** tree.

As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes that have been detected in both base and compared projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To navigate from the **Specification** panel to the **Differences** tree, do either

- In the Properties column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu, point to Select in Differences tree and choose an element to which you want to navigate.
- I n the Base or Compared column, right-click the value of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 **Inspecting differences between textual values**

You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post- conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are appropriately highlighted. As of version 18.3, the differences between compared text written in HTML are now represented as rich text, enabling you to see all changes in formatting as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 To see the differences of compared texts, do either

- Select a property with a modified text on the Specification panel and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='compare_property_values_button.png'] on the toolbar.
- Double-click the property on the Specification panel. The Compare property dialog opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142383 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00616: Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel

- page_id: `189142383`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142383/Inspecting+element+property+changes+in+the+Specification+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:11.437+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['merge-specification-panel', 'property-changes']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1086956613

1086956615

1086956614

You can see all the details of the element property changes in the **Specification** panel that is located at the top right of the **Merge** window. All you need to do is selecting the element in the **Merged Result** tree or list.

As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes that have been detected in both source and the and target. Third column Merge Results Preview shows the final merge result. Properties changes can be accepted or rejected in this panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To navigate from the **Specification** panel to the **Merged Result** tree, do either

- In the Properties column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu, point to Select in Merged Result Tree and choose an element to which you want to navigate.
- In the Source , Ancestor , Target or Merge Result Preview column, right-click the value of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Ancestor** column by default is hidden. To show it in the **Specification**panel, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the **Merge**toolbar and select **Show Ancestor**.

The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed into numerical/text types accordingly.

**Inspecting differences between textual values**

You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post-conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are highlighted.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
To see the differences of compared texts, do either

- Select a property with a modified text on the Specification panel and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='compare_property_values_button.png'] on the toolbar.
- Double-click the property on the Specification panel. The Compare property dialog opens.

1086956612

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144578 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00617: Installation

- page_id: `189144578`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144578/Installation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:00.811+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard template editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To install Report Wizard Template Editor in Microsoft Word

1. Copy all files and subdirectories from the <install.dir>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\vba folder to %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP**** (%APPDATA% is the location where user data is stored). For example,
  - On Windows 2000 and Windows XP: C:\Documents and Settings\User Name\Application Data\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP
  - On Windows Vista: C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP
  - On Windows 7, 8, and later: C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP
2. Restart Microsoft Word.

Microsoft Word 64bit may require mscomctl.ocx for this add-in. To install mscomctl.ocx:

1. Go to [https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019](https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019)

2. Download Microsoft Visual Basic 6.0 Common Controls

3. Install mscomctl.ocx by extracting VisualBasic6-????.exe and then copy mscomctl.ocx to C:\Windows\System32 or C:\Windows\SysWOW64

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To install Report Wizard Template Editor in Microsoft Word</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy all files and subdirectories from the <em>&lt;install.dir&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\vba</em> folder to <em>%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP<strong> </strong></em>(%APPDATA% is the location where user data is stored). For example,<br /><ul><li>On Windows 2000 and Windows XP: <em>C:\Documents and Settings\User Name\Application Data\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP</em></li><li>On Windows Vista: <em>C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP</em></li><li>On Windows 7, 8, and later: <em>C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP</em></li></ul></li><li>Restart Microsoft Word.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edbea50e-de80-4591-a358-d537c22b71e9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Microsoft Word 64bit may require mscomctl.ocx for this add-in. To install mscomctl.ocx:</p><p>1. Go to <a href="https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019">https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019</a></p><p>2. Download Microsoft Visual Basic 6.0 Common Controls</p><p>3. Install mscomctl.ocx by extracting VisualBasic6-????.exe and then copy mscomctl.ocx to C:\Windows\System32 or C:\Windows\SysWOW64</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=209257583 space=MD2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00619: Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin

- page_id: `209257583`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/209257583/Installing+Cameo+Collaborator+Publisher+plugin
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-02-04T14:55:03.234+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Using Cameo Collaborator
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To publish Cameo Collaborator documents to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you must install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin in your modeling tool.

#### WARNING: Compatibility

Compatibility

Make sure the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin version is compatible with the version of your modeling tool. See the [compatibility table](https://www.nomagic.com/support/compatibility#Cameo%20Collaborator%20Publisher%20plugin).

For information regarding installation, licensing, and system requirements, visit the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='IL2024xR2']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To publish Cameo Collaborator documents to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, you must install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin in your modeling tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e30a40f-77ee-403b-876b-18c68c81d94f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin version is compatible with the version of your modeling tool. See the <a href="https://www.nomagic.com/support/compatibility#Cameo%20Collaborator%20Publisher%20plugin">compatibility table</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>For information regarding installation, licensing, and system requirements, visit the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024xR2" ri:content-title="Installation, licensing, and system requirements" /></ac:link><span> page.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145124 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00620: Installing custom tool

- page_id: `189145124`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145124/Installing+custom+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:28.650+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To install the extension, copy the *JAR* file containing a custom tool class into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. Report Wizard will automatically load a new *JAR* file when a new report is generated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To install the extension, copy the <em>JAR</em> file containing a custom tool class into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. Report Wizard will automatically load a new <em>JAR</em> file when a new report is generated.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141501 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00621: Instance Specification

- page_id: `189141501`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141501/Instance+Specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:18.993+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: ['slot', 'slot-specification']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

72524162

72524165

116896173

**On this page**

4

72524164

INLINE

##### Introduction

An instance specification specifies the existence of an entity in a modeled system and completely or partiallydescribes the entity.

The description may include:

- The classification of an entity by one or more classifiers of which the entity is an instance. If the only classifier specified is abstract, then the instance specification only partially describes the entity.
- A kind of instance based on its classifier or classifiers. For example, an instance specification whose classifier is a class describes an object of that class, while an instance specification whose classifier is an association describes a link of that association.
- A specification of structural features values of the entity. Not all structural features of all classifiers of the instance specification need to be represented by slots, in which case the instance specification is a partial description.
- A specification of how to compute, derive, or construct the instance (optional).

Modeling tool allows you to create the instances of classifiers such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space=''],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manifestations' space=''].

In the Component (or Deployment) diagram Node Instance, Component Instance, Artifact Instance elements are the same Instance Specification elements with an assigned component, node or artifact.

The instances are shown using a rectangle by underlining the name string of an instance element. The instance of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='']is shown as an actor “stick man” figure with the actor’s name below the symbol.

You can specify instance properties in the instance [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. In the same window, you can find a description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window. If you need to handle a huge amount of instance specifications, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space=''].

##### Assigning a classifier to an Instance Specification

To assign a classifier to an instance, do one of the following

- On the diagram, click an instance shape, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In the open list, select the classifier.
- On the diagram, click an instance shape, and then press **Ctrl+T**. In the open list, select the classifier.
- On the diagram, click an instance shape, then click the name area. Type the “:” and then type the classifier name. Press **Enter**or click anywhere on the diagram. The classifier is created and assigned. Type the “:” colon, then press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+Backspace and then in the open list, select the classifier.
- In the Containment tree, select a classifier and drag it to the instance shape.

Use the Node Instance button to create the Instance Specification withassigned Node and the Node Instance will have a Node shape.

##### Assigning Instance Specification as Default Value

To assign Instance Specification as Default Value

- Drag Instance Specification element on Classifier property on a diagram.

Drag-and-drop feature is available only if Instance Specification classifiers are compatible with the Property type and ifProperty is editable.

Setting the initial value to an attribute of the assigned classifier

To set the initial value to an attribute of the assigned classifier

1. Open the Instance Specification window.
2. In the **Slots**property group, select a property and c lick the **Create Value**button.
3. Type the needed value in the field on the right.

##### Showing slots of the assigned classifier

To show slots of the assigned classifier

1. Select an instance shape and click the Compartments button [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
2. In the menu, select **Slots**.

By default slots of the classifier are suppressed.

##### Displaying slot type on the Instance Specification symbol

To display slot type on the instance symbol

1. Open instance **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']** dialog.
2. Set the **Show Slot Type**property to one of the following:

- None
- Name
- Qualified name

##### Displaying specification value on the Instance Specification symbol

To display specification value on the Instance Specification symbol

1. Open instance [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space=''] dialog.
2. Select the **Show Specification Value** check box.

##### Saving Instance values to the default property values of the Class (Block)

When you have a Class (Block) and an Instance of that Class (Block)which contains some particular values, you can save those values as default to the Values property of the Class (Block).

To save the Instance values as default

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Instance.
  - In the Containment tree, select an Instance.
  - In the Instance table, select an Instance.
2. Right-click an Instance and from the shortcut menu, select Tools > Save to Default Value(s) .
3. In the open dialog, select the needed options:
  - **Save to composite parts recursively**.
  - **Redefine inherited properties**.
  - Save derived property values . [ATTACHMENT filename='save_to_default_values.png']
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of Instance property values saved as default in the Block value properties. The "Save to composite parts recursively" option was selected.

72524160

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="760fdf2d-c081-427b-b5a9-a1f6d245a08c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">72524162</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f37872a-fe54-4b87-a500-93791efba476"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">72524165</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1cde8414-1e30-4bf1-8d90-8b2849089f88"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">116896173</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b95a1e46-af1b-4e25-bcb5-30f02d189187"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bfdd79c-7276-4841-bd2f-5e4614a625b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">72524164</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57173d36-b39a-4f48-8d7b-26040a4085e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Introduction</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An instance specification specifies the existence of an entity in a modeled system and completely or partially </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">describes the entity.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The description may include:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The classification of an entity by one or more classifiers of which the entity is an instance. If the only classifier specified is abstract, then the instance specification only partially describes the entity.</span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(199,200,202);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(168,169,173);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A kind of instance based on its classifier or classifiers. For example, an instance specification whose classifier is a class describes an object of that class, while an instance specification whose classifier is an association describes a link of that association.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(199,200,202);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(168,169,173);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A specification of structural features v<span>alues</span> of the entity. Not all structural features of all classifiers of the instance specification need to be represented by slots, in which case the instance specification is a partial description.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(199,200,202);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(168,169,173);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A specification of how to compute, derive, or construct the instance (optional).</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Modeling tool allows you to create the instances of classifiers such as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[class]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interface]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enumeration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[enumeration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use case]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[actor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Node" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[node]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Component" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[component]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manifestations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[artifact]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the Component (or Deployment) diagram Node Instance, Component Instance, Artifact Instance elements are the same Instance Specification elements with an assigned component, node or artifact.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The instances are shown using a rectangle by underlining the name string of an instance element. The instance of an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[actor ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>is shown as an actor “stick man” figure with the actor’s name below the symbol.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span>You can specify instance properties in the instance <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. In the same window, you can find a description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window. <span>If you need to handle a huge amount of instance specifications, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Instance table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[instance table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></span></span></span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Assigning a classifier to an Instance Specification</span><br /></span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To assign a classifier to an instance, do one of the following</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">On the diagram, click an instance shape, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Specify classifier" ac:alt="Specify classifier"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specify_classifier.png" /></ac:image>. In the open list, select the classifier.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">On the diagram, click an instance shape, and then press <strong>Ctrl+T</strong>. In the open list, select the classifier.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">On the diagram, click an instance shape, then click the name area. Type the “:” and then type the classifier name. Press <strong>Enter </strong>or click anywhere on the diagram. The classifier is created and assigned.<br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d7e8dfb-0b4c-4ecc-893c-1600df9969f9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Type the “:” colon, then press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+Backspace and then in the open list, select the classifier.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the Containment tree, select a classifier and drag it to the instance shape.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Use the Node Instance button to create the Instance Specification with </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">assigned Node and the Node Instance will have a Node shape.</span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Assigning Instance Specification as Default Value</span><br /></span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To assign Instance Specification as Default Value</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span>Drag Instance Specification element on Classifier property on a diagram.</span></span></span></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59526042-f1f1-4b89-a141-3b6119830bea"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Drag-and-drop feature is available only if Instance Specification classifiers are compatible with the Property type and if </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Property is editable.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;letter-spacing: -0.006em;"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;letter-spacing: -0.006em;">S</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;letter-spacing: -0.006em;">etting the initial value to an attribute of the assigned classifier</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /> </span>To set the initial value to an attribute of the assigned classifier</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Open the Instance Specification window.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the <strong>Slots </strong>property group, select a property and c</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">lick the </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Create Value </strong>button.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Type the needed value in the field on the right.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Showing slots of the assigned classifier</span><br /></span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To show slots of the assigned classifier</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select an instance shape and click the Compartments button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartments.png" /></ac:image>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the menu, select <strong>Slots</strong>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcc298f1-10a6-4be1-8e08-8f174e553f7f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">By default slots of the classifier are suppressed.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Displaying slot type on the Instance Specification symbol</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To display slot type on the instance symbol</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Open instance <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Set the </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Show Slot Type </strong>property to one of the following:</span></li></ol><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">None</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Name</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Qualified name</span></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(199,200,202);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(168,169,173);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Displaying specification value on the Instance Specification symbol</span><br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To display specification value on the Instance Specification symbol</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Open instance </span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> dialog.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select the <strong>Show Specification Value</strong> check box.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Saving Instance values to the default property values of the Class (Block)</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">When you have a <span>Class (Block)</span> and an Instance of that <span>Class (Block) </span>which contains some particular values, you can save those values as default to the Values property of the Class (Block).</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To save the Instance values as default</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the diagram pane, select an Instance.</li><li>In the Containment tree, select an Instance.</li><li>In the Instance table, select an Instance.</li></ul></li><li>Right-click an Instance and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt;<strong> Save to Default Value(s)</strong>.</li><li>In the open dialog, select the needed options:<ul><li><p><strong>Save to composite parts recursively</strong>.</p></li><li><p><strong>Redefine inherited properties</strong>.</p></li><li><strong>Save derived property values</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_to_default_values.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_to_default_values_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of Instance property values saved as default in the Block value properties. The &quot;Save to composite parts recursively&quot; option was selected.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="487cc943-1a6b-47f9-985e-b35d790c625a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">72524160</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140766 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00622: Instance table

- page_id: `189140766`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140766/Instance+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:10.804+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

221798943

221798946

221798941

**On this page**

221798945

##### **Description**

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The Instance Table is one of four tables which allow you to manage Instance Specifications of your model in a spreadsheet-like form. Each row in the table represents an instance.****

##### **Purpose**

An Instance Table allows you to:

- Create Instance Specifications (instances) for the classifiers.
- Review the instances of one or more of the classifiers in a single place.
- Edit Slot values of the instances displayed in the table.
- Customize the representation of the table.
- Export the data into .html , .csv, or * .xlsx files.

##### **Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Specific tasks in the Instance Table**

The following outlines the specific procedures when working with the Instance Table:

221837881

Each row in the table represents an instance.<strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>Purpose</strong></h3><p>An Instance Table allows you to:</p><ul><li>Create Instance Specifications (instances) for the classifiers.</li><li>Review the instances of one or more of the classifiers in a single place.</li><li>Edit Slot values of the instances displayed in the table.</li><li>Customize the representation of the table.</li><li>Export the data into <em>.html</em>, <em>.csv,</em> or *<em>.xlsx</em> files.</li></ul><h3><strong>Example</strong></h3><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h3><strong>Specific tasks in the Instance Table</strong></h3><p>The following outlines the specific procedures when working with the Instance Table:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="92c20da9-bc9d-453a-acda-884f64ca0f32" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13f10737-34f5-4087-ad3c-e06ad59cedd9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">221837881</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Basic tasks in tables" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Hierarchy in tables" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generic table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Glossary table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Metric table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00624: Interaction

- page_id: `189141509`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141509/Interaction
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:19.332+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Interaction is a unit of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space=''] focusing on the observable exchange of information between connectable elements, a specialization of Interaction Fragment and of Behavior.

An Interaction originates in a modeling tool while creatingCommunicationandSequencediagrams. When you double click on an Interaction, a diagram is opened. 
In theelementdialog box, you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. For more information about element dialog boxes and panes of the element dialog boxes,click here. 
 
To open the**Interaction**dialog box, choose the**Specification**command from the Interaction shortcut menu. 
 
In the**Interaction**dialog box, the**Messages**pane lists Messages contained in this Interaction.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Interaction is a unit of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link> focusing on the observable exchange of information between connectable elements, a specialization of Interaction Fragment and of Behavior.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An Interaction originates in a modeling tool while creating </span><a>Communication</a><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> and </span><a>Sequence</a><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> diagrams. When you double click on an Interaction, a diagram is opened. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">element</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> dialog box, you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. For more information about element dialog boxes and panes of the element dialog boxes, </span><a>click here</a><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To open the </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Interaction</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> dialog box, choose the </span><strong>Specification</strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> command from the Interaction shortcut menu.   </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Interaction</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> dialog box, the </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Messages</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> pane lists Messages contained in this Interaction.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141510 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00625: Interaction operand

- page_id: `189141510`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141510/Interaction+operand
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:19.721+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Interaction
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Interaction operand represents an Interaction with an optional guard expression. The Interaction operand is executed only if the guard expression tests true at runtime. The object that starts the interaction requires all the information necessary to make the decision about whether to proceed. The absence of any guard condition means that the interaction is always executed. 
In the Interaction operand [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''], you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An Interaction operand represents an Interaction with an optional guard expression. The Interaction operand is executed only if the guard expression tests true at runtime. The object that starts the interaction requires all the information necessary to make the decision about whether to proceed. The absence of any guard condition means that the interaction is always executed. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Interaction operand <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140259 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00626: Interaction Overview diagram

- page_id: `189140259`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140259/Interaction+Overview+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:49.028+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308856586

308856588

308856587

**Overview**

An Interaction Overview diagram is a type of Interaction diagram, which falls under the behavioral diagramming family.An Interaction Overview diagram focuses on the flow of control between interactions. It is based on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''] notation. Interactions in the Interaction Overview diagram are represented using the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space=''] element. All elements in the diagram, except for the Interaction Use, are the same as in the Activity diagram.

**Purpose**

An Interaction Overview diagram is intended to be used to model the flow of logic in a series of interactions. To put it another way, the Interaction Overview diagram uses blocks of interactions and individual interactions instead of modeling a series of discrete behaviors (activities).

**Usage**

Interaction Overview diagrams visualize a sequence of activities just like Activity diagrams do; however, they picture each individual entry as a frame which can contain a nested interaction diagram.

**Summary**

Interaction Overview diagrams are valuable because they:

- provide a high-level view of the logical progression through a set of interactions
- keep the focus on the "big picture" and away from implementation details
- encourage the reuse of interactions

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of an Interaction Overview diagram

308856585

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']

To put it another way, the Interaction Overview diagram uses blocks of interactions and individual interactions instead of modeling a series of discrete behaviors (activities). </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>Interaction Overview diagrams visualize a sequence of activities just like Activity diagrams do; however, they picture each individual entry as a frame which can contain a nested interaction diagram. </p><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Interaction Overview diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>provide a high-level view of the logical progression through a set of interactions</li><li>keep the focus on the &quot;big picture&quot; and away from implementation details</li><li>encourage the reuse of interactions </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interaction_overview_diagram_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of an Interaction Overview diagram<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2a23b37-52a4-4422-9417-7709aa16f0f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308856585</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00627: Interaction Overview diagram elements

- page_id: `189139858`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139858/Interaction+Overview+diagram+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:27.321+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import > Special Transformation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

560251696

560251698

560251694

**On this page**

3

560251697

The following sections describe additional EA specific Interaction Overview diagram information.

##### Interaction Overview Diagram

An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Overview diagram' space=''] is one of four types of Interaction diagrams. The other three are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time diagram' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space='']diagrams. An Interaction Overview diagram, just like an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''], visualizes a sequence of activities. Most of the notation elements for Interaction Overview diagrams are the same as those for Activity diagrams (such as Initial, Decision, Fork, Join, and Final [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='']). Interaction Occurrences and Interaction elements are two new elements in the interaction overview diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Overview Diagram.

Afterconversion, the Interaction Overview diagram will be placed within anActivityelement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Overview Diagram Placement.

###### Interaction Element

Interaction elements display an inline Interaction diagram (Interaction Overview, Timing, Sequence, or Communication diagram). In EA, Interaction elements can be created to display other diagrams than those classified as Interaction diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Element in Interaction Overview Diagram.

###### Interaction Occurrence

Interaction Occurrence elements refer to an existing Interaction diagram. They are visually represented by a rectangular frame, which can be created by dragging the Interaction diagram from the project Browser to an Interaction Overview diagram and selecting the **Interaction Occurrence** command from the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Occurence element.

560258099

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Overview diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141511 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00628: Interaction Use

- page_id: `189141511`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141511/Interaction+Use
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:19.954+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Interaction
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Interactions are units of behavior of an enclosing Classifier. They focus on the passing of information with Messages between the Connectable Elements of the Classifier.

A reference to the interaction can be created.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space=''] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']Specification window.

To add a reference to an interaction use, do one of the following

- Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of the interaction use and click the cell of the **Refers To**property value. Then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and in the open dialog, select the element. Click **OK**when you are done.
- On the diagram, right-click the interaction use shape, and then point to **Refers To**. Then select an element you wish to refere to.
- On the diagram, click an interaction use shape, and then press **Ctrl+T**. Then select an element you wish to refere to.
- On the diagram, click an interaction use shape, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Then select an element you wish to refere to.
- In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the interaction use shape.

To add an actual [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Gate' space='']

1. Add a reference to the diagram, from which frame the formal message is created.
2. Draw an actual message with the selected formal [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Gate' space=''] to the current interaction use.

The rake icon[IMAGE alt='' src='']is displayed on the shape if the interaction use refers to another sequence diagram. The rake icon is not shown by default therefore you need to set the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying rake icon' space='']property value to true in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']dialog.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00629: Interface

- page_id: `189141514`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141514/Interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:20.026+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An interface is a specifier for the externally-visible operations of a class, component, or other classifiers (including subsystems) without a specification of the internal structure. Each interface often specifies only a limited part of the behavior of an actual class.

The set of interfaces realized by a classifier is its provided interfaces, which represent the obligations that instances of that classifier have to their clients. They describe the services that the instances of that classifier offer to their clients.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space=''] in the interface [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='']Activity diagram

Each interface often specifies only a limited part of the behavior of an actual class.</p><p>The set of interfaces realized by a classifier is its provided interfaces, which represent the obligations that instances of that classifier have to their clients. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141523 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00630: Interface realization

- page_id: `189141523`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141523/Interface+realization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:20.247+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Interface realization is a specialized [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realization' space=''] relationship between a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Classifier Behavior property' space='CST190'] and an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space=''], signifying that the realizing classifier conforms to the contract specified by the Interface.

A classifier that implements an interface specifies instances conforming to the interface and its ancestors. A classifier may implement a number of interfaces.

In the Interface realization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''], you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. 
 
To open theInterfaceRealizationdialog box, choose theSpecificationcommand from the Realization shortcut menu. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the Interface Realization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''], the Implementing Classifier property references the classifier that realizes the Interface to which it points.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Interface realization is a specialized <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realization" /></ac:link> relationship between a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST190" ri:content-title="Classifier Behavior property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Classifier Behavior]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interface" /></ac:link>, signifying that the realizing classifier conforms to the contract specified by the Interface.</p><p>A classifier that implements an interface specifies instances conforming to the interface and its ancestors. A classifier may implement a number of interfaces.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> In the Interface realization <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To open the </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Interface</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Realization</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> dialog box, choose the </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Specification</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> command from the Realization shortcut menu. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Interface_realization.png" /></ac:image> </span></p><p>In the <span class="hcp1">Interface</span> <span class="hcp1">Realization</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, the <span class="hcp1">Implementing</span> <span class="hcp1">Classifier</span> property references the classifier that realizes the Interface to which it points.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141525 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00631: Internal Transition

- page_id: `189141525`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141525/Internal+Transition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:20.285+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In all other cases, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space=''] label identifies the event that triggered the corresponding Action expression. These events are called Internal Transitions and are semantically equivalent to self transitions except that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space=''] is neither exited nor re-entered. This means that the corresponding exit and entry actions are not performed.

#### TIP: Fork And Join

Fork And Join

For more information about defining transitions, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fork and Join' space=''].

Specify the Internal Transition in the Transition's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

#### TIP: Fork And Join

Fork And Join

For a more detailed description of this dialog, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space=''].

To create and specify an Internal Transition

In your project, if you have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space=''] created, do the following:

1. Double-click the State or select Specification from the State's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH185'] . The State's Specification Window opens.
2. Click the Internal Transitions group.
3. Click the Create button. The Transition's Specification window opens.
4. Specify an Internal Transition .
5. Click Close.

To remove an Internal Transition

1. Double-click the State or select Specification from the State's shortcut menu. The State's Specification Window opens.
2. Click the Internal Transitions group.
3. The Transition's Specification window opens.
4. Click Delete . Then, Click Close .

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fork and Join' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='']
