int main() { </span></p><p><span>char buf[100];<br /> int __unaligned *p1 = (int*)(&amp;buf[37]); int *p2 = (int *)p1;<br /> *p1=0; //ok<br /> __try { </span></p><p><span>*p2 = 0; // throws an exception } </span></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Code</th><th>MD-UML</th></tr><tr><td><pre>__interface IEvents { </pre><pre>  };</pre><pre>class CSource { </pre><pre>public:</pre><pre>   __event __interface IEvents; </pre><pre>};</pre></td><td><p>__event __interface IEvents;</p><p>is mapped to attribute without name </p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span>Code </span></p><p><span>__except(1) { puts(&quot;exception&quot;); </span></p><p><span>}} </span></p><p><span>int __unaligned *p1; </span></p><p><span>MD-UML </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>221 </span></p><p><span>Copyright © 1998-2015 No Magic, Inc. </span></p><p><span>C++ CODE ENGINEERING </span><span>Microsoft C++ Profiles </span></p><p><span>__w64 </span></p><p><span>type </span><span>__w64 </span><span>identifier </span></p><p><span>Parameters </span></p><p><span>type </span></p><p><span>One of the three types that might cause problems in code being ported from a 32-bit to a 64-bit compiler: </span><span>int</span><span>, </span><span>long</span><span>, or a pointer. </span></p><p><span>identifier<br /> The identifier for the variable you are creating. </span></p><p><span>__w64 lets you mark variables, such that when you compile with /Wp64 the compiler will report any warnings that would be reported if you were compiling with a 64-bit compiler. </span></p><p><span>Example </span></p><p><span>// compile with: /W3 /Wp64 typedef int Int_32;<br /> #ifdef _WIN64<br /> typedef __int64 Int_Native; #else </span></p><p><span>typedef int __w64 Int_Native; #endif<br /> int main() { </span></p><p><span>Int_32 i0 = 5;<br /> Int_Native i1 = 10;<br /> i0 = i1; // C4244 64-bit int assigned to 32-bit int </span></p><p><span>// char __w64 c; error, cannot use __w64 on char } </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>222 </span></p><p><span>Copyright © 1998-2015 No Magic, Inc. </span></p><p><span>C++ CODE ENGINEERING </span><span>Microsoft C++ Profiles </span></p><table><tbody><tr><th>Code</th><th>MD-UML</th></tr><tr><td><pre>__interface IEvents { </pre><pre>  };</pre><pre>class CSource { </pre><pre>public:</pre><pre>   __event __interface IEvents; </pre><pre>};</pre></td><td><p>__event __interface IEvents;</p><p>is mapped to attribute without name </p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>Code </span></p><p><span>int __w64 i; </span></p><p><span>Extended storage-class attributes with __declspec </span></p><p><span>The extended attribute syntax for specifying storage-class information uses the </span><span>__declspec </span><span>keyword, which specifies that an instance of a given type is to be stored with a Microsoft-specific storage-class attribute listed below. Examples of other storage-class modifiers include the </span><span>static </span><span>and </span><span>extern </span><span>keywords. However, these key- words are part of the ANSI specification of the C and C++ languages, and as such are not covered by extended attribute syntax. The extended attribute syntax simplifies and standardizes Microsoft-specific extensions to the C and C++ languages. </span></p><p><span>decl-specifier:<br /> </span><span>__declspec ( </span><span>extended-decl-modifier-seq </span><span>) </span><span>extended-decl-modifier-seq: extended-decl-modifieropt<br /> extended-decl-modifier extended-decl-modifier-seq extended-decl-modifier:<br /> </span><span>align(</span><span>#</span><span>)<br /> allocate(&quot;</span><span>segname</span><span>&quot;)<br /> appdomain<br /> deprecated<br /> dllimport<br /> dllexport<br /> jitintrinsic<br /> naked </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>MD-UML </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>223 Copyright © 1998-2015 No Magic, Inc. </span></p><p><span>C++ CODE ENGINEERING </span><span>Microsoft C++ Profiles </span></p><p><span>noalias<br /> noinline<br /> noreturn<br /> nothrow<br /> novtable<br /> process property({get</span><span>=get_func_name|</span><span>,put</span><span>=put_func_name</span><span>}) restrict </span></p><p><span>selectany<br /> thread uuid(&quot;</span><span>ComObjectGUID</span><span>&quot;) </span></p><p><span>White space separates the declaration modifier sequence. </span></p><p><span>Extended attribute grammar supports these Microsoft-specific storage-class attributes: </span><span>align</span><span>, </span><span>allocate</span><span>, </span><span>appdo- main</span><span>, </span><span>deprecated</span><span>, </span><span>dllexport</span><span>, </span><span>dllimport</span><span>, </span><span>jitintrinsic</span><span>, </span><span>naked</span><span>, </span><span>noalias</span><span>, </span><span>noinline</span><span>, </span><span>noreturn</span><span>, </span><span>nothrow</span><span>, </span><span>novtable</span><span>, </span><span>process</span><span>, </span><span>restrict</span><span>, </span><span>selectany</span><span>, and </span><span>thread</span><span>. It also supports these COM-object attributes: </span><span>property </span><span>and </span><span>uuid</span><span>. </span></p><p><span>The </span><span>dllexport</span><span>, </span><span>dllimport</span><span>, </span><span>naked</span><span>, </span><span>noalias</span><span>, </span><span>nothrow</span><span>, </span><span>property</span><span>, </span><span>restrict</span><span>, </span><span>selectany</span><span>, </span><span>thread</span><span>, and </span><span>uuid </span><span>storage- class attributes are properties only of the declaration of the object or function to which they are applied. The </span><span>thread </span><span>attribute affects data and objects only. The </span><span>naked </span><span>attribute affects functions only. The </span><span>dllimport </span><span>and </span><span>dll- export </span><span>attributes affect functions, data, and objects. The </span><span>property</span><span>, </span><span>selectany</span><span>, and </span><span>uuid </span><span>attributes affect COM objects. </span></p><p><span>The </span><span>__declspec </span><span>keywords should be placed at the beginning of a simple declaration. The compiler ignores, without warning, any </span><span>__declspec </span><span>keywords placed after * or &amp; and in front of the variable identifier in a declara- tion. </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>Note: </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(90,109,148);">• </span><span>A </span><span>__declspec </span><span>attribute specified in the beginning of a user-defined type declaration applies to the variable of that type. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(90,109,148);">• </span><span>A </span><span>__declspec </span><span>attribute placed after the </span><span>class </span><span>or </span><span>struct </span><span>keyword applies to the user-defined type. </span></p><p><br /></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Code</th><th>MD-UML</th></tr><tr><td><pre>__interface IEvents { </pre><pre>  };</pre><pre>class CSource { </pre><pre>public:</pre><pre>   __event __interface IEvents; </pre><pre>};</pre></td><td><p>__event __interface IEvents;</p><p>is mapped to attribute without name </p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>Code MD-UML </span></p><p><span>class __declspec(dllimport) X {}; </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>224 </span></p><p><span>Copyright © 1998-2015 No Magic, Inc. </span></p><p><span>C++ CODE ENGINEERING </span><span>Microsoft C++ Profiles </span></p><table><tbody><tr><th>Code</th><th>MD-UML</th></tr><tr><td><pre>__interface IEvents { </pre><pre>  };</pre><pre>class CSource { </pre><pre>public:</pre><pre>   __event __interface IEvents; </pre><pre>};</pre></td><td><p>__event __interface IEvents;</p><p>is mapped to attribute without name </p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>Code MD-UML </span></p><p><span>__declspec(dllimport) class X {} varX; </span></p><p><span>__restrict </span></p><p><span>The </span><span>__restrict </span><span>keyword is valid only on variables, and </span><span>__declspec(restrict) </span><span>is only valid on function declara- tions and definitions. </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>225 Copyright © 1998-2015 No Magic, Inc. </span></p><p><span>C++ CODE ENGINEERING </span><span>Microsoft C++ Profiles </span></p><p><span>When </span><span>__restrict </span><span>is used, the compiler will not propagate the no-alias property of a variable. That is, if you assign a </span><span>__restrict </span><span>variable to a non-</span><span>__restrict </span><span>variable, the compiler will not imply that the non-</span><span>__restrict </span><span>variable is not aliased. </span></p><p><span>Generally, if you affect the behavior of an entire function, it is better to use the </span><span>__declspec </span><span>than the keyword. </span><span>__restrict </span><span>is similar to </span><span>restrict </span><span>from the C99 spec, but </span><span>__restrict </span><span>can be used in C++ or C programs. </span></p><p><span>Example </span></p><p><span>// __restrict_keyword.c<br /> // compile with: /LD<br /> // In the following function, declare a and b as disjoint arrays // but do not have same assurance for c and d.<br /> void sum2(int n, int * __restrict a, int * __restrict b, </span></p><p><span>int * c, int * d) { int i; </span></p><p><span>for (i = 0; i &lt; n; i++) { a[i] = b[i] + c[i]; c[i] = b[i] + d[i]; </span></p><p><span>} } </span></p><ul><li><p><span>//  By marking union members as __restrict, tells the compiler that </span></p></li><li><p><span>//  only z.x or z.y will be accessed in any given scope. union z { </span></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><span>int * __restrict x; </span></p><p><span>double * __restrict y; }; </span></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Code</th><th>MD-UML</th></tr><tr><td><pre>__interface IEvents { </pre><pre>  };</pre><pre>class CSource { </pre><pre>public:</pre><pre>   __event __interface IEvents; </pre><pre>};</pre></td><td><p>__event __interface IEvents;</p><p>is mapped to attribute without name </p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span>Code </span></p><p><span>MD-UML </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>int * __restrict x; </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>226 </span></p><p><span>Copyright © 1998-2015 No Magic, Inc. </span></p><p><span>C++ CODE ENGINEERING </span><span>Microsoft C++ Profiles </span></p><p><span>__forceinline, __inline </span></p><p><span>The insertion (called inline expansion or inlining) occurs only if the compiler's cost/benefit analysis show it to be profitable. Inline expansion alleviates the function-call overhead at the potential cost of larger code size. </span></p><p><span>The </span><span>__forceinline </span><span>keyword overrides the cost/benefit analysis and relies on the judgment of the programmer instead. Exercise caution when using </span><span>__forceinline</span><span>. Indiscriminate use of </span><span>__forceinline </span><span>can result in larger code with only marginal performance gains or, in some cases, even performance losses (due to increased pag- ing of a larger executable, for example). </span></p><p><span>Using inline functions can make your program faster because they eliminate the overhead associated with function calls. Functions expanded inline are subject to code optimizations not available to normal functions. </span></p><p><span>The compiler treats the inline expansion options and keywords as suggestions. There is no guarantee that functions will be inlined. You cannot force the compiler to inline a particular function, even with the </span><span>__forcein- line </span><span>keyword. When compiling with </span><span>/clr</span><span>, the compiler will not inline a function if there are security attributes applied to the function. </span></p><p><span>The </span><span>inline </span><span>keyword is available only in C++. The </span><span>__inline </span><span>and </span><span>__forceinline </span><span>keywords are available in both C and C++. For compatibility with previous versions, </span><span>_inline </span><span>is a synonym for </span><span>__inline</span><span>. </span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>Grammar: </span></p><p><span>__inline </span><span>function_declarator</span><span>; </span><span>__forceinline </span><span>function_declarator</span><span>; </span></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Code</th><th>MD-UML</th></tr><tr><td><pre>__interface IEvents { </pre><pre>  };</pre><pre>class CSource { </pre><pre>public:</pre><pre>   __event __interface IEvents; </pre><pre>};</pre></td><td><p>__event __interface IEvents;</p><p>is mapped to attribute without name </p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>Code </span></p><p><span>__inline int max( int a , int b ) { if( a &gt; b ) </span></p><pre><span> return a; return b; </span></pre><p><span>} </span></p><p><span>MD-UML</span></p><h3><span>C++ Attributes </span></h3><p><span>Attributes are designed to simplify COM programming and .NET Framework common language runtime development. When you include attributes in your source files, the compiler works with provider DLLs to insert code or modify the code in the generated object files. </span></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Code</th><th>MD-UML</th></tr><tr><td><pre>[ coclass,</pre><pre>aggregatable(allowed), </pre><pre>  uuid(&quot;1a8369cc-1c91-</pre><pre>42c4-befa-5a5d8c9d2529&quot;)] </pre><pre>class CMyClass {};</pre><pre><br /></pre></td><td><p>__event __interface IEvents;</p><p>is mapped to attribute without name </p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139864 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00666: Known Limitations and Constraints

- page_id: `189139864`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139864/Known+Limitations+and+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:27.625+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following tableshows the conversion limitations and constraints in EAexported XMIand EAImport.

| No. | Constraint name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | N-ary Association | Due to the difference between the EA exported XMI and MagicDraw, CEA or CSM XMI, the view of will not be imported. |
| 2 | Diagram Legend | MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM does not have a similar element. Diagram legends will not be imported. |
| 3 | Diagram Note | The element most similar to Diagram Note is the MagicDraw, CEA and CSM diagram information table (Option > Show diagram info). However, in EA it will be mapped to the Option > Show diagram details. So Diagram Notes will not be imported to MagicDraw, CEA or CSM. |
| 4 | Text size | The size of text displayed in EA and MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM may vary. The text size in EA is mostly larger. |
| 5 | Word wrap | Due to size constraints in text displayed in element blocks, the result of word wrap in EA and MagicDraw, CEA, and CSM is likely to be different. |
| 6 | Contact point between element and link | The position of both ends of a link element cannot be mapped to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM because the last segments of the link element in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM always point to the center of connected elements. |
| 7 | Display alternative image | In EA, you can display an element in a diagram by using an alternative image. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM will not import that particular image and will use a default shape instead. |
| 8 | Link label position | A Link element, such as an Association, has many labels, including multiplicity and role name labels. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM will not import the position of these labels and will use a default position instead. |
| 9 | Pin position | A position in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM may have been slightly moved from its original position in EA. If the pin is placed at the corner of its containing element, it will be shifted a little away from the corner (mostly in a clockwise direction). |
| 10 | Nested CallBehavior- Action | A element nested with another CallBehaviorAction element. The outermost part of the element will remain. The other will be removed. |
| 11 | DataStoreNode inner element | A DataStoreNode element containing Activity Diagram related elements. Every element inside that particular DataStoreNode element will be removed. Only the DataStoreNode element will remain. |
| 12 | Object inner element | An Object element containing Activity Diagram related elements. Every element inside that particular Object element will be removed. Only the Object element will remain. |
| 13 | Lifeline position | The position in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM will not correspond to the original position in EA. The position is fixed. |
| 14 | Diagonal Sequence Message | MagicDraw, CEA, and CSM do not support Diagonal Sequence Messages. |
| 15 | Sequence Activation Options | MagicDraw, CEA, and CSM do not support manipulating Sequence Activation through Sequence Activation Options. |
| 16 | State contains diagram | A diagram element placed inside a element will be removed. |
| 17 | Region in Orthogonal State | EA exported XMI contains incorrect information when:more than one region has identical names.a region was created and then removed before exporting to XMI.This will cause an unexpected result after importing it into MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. |
| 18 | Assembly Relationship in Composite Structure diagram | Assembly relationships in EA are exported to XMI as Connectors. They will then be imported to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM as . |
| 19 | Interaction elements as diagram frame | MagicDraw, CEA, and CSM do not import Interaction elements displayed as a diagram frame in an diagram. |
| 20 | Message timing details | Duration Observation, and Timing Observation are not imported to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. |
| 21 | Concurrent State Regions in StateMachine | Adding and removing multiple Concurrent State Regions to and from StateMachine in EA can cause the EA XMI to be incorrectly exported. If the EA exported XMI is in this state, the result of the imported to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM cannot be determined.The same problem also occurs if there are multiple Concurrent State Regions with the same name. |
| 22 | Combined Fragment | Adding and removing multiple Interaction Operands to and from Combined Fragment in EA can cause the EA XMI to be incorrectly exported. The problem can be fixed by importing the EA XMI back to a new project in EA and exporting it back before importing it to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. |
| 23 | Problem occurs during conversion of . | Importing EA XMI to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM sometimes results in the following error message: “Problem occurred during conversion of Sequence diagram”. If this occurs, please try to import the XMI back to a new project in EA and export it back again before importing it to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141120 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00667: Label layout in the diagram

- page_id: `189141120`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141120/Label+layout+in+the+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:53.554+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Layout
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Default label positions

Default label positions are retained after moving a path, shape, or related element if it is a semantically logical decision. See the example below, where association multiplicities remain at their default positions after class is moved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If labels are at their default position, reset labels position functionality is not available.

##### Label positions after moving a path, shape, or related element

After moving a path, shape, or related element, default label positions are retained if it is a semantically logical decision.

For a nicer representation of labels in a diagram, the label positions are reset to default automatically if:

1. Symbol properties are edited. When symbol properties are edited by adding or removing a label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.
2. Path, path end, or port properties are edited. When path, path end, or port data are edited by adding or removing the label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.
3. Path, shape, or related element are moved.

To check the label deviation from its default position

- Select a label and begin to move it. The dashed line shows the deviation from the default position. This helps to show the current label's owner.

When the label is not in its default position, you may indicate it by the icon on the bottom right corner, which appears after selecting the label's owner.

##### Label consideration mode

When arranging shapes for the hierarchic layout mechanisms, you may choose the **Label Consideration** mode:

- Hierarchic Layout.
- Class Diagram Layout.
- Composite Diagram Layout.
- Activity Diagram Layout.
- State Machine Diagram Layout.
- Business Process Diagram Layout.
- Ordered Hierarchic Layout.

In order to do this, you must specify the **Label consideration** mode (Full, Partial, or None) in the **Diagram Layout Options** dialog.

To specify the label consideration mode

1. From the diagram toolbar, expand [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diagram_layout.png'] and select Layout Options command.
2. On the left side of the dialog, select one of the following layout styles: - Under the General Layout group, select Hierarchic Layout . - Under the Specialized Layout group, select any layout style:
  - Class Diagram Layout.
  - Composite Diagram Layout.
  - Activity Diagram Layout.
  - State Machine Diagram Layout.
  - Business Process Diagram Layout.
  - Ordered Hierarchic Layout.
3. On the right side of the dialog, find the Label consideration option.
4. Click on the value of this option and select one of the following modes:
  - None - does not consider any labels, does not increase the shape size.
  - Partial - considers only the center path labels, does not increase shape sizes by labels.
  - Full - considers all labels, creates additional space for them and increases the shape sizes.
5. Click the Layout button.
6. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Default label positions</h3><p>Default label positions are retained after moving a path, shape, or related element if it is a semantically logical decision. See the example below, where association multiplicities remain at their default positions after class is moved.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Default label position example" ac:alt="Default label position example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="labels_defaul1.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0ec3e34-5f2c-4d0e-8d61-db04d8ee6bef"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If labels are at their default position, reset labels position functionality is not available.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Label positions after moving a path, shape, or related element</h3><p>After moving a path, shape, or related element, default label positions are retained if it is a semantically logical decision.</p><p>For a nicer representation of labels in a diagram, the label positions are reset to default automatically if:</p><ol><li>Symbol properties are edited. When symbol properties are edited by adding or removing a label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.</li><li>Path, path end, or port properties are edited. When path, path end, or port data are edited by adding or removing the label text box in the diagram pane, label positions are reset.</li><li>Path, shape, or related element are moved.</li></ol><p>To check the label deviation from its default position</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a label and begin to move it. The dashed line shows the deviation from the default position. does not consider any labels, does not increase the shape size.</li><li><strong>Partial</strong> - considers only the center path labels, does not increase shape sizes by labels.</li><li><strong>Full</strong> - considers all labels, creates additional space for them and increases the shape sizes.</li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Layout</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141122 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00668: Label orientation

- page_id: `189141122`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141122/Label+orientation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:53.879+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Layout
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Label Orientation symbol property

1409097048

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Label Orientation symbol property

1409097051

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Label Orientation symbol property

1409097050

You can optimize diagram layout by changing the orientation of symbol labels. Rotate labels horizontally or vertically, or select the automatic label orientation option, which adjusts labels according to the position of shapes or paths.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This sample diagrams illustrate how changing label orientation can optimize diagram layout.

To change the orientation of independent labels

1. In an open diagram, select one or several labels that you want to rotate.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] to change the label orientation from horizontal to vertical or vice versa.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click the arrow next to [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] and from the menu select the desired label orientation option: Horizontal , Vertical , or Automatic .

The labels are rotated to the selected position. If you select the **Automatic** label orientation option, labels are rotated according to the position of shapes or paths they are on.

To change the orientation of labels on a shape or path

1. In an open diagram, select the path or shape on which you want to rotate labels.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] to change the label orientation from horizontal to vertical or vice versa.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click the arrow next to [ATTACHMENT filename='rotate_label.png'] and from the menu select the desired label orientation option: Horizontal , Vertical , or Automatic .

The labels on the shape or path are rotated to the selected position. If you select the **Automatic** label orientation option, labels are rotated according to the position of the shape or path.

#### NOTE: Label Orientation symbol property

Label Orientation symbol property

When you change the orientation of individual labels or labels displayed on a shape or path, the value of the **Label Orientation** symbol property of the related shapes or paths changes as well. To open the **Symbol Properties** dialog, right-click a symbol in a diagram and select **Symbol Properties**.

##### Default label orientation

By default, all labels on shapes and paths are displayed horizontally. You can change the default label orientation for a project as explained below.

To change the default label orientation

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Project .
2. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, select Symbol styles > Default > Shapes project option group.
3. On the right side of the dialog, click the property value box of the Label Orientation property and select one of the following values:
  - Horizontal - to display the labels of newly created shapes horizontally.
  - Vertical - to display the labels of newly created shapes vertically.
  - Automatic - to display the labels of newly created shapes according to their position in a diagram.
4. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, select Symbol styles > Default > Paths project option group.
5. On the right side of the dialog, click the property value box of the Label Orientation property and select one of the following values:
  - Horizontal - to display the labels of newly created paths horizontally.
  - Vertical - to display the labels of newly created paths vertically.
  - Automatic - to display the labels of newly created paths according to their position in a diagram.
6. Click OK on the bottom of the Project Options dialog.

The labels of newly created symbols in the diagrams of a project will be displayed according to your selected property values.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139713 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00670: Launchers

- page_id: `189139713`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139713/Launchers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:19.638+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

221276213

INLINE

221276216

INLINE

221276215

INLINE

Launchers show which programs will be used for opening different file types in the application. Add new, edit, or remove launchers and specify their properties (file extension, program path or name, program arguments).

To open the **Launcher Properties** dialog

1. From the **Options** menu, choose **Environment**.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment Options' space=''] dialog, from the options group list select **Launchers**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To specify properties of the default launchers

1. To edit the Default launcher, click the**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**button next to the **Default Launcher** or**Default Browser** text boxes.
2. The Launcher Properties dialog appears.
3. You can change Launcher`s executable file and arguments.

To create the defined launcher

1. Click the Add button, found below the Defined Launchers list.
2. The Launcher Properties dialog appears.
3. Specify the file extension, which will be opened with the specified launcher.
4. Type the name of the launcher in the text box, or browse to the location of the launcher by clickingthe [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
5. Specify external launcher arguments.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Properties are described in the following table.

| Property Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Extensions | Type the extensions of the files which will be opened with the specified launcher. You can define more than one extension. Separate them using commas (i.e., html, xls, doc, cpp, h). |
| Launcher | An executable file with which the extension file will be opened. |
| Arguments | Arguments are required by the external launcher, so the modeling tool provides the ability to pass some common arguments to the launcher:$f - a file name$md_dir - the modeling tool installation directory$prj_dir - the project directory (if the project is saved)$r - a row (used when opening Code Engineering object source code file with "Edit Source" action)$c - a column (used when opening Code Engineering object source code file with "Edit Source" action). |

221276212

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']

## PAGE 00671: Layout

- page_id: `189141118`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141118/Layout
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:52.997+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

903811718

903811721

903811720

The diagram layout engine is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

It is easy to manage simple or complex diagrams using automated layout features that optimize diagram layout.

Symbols are automatically laid out on diagrams when displaying related elements, creating diagrams by using wizards, and creating related diagrams.

You can layout diagrams quickly by using the Quick Diagram layout. The quick layout is a recommended type of layout, and it is specific for each diagram.

The layout mechanism is built on various layout tools. There are 2 groups of layout tools: general layout tools and specific diagram layout tools. These are the general layout tools:

- Orthogonal Layout
- Hierarchic Layout
- Organic Layout
- Tree Layout
- Circular Layout
- Oblique Path Router
- Rectilinear Path Router

These layout tools are provided by yFiles layout tool component. You can arrange each diagram (except the Sequence diagram) by using any of the following general layout tools:

- Class Diagram Layout
- Composite Structure Diagram Layout
- Activity Diagram Layout
- State Machine Diagram Layout
- Business Process Diagram Layout

To layout diagrams by using the quick diagram layout, do one of the following

- Press Ctrl+Q.
- On the diagram toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diagram_layout.gif'] Quick Diagram Layout button.
- On the Main menu, click Layout > Quick Diagram Layout .

To layout diagrams by using layout tools, do one of the following

- On the Main menu, click Layout and select one of the layout tools.
- On the diagram toolbar, click the small arrow next to the [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diagram_layout.gif'] Quick Diagram Layout button, and select one of the layout tools.
- Select the element symbols you want to layout, right-click any of them, and in the shortcut menu, select Layout , then select one of the layout options.

To change the diagram layout options

1. On the Main menu, click Layout > Layout Options .
2. In the Diagram Layout Options dialog, specify the layout options.
3. Click OK .

903811717

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141158 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00672: Layout template creation mode

- page_id: `189141158`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141158/Layout+template+creation+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:57.986+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Layout templates > Creating layout template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating layout templates from Composite Structure Diagrams, you can select whether layout templates are created as Class Diagrams (default option) or Composite Structure Diagrams. To do this, specify layout template creation mode as described below.

To specify layout template creation mode

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, select Options > Project .
2. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, expand the General node and select the General option group.
3. Click the specification cell of the Layout Template Creation Mode option on the left side of the dialog and select one of the following values as shown below:
  - Usage - if you want to create layout templates as Composite Structure Diagrams.
  - Definition - if you want to create layout templates as Class Diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying layout template creation mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before creating layout templates from Composite Structure Diagrams, you can select whether layout templates are created as Class Diagrams (default option) or Composite Structure Diagrams. To do this, specify layout template creation mode as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify layout template creation mode</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> node and select the <strong>General</strong> option group.</li><li>Click the specification cell of the <strong>Layout Template Creation Mode</strong> option on the left side of the dialog and select one of the following values as shown below:<br /><ul><li><strong>Usage</strong> - if you want to create layout templates as Composite Structure Diagrams.</li><li><strong>Definition</strong> - if you want to create layout templates as Class Diagrams.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="layout_template_creation_mode.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Specifying layout template creation mode.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141133 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00673: Layout templates

- page_id: `189141133`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141133/Layout+templates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:21.816+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Important

1350756844

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Important

1350756847

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Important

1350756846

#### EXCERPT: Important

INLINE

The layout templates functionality, which is driven by <<layoutTemplate>> stereotype, assists you in creating well-formed diagrams. It allows you to define Class appearance once, and reuse it later when creating Parts typed by that Class and Ports in different diagrams. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space=''] and their Ports are represented identically as part's type [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] whose layout and style is defined in the layout template diagram. Often, Port position reflects its physical location in the system, so it is beneficial to maintain the same appearance of Parts, layout, and style of Ports throughout the whole model. In the figure below, the appearance of the *Modem Card* Block is the same in two different diagrams, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'].

700500

#### INFO: Additional information

Additional information

You can reuse layout templates in other projects by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting packages to new projects' space='']

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Usage of a layout template. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

You can create a layout template in the Class Diagram or derive it from an existing Composite Structure Diagram as described below.

To define a layout template in the Class Diagram and apply it to other diagrams:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining layout template' space='']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting diagram as layout template' space='']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying layout template' space='']

To derive a layout template for the Composite Structure Diagram and apply it to other diagrams:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout template creation mode' space='']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating layout template' space='']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying layout template' space='']

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Only one Class shape with its Ports can be defined in one layout template diagram.

Read the following pages to learn how to manage layout templates:

- Stop using Class diagram as a layout template
- Removing applied layout template from shape
- Multiple layout template
- Setting layout template as default
- Navigation to layout templates
- Using layout templates with diagram aspects

1350756842

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram aspects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting packages to new projects' space='']

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is **Modem Cable** which you can download *[ATTACHMENT filename='Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip'].*

## PAGE 00675: Legends

- page_id: `189140941`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140941/Legends
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:23.344+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tips for working with Legends

37096963

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tips for working with Legends

37096965

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tips for working with Legends

37096964

A Legend is a model element that allows you to define the styles of diagram symbols and table rows. It helps you to visually group model elements according to specified criteria. Use Legends to increase the readability of the diagram and table contents by highlighting important information, e.g., specific element property values or statuses. In addition, the styles applied to model elements are dynamically updated when you modify your model, so all changes are instantly visible in diagrams and tables where Legends are used.

800450

#### TIP: Tips for working with Legends

Tips for working with Legends

- You can apply multiple Legend Items to a single model element.
- You can reuse Legends in different diagrams and tables of a model, as well as in different models.

For more information about working with Legends, see the following pages:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *Risk* and *Status* legends used in a Requirement Diagram.

37096962

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141528 space=MD2024xR2 version=6 -->
## PAGE 00676: Lifeline

- page_id: `189141528`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141528/Lifeline
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2024-10-16T08:53:30.943+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

A lifeline represents an individual participant in the Interaction. The lifeline represents only one interacting entity, symbolized by a rectangle.

The following steps show you how to work with a lifeline:

##### Assigning a type to a lifeline

To assign a type (classifier) to a lifeline, do one of the following:

- In the lifeline Specification window, in the Type property value, click the ... button, and in the open dialog, select the type. When you are finished, click OK .
- On the diagram, from the lifeline shortcut menu, select Type . In the open list, select the type.
- On the diagram, click a lifeline, and then, on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='type_command.png'] . In the open list, select the type.
- On the diagram, click a lifeline, and then press Ctrl+T. In the open list, select the type.
- On the diagram, select a lifeline, and in the name area, type the “:” and the name of the type. Press Enter or click anywhere on the diagram. The type is created and assigned. Type the “:”, press Ctrl+Space, or Ctrl+Backspace. In the open list, select the type.
- In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the lifeline shape.

##### Showing/hiding a base classifier

To hide/show a base classifier

- On the diagram, right-click the lifeline. From the shortcut menu, select the Show Classifier check box.

##### Displaying lifelines

You can display lifelines on the Sequence or Communication diagram in the following ways:

- By manually dragging lifeline elements from the Containment tree.
- By automatically using the Display Lifelines dialog.

There are two situations in which you can use the **Display Lifelines** dialog to automatically display lifelines:

- .
- .

newTo display lifelines when creating a new diagram

1. When creating a new Sequence or Communication diagram for an existing classifier, the Display Lifelines dialog opens automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='Display Lifelines.png']
2. In the opened Display Lifelines dialog, select the lifelines you want to display, and click OK . Selected lifelines are displayed on the diagram pane.

modifyTo display lifelines in a preexisting diagram

1. Select the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] .
2. Open the Display Lifelines dialog by performing one of the following steps:
  - From the diagram shortcut menu, select Display > Display Lifelines . [ATTACHMENT filename='Shortcut menu_ Display Lifelines..png']
  - On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] , click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Display Lifelines .
3. In the dialog, select the lifelines you want to display, and click OK . Selected lifelines are displayed on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='displayed_lifelines_in_sequence_diagram.png']

##### Deeply nested parts

The “dot notation” is available for the lifelines. Now you can send messages directly to the deeply nested parts.

To display a deeply nested part

1. Create a Sequence diagram. The **Display Lifelines** dialog opens. You can open the **Display Lifelines** dialog in the existing diagram. On the diagram pane, click the right mouse button and from the shortcut menu, select **Related Elements** > **Display Lifelines**.
2. Select a nested part you want to display. Make sure that the nesting part is not selected, otherwise it will be displayed instead of the nested part.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Activation bars on lifelines

By default, when there is a message sent between lifelines in a sequence diagram, the lifelines are displayed with activation bars on them. However, you can hide the activation bars from the diagram. 
 
To hide activation bars from lifelines in a sequence diagram

1. Right-click on the diagram pane. From the shortcut menu, select Diagram properties .
2. The Diagram properties of Sequence Diagram dialog opens. Deselect Show Activations check box, setting the value from true to false . [ATTACHMENT filename='Show Activations_Dialog.png']

**Sample model**

The models used in the figures of this page is the **Communication diagram** and **Sequence diagram** sample models.

To open these samples

- Find in modeling tool: - <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\communication diagram.mdzip. 
 - <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\sequence diagram.mdzip.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

However, you can hide the activation bars from the diagram.<br /><br />To hide activation bars from lifelines in a sequence diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click on the diagram pane. From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Diagram properties</strong>.</li><li>The <strong>Diagram properties of Sequence Diagram</strong> dialog opens. Deselect <strong>Show Activations</strong> check box, setting the value from <strong>true</strong> to <strong>false</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show Activations_Dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The models used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Communication diagram</strong> and <strong>Sequence diagram</strong> sample models.</p><p>To open these samples</p><hr /><ul><li>Find in modeling tool:<br />   - <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\communication diagram.mdzip.<br />   - &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\sequence diagram.mdzip.</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Communication diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144565 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00677: Limiting image size

- page_id: `189144565`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144565/Limiting+image+size
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:58.659+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

These functions are for limiting image size by scaling an image when its size overlaps the paper margin.

**$image.limitImageSize(image)** 
 Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page (like the Fit image to paper option).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | A resized image. |

For example:

```text

```

**$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidth, maximumHeight)**

Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified **maximumWidth** and **maximumHeight** in terms of measurements (px, in, cm, mm, pt).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element. |
|  | maximumWidth | java.lang.String | The maximum width of the resized image in terms of measurements. |
|  | maximumHeight | java.lang.String | The maximum height of the resized image in terms of measurements. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | A resized image. |

For example:

```text

```

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.

******$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidthPixel, maximumHeightPixel)** 
 Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified **maximumWidthPixel** and **maximumHeightPixel** in terms of pixels.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element. |
|  | maximumWidthPixel | int | The maximum width of the resized image in terms of pixels. |
|  | maximumHeightPixel | int | The maximum height of the resized image in terms of pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | A resized image. |

For example:

```text

```

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>These functions are for limiting image size by scaling an image when its size overlaps the paper margin.</p><p><br /> <strong>$image.limitImageSize(image)</strong><br /> Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page (like the Fit image to paper option).</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>image</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object for the element.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>A resized image.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6b15a92-9c83-4056-8168-78192a71a472"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidth, maximumHeight)</strong></p><p>Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified <strong>maximumWidth</strong> and <strong>maximumHeight</strong> in terms of measurements (px, in, cm, mm, pt).</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>image</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object for the element.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumWidth</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The maximum width of the resized image in terms of measurements.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumHeight</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The maximum height of the resized image in terms of measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>A resized image.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e69527f-1cbb-461f-80ec-d23fb3f8884d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, "5in", "8in")
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, "-1", "8in")
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, "5in", "-1")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5378cabf-6a1d-4811-b8d1-bcbbe5cc3e04"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong><strong>$image.limitImageSize(image, maximumWidthPixel, maximumHeightPixel)</strong><br /> Return an image that is scaled to fit the paper page but not bigger than the specified <strong>maximumWidthPixel</strong> and <strong>maximumHeightPixel</strong> in terms of pixels.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>image</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object for the element.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumWidthPixel</td><td>int</td><td>The maximum width of the resized image in terms of pixels.</td></tr><tr><td> </td><td>maximumHeightPixel</td><td>int</td><td>The maximum height of the resized image in terms of pixels.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>A resized image.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1db4c789-1956-4292-8f14-d5c9fe867096"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, 480, 680)
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, -1, 680)
#end

#foreach($d in $Diagram)
$image.limitImageSize($d.image, 480, -1)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="043cfae2-7bd4-41f7-a75f-d84671a615a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not want to specify the value in the parameter, use -1 instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141536 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00678: Link

- page_id: `189141536`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141536/Link
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:20.455+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An instance of an association is called a Link. A Link is a tuple with one value for each end of the association, where each value is an instance of the type of the end. Links can be represented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space=''].

With our modeling tool, you can display on diagrams the following:

- Links created between Instance specifications (the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space=''] command on the Instance specification [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH185'] ).
- Instance specifications at the other end of a Link (the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''] command on the Instance specification shortcut menu).

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Links can be represented in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Relation Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Relationship Map]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143791 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00679: Linking an embedded project with relevant server project

- page_id: `189143791`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143791/Linking+an+embedded+project+with+relevant+server+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:24.103+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To link an embedded project with relevant server project, you must have an **Administer Resources** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud185'].

Linking replaces the privately embedded project (also referred to as the local project) with a relevant server project.

An embedded project appears in your project after you:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space=''] in your project.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space=''] to Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) together with the main project.

The embedded project can be:

- Used (referenced) in the main project
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing the content of a used project into your project' space='']Importedinto the main project
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stop using the project in your project' space='']

However, because it is not a server project, it has no versions and cannot be updated.

If a relevant project is stored on the server (it may be added to the server individually, separately from the project using it), you can easily link the embedded project with it.

To link an embedded project with a relevant server project

1. In the Model Browser, under Project Usages , right-click the embedded project.
2. Select Project Usages > Project Usage Options . The Project Usages dialog opens. The embedded project is already selected.
3. Click the Link with Server Project button (see the following figure). The Use Server Project wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_usages_link_with_server_project.png']
4. The same steps of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space=''] are outlined below.

The following figure highlights the differences between displaying an unlinked embedded project and one linked with the server project (converted to the used project).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Notation differences in Model Browser for embedded and used projects.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

as the local project) with a relevant server project.</p><p>An embedded project appears in your project after you:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Start using a project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Start using it]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in your project.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="usages"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Refuse to add a used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) together with the main project.</li></ul><p>The embedded project can be:</p><ul><li>Used (referenced) in the main project</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing the content of a used project into your project" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Imported </span>into the main project</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stop using the project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Stopped being used]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>However, because it is not a server project, it has no versions and cannot be updated.</p><p>If a relevant project is stored on the server (it may be added to the server individually, separately from the project using it), you can easily link the embedded project with it.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><p>To link an embedded project with a relevant server project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, under <strong>Project Usages</strong>, right-click the embedded project.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt;<strong> Project Usage Options</strong>. The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens. The embedded project is already selected.</li><li>Click the <strong>Link with Server Project</strong> button (see the following figure). The <strong>Use Server Project</strong> wizard opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_usages_link_with_server_project.png" /></ac:image></li><li>The same steps of<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Start using a project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ using a project in the server project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are outlined below.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>The following figure highlights the differences between displaying an unlinked embedded project and one linked with the server project (converted to the used project).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Differences in displaying embeded and used projects in the model" ac:alt="Differences in displaying embeded and used projects in the model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="representation_of_usages.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> Notation differences in Model Browser for embedded and used projects.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00680: Linking report templates to the modeling tool

- page_id: `189145067`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`

- labels: []

1358373575

1358373574

By now you should know how to write a basic template that makes a simple reference to an element. But how will you know if the element is referenced or not? This section shows you how elements are referenced and how to call them.

1358373573

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144529 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00681: List tool

- page_id: `189144529`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144529/List+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:53.156+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050360272

2050360275

2050360269

**On this page**

43

2050360274

$list is a list tool used to work with lists and arrays in a template. It provides a method to get and manage specified elements. You can also use it as a means to perform some actions on a list or array such as:

- Check if it is empty.
- Check if it contains certain elements.

For example:

| $primes$list.size($primes)$list.get($primes, 2)$list.set($primes, 2, 1)$list.get($primes, 2)For example:$list.isEmpty($primes)$list.contains($primes, 7) | -> new int[] {2, 3, 5, 7}-> 4-> 5-> (primes [2] becomes 1)-> 1-> false-> true |
| --- | --- |

###### **$list.contains(list, element)**

Check if a list or array contains certain elements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list or array instance. |
| element | - | An element instance. |  |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if a list or array contains certain elements. Otherwise, return false. |

###### **$list.get(list, index)**

Return a specified element of a list or array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list or array instance. |
| index | - | An index of an object in a list. |  |
| Return | - | object | Return an object in a list. |

###### **$list.isArray(object)**

Check if an object is an array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | object | - | An object |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if the object is an array. Otherwise, returns false. |

###### **$list.isEmpty(list)**

Check if a list or array is empty.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list of objects |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if the list or array is empty. Otherwise, returns false. |

###### **$list.isList(object)**

Check if an object is a list.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | object | - | An object |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true, if the object is a List. Otherwise, return false. |

###### **$list.set(list, index, value)**

Set a specified element of a List/array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list of objects. |
| index | - | An index of an object in the list. |  |
| value | - | An object to be set to the list. |  |
| Return | - | object | An object at the previously-specified position with the set value. |

###### **$list.size(list)**

Return the size of a List or array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list of objects. |
| Return | - | Integer | Return the size of the list as an Integer. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0bd7e9f4-e8bf-4110-b24f-04741c75b746"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050360272</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb1a00f6-5476-4e55-ab42-7400adc744cb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050360275</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0f4a33e-593e-42d2-a69e-e17f1f1f95cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050360269</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54bbe164-6778-482a-83b7-cb7beae519e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050360274</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>$list is a list tool used to work with lists and arrays in a template. It provides a method to get and manage specified elements. You can also use it as a means to perform some actions on a list or array such as:</p><ul><li>Check if it is empty.</li><li>Check if it contains certain elements.</li></ul><p>For example:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td>$primes<br />$list.size($primes)<br />$list.get($primes, 2)<br />$list.set($primes, 2, 1)<br />$list.get($primes, 2)For example:<br />$list.isEmpty($primes)<br />$list.contains($primes, 7)  </td><td><p><span>-&gt; new int[] {2, 3, 5, 7}<br /></span>-&gt; 4<br />-&gt; 5<br />-&gt; (primes [2] becomes 1)<br />-&gt; 1<br />-&gt; false<br />-&gt; true </p></td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$list.contains(list, element)</strong></h4><p>Check if a list or array contains certain elements.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">Parameter(s)  </td><td>list </td><td>- </td><td>A list or array <br />instance.</td></tr><tr><td><span>element</span> </td><td>- </td><td>An element <br />instance. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>boolean </td><td>Return true if a <br />list or array contains <br />certain elements. <br />Otherwise, return false.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$list.get(list, index)</strong></h4><p>Return a specified element of a list or array.</p><p><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">Parameter(s) </td><td>list </td><td>- </td><td> A list or array <br />instance.</td></tr><tr><td>index</td><td>- </td><td>An index of an <br />object in a list.</td></tr><tr><td>Return </td><td>-</td><td>object</td><td>Return an object <br />in a list.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$list.isArray(object)</strong></h4><p>Check if an object is an array.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td> Parameter(s)</td><td>object </td><td>- </td><td> An object</td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>boolean </td><td>Return true if <br />the object is an <br />array. Otherwise, <br />returns false.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$list.isEmpty(list)</strong></h4><p>Check if a list or array is empty.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td> Parameter(s)</td><td> list</td><td>- </td><td>A list of <br />objects </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td>- </td><td>boolean </td><td>Return true if <br />the list or array <br />is empty. Otherwise, <br />returns false. </td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$list.isList(object)</strong></h4><p>Check if an object is a list.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td> object</td><td>- </td><td>An object</td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td>- </td><td>boolean </td><td>Return true, <br />if the object <br />is a List. Otherwise, <br />return false.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$list.set(list, index, value)</strong></h4><p>Set a specified element of a List/array.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3">Parameter(s)   </td><td> list </td><td>- </td><td> A list of <br />objects.</td></tr><tr><td> index</td><td>-</td><td>An index of <br />an object in <br />the list. </td></tr><tr><td> value</td><td>- </td><td>An object to be <br />set to the list. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>object </td><td>An object at the <br />previously-specified <br />position with the set <br />value.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$list.size(list)</strong></h4><p>Return the size of a List or array.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>list </td><td>- </td><td>A list of <br />objects.</td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>Integer </td><td>Return the size <br />of the list as <br />an Integer. </td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139624 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00682: Lock View tab

- page_id: `189139624`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139624/Lock+View+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:13.977+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425813371

1425813374

1425813373

INLINE

The Lock View tab is displayed only when working with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaborative modeling' space=''].

The Lock View tab is used to display what was locked in the project (locked elements, project structure, symbol styles) and by which users. Next to the locked element, you can see the name of the user who has locked it. Using the **Filter by user**list in the Lock View tab toolbar, you can see the elements, locked by the particular user.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the Lock View tab, do one of the following

- At the top of the Model Browser, click the Lock View tab.
- If the Containment tab is hidden, from the Window menu, select Lock View .

The Locked Elements tab consists of the following two components:

- Lock View tab toolbar
- Lock View tree

##### Lock View tab toolbar

The Lock View tab toolbar contains the following:

- Update Lock Information button
- Filter by user list
- Collapse All button
- Collapse Selected Recursively button

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Using the **Filter by user** list you can select to display only the elements locked by the particular user.

##### Working with model elements in the Lock View tree

You can work with elements in the Lock View tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Lock | Locks or unlocks the particular element for edit. |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Select in Inheritance Tree | Opens the Inheritance tab and selects the current element in the Inheritance tree. |

1425813370

**Related pages**

- Model Browser
