<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141709 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00699: Managing contents of smart package

- page_id: `189141709`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141709/Managing+contents+of+smart+package
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:25.849+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Smart Package
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

935555568

935555571

935555570

The following sections outline how to manage the smart package's contents once it is created. 
5

##### Including elements into a smart package

An element can be included in the smart package in one of two ways:

- Manually - the user selects to include the particular element. Manually included elements constitute the static contents.
- Automatically - the element meets the set of criteria defined by the user. Automatically included elements constitute the dynamic contents.

The membership in the smart package is not the UML ownership association. The owner of the element will not change after the element is included in the smart package.

To include an element into a smart package manually

Do either:

- In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the smart package. 
See the element included in the contents of the smart package. The element becomes a value of the **Additional Elements**property as well. You can include more than one element at once.To include *adjacent*elements, select a single element, and then hold down the Shift key while you click any other elements you want to add.To include *nonadjacent*elements, select a single element, and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click any other elements you want to add.

- Open the Specification window of the smart package and click the cell of the Additional Elements property value. Then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] and in the open dialog, select one or more elements. Click OK when you are done. See the element(s) included in the contents of the smart package.

To include elements into a smart package automatically

1. Open the Specification window of the smart package.
2. Under the Content category, click the cell of the Query property value.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . The Query dialog opens.
4. In the dialog, define a set of criteria (query) for gathering the elements into the contents of the smart package. For instructions about defining criteria in the **Query**dialog, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='']. You can select to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting criteria' space=''] (logical relationships calculated from inherited or inner elements of the model element) to provide additional information.
5. Close the Specification window. See the contents of the smart package updated with elements that meet the defined set of criteria. If the contents of the smart package are not updated, right-click the smart package and select **Refresh Contents** in the shortcut menu. If the smart package contains manually included elements (values of the **Additional Elements** property), they are joined with the automatically included elements, skipping duplicates.If the smart package has excluded elements (values of the **Excluded Elements** property), they do not appear in the dynamic contents of the smart package.

##### Removing elements from a smart package

To remove an element from a smart package

Do either:

- In the Containment tree, under the smart package, right-click the element. From the shortcut menu, select **Delete from Contents**or press**Delete**. You can exclude more than one element at once.To exclude *adjacent*elements, select a single element, and hold down the Shift key while you click other elements you want to exclude.To exclude *nonadjacent*elements, select a single element, and hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements that you want to exclude.

- Open the Specification window of the smart package and click the cell of the Excluded Elements property value. Then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] and in the open dialog, select one or more elements. Click OK when you are done.
- Open the Specification window of the smart package and click the cell of the **Additional Elements**property value. Then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and in the open dialog, unselect one or more elements. Click **OK**when you are done. This is only valid for manually added elements. See the element removed from the contents of the smart package, though it is not removed from the model. You can still see the element displayed under the owner of the element in the Containment tree.

##### Freezing the contents of a smart package

If you do not want the contents of the Smart Package to be updated automatically, you can suspend the calculation of the Smart Package contents. As a result, elements gathered according to a set of criteria specified in the **Query** dialog are no longer automatically added to the Smart Package contents. You can undo this at any time by changing the **Frozen** state.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of the Smart Package with the suspended contents.

To freeze the contents of a Smart Package

- In the Containment tree, right-click the Smart Package with the dynamic contents, and from the shortcut menu, select **Frozen**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The contents of the smart package will no longer be automatically updated after the relevant changes are made in the model.

You can update the contents of the Smart Package on demand without the need to change the **Frozen** state to disabled by performing the **Refresh Contents** action.

To refresh the contents of a Smart Package on demand

- In the Containment tree, right-click the Smart Package with the dynamic contents, and from the shortcut menu, select Refresh Contents. The contents of the Smart Package is successfully refreshed. [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_smart_package_content_frozen_enabled.png']

##### Making a snapshot of the smart package

You can create a copy of the smart package with the static contents, that is, a snapshot.

To make a snapshot of a smart package

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the smart package with dynamic contents, and from the shortcut menu, select Snapshot Contents . The element Selection dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select the owner of the new smart package and click OK . See the new smart package created under the selected owner in the Containment tree with the name Snapshot of <smart package name> , for example, Snapshot of My Bookmarks . The contents of the snapshot equal the contents of the smart package, but the list of elements is static.

935555565

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52e78da9-d73e-4229-8297-f58cd9568845"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">935555568</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="881854cd-ce94-40e2-8806-df3bb17a733b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">935555571</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="654e8295-ef75-4b45-9d39-3d5d73a2040b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">935555570</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><div class="WordSection1"><p>The following sections outline how to manage the smart package's contents once it is created. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7bf5705a-7429-40b4-8414-d9e8298c8b58"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Including elements into a smart package</h3><p>An element can be included in the smart package in one of two ways:</p><ul><li><em>Manually - </em>the user selects to include the particular element. Manually included elements constitute the static contents.</li><li><em>Automatically - </em>the element meets the set of criteria defined by the user. Automatically included elements constitute the dynamic contents.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="229d6dc6-1914-47e1-8f3f-54e625159d55"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The membership in the smart package is not the UML ownership association. The owner of the element will not change after the element is included in the smart package.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>To include an element into a smart package manually</p><hr /><p>Do either:</p><ul><li><p>In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the smart package.<br />See the element included in the contents of the smart package. The element becomes a value of the <strong>Additional Elements </strong>property as well.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd149c1e-1d24-436a-a736-e5c4b9317d74"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can include more than one element at once.</p><ul><li>To include <em>adjacent </em>elements, select a single element, and then hold down the Shift key while you click any other elements you want to add.</li><li>To include <em>nonadjacent </em>elements, select a single element, and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click any other elements you want to add.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ul><li>Open the Specification window of the smart package and click the cell of the <strong>Additional Elements </strong>property value. Then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png" /></ac:image>and in the open dialog, select one or more elements. Click <strong>OK </strong>when you are done. See the element(s) included in the contents of the smart package.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To include elements into a smart package automatically</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of the smart package.</li><li>Under the <strong>Content </strong>category, click the cell of the <strong>Query </strong>property value.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>Query </strong>dialog opens.</li><li><p>In the dialog, define a set of criteria (query) for gathering the elements into the contents of the smart package.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="079a0122-00f3-4074-bfeb-d4183135a010"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For instructions about defining criteria in the <strong>Query </strong>dialog, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specifying Criteria for Querying Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65269177-b29f-4fa9-9acb-5c7462177886"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select to<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting criteria" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ show implied relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (logical relationships calculated from inherited or inner elements of the model element) to provide additional information.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Close the Specification window. See the contents of the smart package updated with elements that meet the defined set of criteria.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0a8a719-1b60-4dfa-9faf-7453c2a57ca5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the contents of the smart package are not updated, right-click the smart package and select <strong>Refresh Contents</strong> in the shortcut menu<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="90afe3e6-1793-4fb2-906d-fe8b28c9216f">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba6bef7f-300f-4f61-a3df-4857d190b03b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the smart package contains manually included elements (values of the <strong>Additional Elements</strong> property), they are joined with the automatically included elements, skipping duplicates.</li><li>If the smart package has excluded elements (values of the <strong>Excluded Elements</strong> property), they do not appear in the dynamic contents of the smart package.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection3"><h3>Removing elements from a smart package</h3><p style="text-align: left;">To remove an element from a smart package</p><hr /><p>Do either:</p><ul><li><p>In the Containment tree, under the smart package, right-click the element. From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Delete from Contents </strong>or press<strong> Delete</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b5617da-2b10-4d77-9d40-052042a0c99f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can exclude more than one element at once.</p><ul><li>To exclude <em>adjacent </em>elements, select a single element, and hold down the Shift key while you click other elements you want to exclude.</li><li>To exclude <em>nonadjacent </em>elements, select a single element, and hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements that you want to exclude.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ul><li>Open the Specification window of the smart package and click the cell of the <strong>Excluded Elements </strong>property value. Then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png" /></ac:image> and in the open dialog, select one or more elements. Click <strong>OK </strong>when you are done.</li><li><p>Open the Specification window of the smart package and click the cell of the <strong>Additional Elements </strong>property value. Then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png" /></ac:image> and in the open dialog, unselect one or more elements. Click <strong>OK </strong>when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="925d7eec-7e9e-40c0-9357-f7c77d80f465"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This is only valid for manually added elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>See the element removed from the contents of the smart package, though it is not removed from the model. You can still see the element displayed under the owner of the element in the Containment tree.</p></li></ul><h3>Freezing the contents of a smart package</h3><p>If you do not want the contents of the Smart Package to be updated automatically, you can suspend the calculation of the Smart Package contents. As a result, elements gathered according to a set of criteria specified in the <strong>Query</strong> dialog are no longer automatically added to the Smart Package contents. You can undo this at any time by changing the <strong>Frozen</strong> state. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="enabling_frozen_state.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Specification window of the Smart Package with the suspended contents.</h6><p><br /></p><p>To freeze the contents of a Smart Package</p><hr /><ul><li><p>In the Containment tree, right-click the Smart Package with the dynamic contents, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Frozen</strong>.<br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7c5f77d7-4fe6-40b4-88bd-395ed609e410"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="frozen_enabled.png" /></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li></ul><div><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97687380-2952-4843-abfe-52d2ccd692ec"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>The contents of the smart package will no longer be automatically updated after the relevant changes are made in the model.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can update the contents of the Smart Package on demand without the need to change the <strong>Frozen</strong> state to disabled by performing the <strong>Refresh Contents</strong> action. </p><p><br /></p><p>To refresh the contents of a Smart Package on demand</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the Smart Package with the dynamic contents, and from the shortcut menu, select Refresh Contents.<br />The contents of the Smart Package is successfully refreshed.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_smart_package_content_frozen_enabled.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></div></div><h3>Making a snapshot of the smart package</h3><p>You can create a copy of the smart package with the static contents, that is, a snapshot.</p><p><br /></p><p>To make a snapshot of a smart package</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the smart package with dynamic contents, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Snapshot Contents</strong>. The element Selection dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select the owner of the new smart package and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br />See the new smart package created under the selected owner in the Containment tree with the name <em>Snapshot of &lt;smart package name&gt;</em>, for example, <em>Snapshot of My Bookmarks</em>. The contents of the snapshot equal the contents of the smart package, but the list of elements is static.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1753ae3f-7edf-483b-bbee-a8c48f309ca6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">935555565</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Profiles" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140854 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00700: Managing Excel and CSV file

- page_id: `189140854`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140854/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:14.023+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Sync with Excel or CSV files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

425937017

425937023

425937014

**On this page**

****

425937022

##### Using UML properties upon importUsing UML properties upon import

If you want to use UML properties when importing a file, you can do by updating the system properties file.

To use UML properties upon import

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -DUSE_UML_TYPES_IN_EXCELSYNC=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import

By default, the import of the Excel sheet's data is stopped if an empty row or column is encountered. If your Excel sheet contains purposefully created empty rows or columns and you do not want the reading of the file to be stopped by them, you need to specify that in the tool. You can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. By indicating the number of consecutive empty rows or columns that should be skipped, the file's data is continued to be read after the empty rows/columns.

- If the options for skipping empty rows/columns are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different numeric values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.
- The empty rows/columns themselves cannot be imported from the file to the modeling tool's table; they can either be treated as the end of the file (default) or skipped (see procedures below).

To skip empty rows/columns via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the numberEmptyRowsSkip tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty rows to be skipped.
5. Select the numberEmptyColumnsSkip tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
6. On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty columns to be skipped.
7. Click **Close**.

To skip empty rows/columns via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the **JAVA_ARGS** line, add the following values, and replace *POSITIVE_NUMBER* with a numeric value of consecutive empty rows/columns to be skipped:
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### To export by IDMapping elements by ID upon export

By default, exporting table data to a synced file removes any previously existing content in the file. If you want to export table data to a synced file while keeping any existing data in the file, you can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. This way, elements are mapped by ID, and the new data is added to the file.

If mapping options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.

To map elements by ID via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the **exportByIdentificationProperty** tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, click the **Edit** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to set the tag value to *true*.
5. Click **Close**.

To map elements by ID via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -Dexport.table.by.identification.property=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### Color marking modified data upon export

If you want to color-mark exported table data based on its changes (added/updated/removed, same as in the modeling tool's table), you can do so either by updating the system properties file or appropriate table tags. This allows you to easily review data changes and make any necessary adjustments based on this information.

- If color marking options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.
- To apply color marking, must be enabled first.
- Any data deleted in the table is marked in red in the exported Excel file. If, upon the file's review, you deem this data truly unnecessary, make sure to remove it from the Excel file. Otherwise, this data will be imported back into the table upon Read From File action.

To color mark modified data via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the **markExportedRows** tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, click the **Edit** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to set the tag value to *true*.
5. Click **Close**.

To color mark modified data via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -Dexport.table.mark.exported.rows=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

As a result, the cells of the exported table are marked based on their changes in comparison to the data stored in the file prior to its export. The colors used for the marking and their meanings:

- Red - removed
- Blue - updated
- Green - added

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Opening linked Excel/CSV file with the table

To open a linked Excel and/or CSV file directly from a modeling tool table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] .
2. Select Excel/CSV File > Open File . The linked file is opened.

##### Removing the reference between the Excel/CSV file and the table

If you linked the Excel/CSV file with modeling tool table, the reference between them is created. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Synchronize with default mapping options' space='']>]]>

To remove it, follow the procedure below.

To remove a reference between a Excel/CSV file and a modeling tool table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] .
2. Select Excel/CSV File > Clear Selection . The reference between tables is removed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fe4a66e-c589-4f2c-8fb6-dc7431c4a05c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">425937017</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0a3b230-7fee-45d7-9997-129ac2ed4208"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">425937023</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63fbf3a2-2cce-4392-ab1c-f0a9f3f6c0e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">425937014</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c5570d8-0dd9-4027-b734-63dec45c7636" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5a54dd7-fe8a-43c2-9d8b-bf12bfe35e46"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">425937022</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3ca1be4-4bfd-4ac4-a218-4d3e42aeac48"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Using UML properties upon import</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Using UML properties upon import</h3><p>If you want to use UML properties when importing a file, you can do by updating the system properties file.</p><p><br />To use UML properties upon import</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li>In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-DUSE_UML_TYPES_IN_EXCELSYNC=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li>Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3>Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import</h3><p>By default, the import of the Excel sheet's data is stopped if an empty row or column is encountered. If your Excel sheet contains purposefully created empty rows or columns and you do not want the reading of the file to be stopped by them, you need to specify that in the tool. You can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. By indicating the number of consecutive empty rows or columns that should be skipped, the file's data is continued to be read after the empty rows/columns.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b2d6e3a-8c64-4300-99b1-5479c4f00fe4"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the options for skipping empty rows/columns are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different numeric values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</li><li>The empty rows/columns themselves cannot be imported from the file to the modeling tool's table; they can either be treated as the end of the file (default) or skipped (see procedures below).</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To skip empty rows/columns via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li>In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>numberEmptyRowsSkip</strong><span> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li><span>On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty rows to be skipped.</span></li><li>Select the <strong>numberEmptyColumnsSkip</strong><span> tag of the «MapToDataSource»  stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li><span>On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty columns to be skipped.</span></li><li><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To skip empty rows/columns via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li><p>In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line, add the following values, and replace <span><em>POSITIVE_NUMBER</em> with a numeric value of consecutive empty rows/columns to be skipped:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8d1721b-4d65-424e-b438-1d455094bac5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-Dnumber.empty.rows.skip=POSITIVE_NUMBER
-Dnumber.empty.columns.skip=POSITIVE_NUMBER]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72ac8d2e-7190-4ed9-bbc8-ab71db7240b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">To export by ID</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Mapping elements by ID upon export</h3><p>By default, exporting table data to a synced file removes any previously existing content in the file. If you want to export table data to a synced file while keeping any existing data in the file, you can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. This way, elements are mapped by ID, and the new data is added to the file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="998aa366-976e-4d34-8afa-5cf0b8c1d789"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If mapping options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To map elements by ID via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li>In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li>Select the <span><strong>exportByIdentificationProperty</strong> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button. </span></li><li><span>On the right side of the window, click the <strong>Edit</strong> <ac:image ac:alt="edit_button.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/106627254/edit_button.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1644244174567&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> button to set the tag value to <em>true</em>.</span></li><li><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To map elements by ID via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li>In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-Dexport.table.by.identification.property=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li>Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3>Color marking modified data upon export</h3><p>If you want to color-mark exported table data based on its changes (added/updated/removed, same as in the modeling tool's table), you can do so either by updating the system properties file or appropriate table tags. This allows you to easily review data changes and make any necessary adjustments based on this information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a294723e-2a8e-428f-a27b-dd6198b28341"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If color marking options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</li><li>To apply color marking, <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="To export by ID"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ mapping elements by ID ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>must be enabled first.</li><li>Any data deleted in the table is marked in red in the exported Excel file. If, upon the file's review, you deem this data truly unnecessary, make sure to remove it from the Excel file. Otherwise, this data will be imported back into the table upon <strong>Read From File</strong> action. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To color mark modified data via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li>In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li>Select the <span><strong>markExportedRows</strong> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li><span>On the right side of the window, click the <strong>Edit</strong> <ac:image ac:alt="edit_button.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/106627254/edit_button.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1644244174567&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> button to set the tag value to <em>true</em>.</span></li><li><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To color mark modified data via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li>In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-Dexport.table.mark.exported.rows=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li>Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><p><br />As a result, the cells of the exported table are marked based on their changes in comparison to the data stored in the file prior to its export. The colors used for the marking and their meanings:</p><ul><li>Red - removed</li><li>Blue - updated</li><li>Green - added</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Color_marking_exported_data.png" /></ac:image></p><h3><span style="font-size: 16.0px;letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Opening linked Excel/CSV file with the table</span></h3><p>To open a linked Excel and/or CSV file directly from a modeling tool table</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Excel/CSV Sync" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>Select <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Open File</strong>.<br />The linked file is opened.</li></ol><h3>Removing the reference between the Excel/CSV file and the table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you linked the Excel/CSV file with modeling tool table, the reference between them is created. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Synchronize with default mapping options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Read step #3 to link Excel or CSV file with the table >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To remove it, follow the procedure below. </span></p><p>To remove a reference between a Excel/CSV file and a modeling tool table</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Excel/CSV Sync" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>Select <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Clear Selection</strong>.<br />The reference between tables is removed.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141912 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00701: Managing Map Groups

- page_id: `189141912`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141912/Managing+Map+Groups
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:31.829+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2091736993

2091736997

2091736996

**On this page**

33

2091736992

##### Introduction

The purpose of the Map Groups is to simplify the import of Import Maps that depend on each other. By using the Map Groups you avoid having to manually import multiple maps individually. You can collect saved Import Maps in map Group(s) to upload them one by one automatically when importing the Map Group. Before importing data by using an existing Map Group, you need to create them first and then add saved Import Maps. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>

It is useful to create a Map Group only if it contains at least two Import Maps.

The following figure demonstrates the *CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements*Map Group that contains **CarBreak Requirments**, **C*arBreak Structure*** and *Car Break Satisfy*Import Maps. When importing this Map Group, the Import Maps are executed in the same order as specified in the **Manage Map Groups** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements*Map Group in the **Manage Map Groups** dialog.

##### Creating Map Groups

To create a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Manage Map Groups** .
2. In the **Tree** tab of the **Manage Maps Groups** dialog, select the *Excel/CSV imports*Package as the owner for the new Map Group.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The Specification window opens. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **Name** property value, type the name for the new Map Group and click the **Close** button. 
The map group is created and becomes visible in the **Select Map Group for Editing**drop-down list box of the**Manage Maps Groups**dialog. Now you can add Import Maps to the newly created Map Group.

##### Deleting Map Groups

To delete the Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Manage Map Groups** .
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. Click the **Delete**button. The Map Group is deleted.

##### Adding import maps to a Map Group

To add Import Maps to a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Manage Map Groups** . The**Manage Maps Groups**dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. From the Tree or List tab, select the Import Map you want to add to the selected Map Group.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']to add only the selected Import Map or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add it recursively. The Import Map(s) are added to the Map Group

##### Removing import maps from a Map Group

To remove an Import Map from a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Manage Map Groups** . The**Manage Maps Groups**dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. In the **Map Groups** area, select the Import Map you want to remove.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to remove only the selected Import Map or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to remove them recursively. The Import Map(s) are removed from the Map Group.

##### Reordering import maps in the Map Group

By specifying the order of the Import Maps in the Map Group, you define the order of their import.

To reorder the Import Maps in the Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Manage Map Groups** . The **Manage Maps Groups** dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. In the **Map Groups** area, select the Import Map you want to reorder.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to move the selected Import Map up or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to move the Import Map down. The Import Map(s) are reordered in the selected Map Group.

2091741873

**Related pages**

****

- [Importing data from Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files)
- [Saving Import Map](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Saving+Import+Map)
- Excel and CSV Import dialog

/></ri:attachment></ac:image> to move the selected Import Map up or click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="down_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to move the Import Map down.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Import Map(s) are reordered in the selected Map Group.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a25a9b16-709e-4690-8242-a478bd5195c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2091741873</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><ul <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143752 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00702: Managing project usages

- page_id: `189143752`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143752/Managing+project+usages
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:56.844+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

650067215

650067218

650067217

To manage project usages, you must have **Administer Resources** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR2'].

Large Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) projects are built from smaller parts.

We recommend project partitioning when the model has weakly dependent parts, such as type libraries, models of different phases of software/system development, etc. A part of the model moved to a separate project can be used in many projects. From the perspective of the project using elements from another project, we refer to the other project as a*project usage* or *used project*.

You can easily detach a selected part of the model by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting a package to a new server project' space=''] it to a separate TWCloud project and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space=''] it in other server projects. Since projects are used as *read-only*, you cannot modify their contents directly from the main project. To [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing the content of a used project' space='']a used project, you should open it as stand-alone. If there is a need, you can simply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the used project version' space=''] to another used project version.

If you no longer need a fragment of the model in a separate project, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing the content of a used project into your project' space='']the project into the main project. Additionally, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stop using the project in your project' space='']using the project.

You can manage TWCloud project usages in the **Project Usages** dialog, which opens after you select **Options** > **Project Usages**.

650067214

**Related pages**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f58cef8f-b350-4268-925d-ac5352329791"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650067215</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd332d6b-dad5-41a7-a0b8-bd0a4a7023bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650067218</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d1d34f7-5f00-4396-ab37-3520b0401d01"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650067217</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="660e2072-9c67-4bfd-a314-4d8ce037351b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To manage project usages, you must have <strong>Administer Resources</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR2" ri:content-title="Permissions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[permission]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Large Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) projects are built from smaller parts.</p><p>We recommend project partitioning when the model has weakly dependent parts, such as type libraries, models of different phases of software/system development, etc. A part of the model moved to a separate project can be used in many projects. From the perspective of the project using elements from another project, we refer to the other project as a<em> project usage</em> or <em>used project</em>.</p><p>You can easily detach a selected part of the model by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exporting a package to a new server project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exporting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it to a separate TWCloud project and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Start using a project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[re-use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it in other server projects. Since projects are used as <em>read-only</em>, you cannot modify their contents directly from the main project. To <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing the content of a used project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[modify ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>a used project, you should open it as stand-alone. If there is a need, you can simply <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing the used project version" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to another used project version.</p><p>If you no longer need a fragment of the model in a separate project, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing the content of a used project into your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>the project into the main project. Additionally, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stop using the project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stop ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>using the project.</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4c85e912-3baa-41a5-b10c-1df34e108d3e">You can manage TWCloud project usages</ac:inline-comment-marker> in the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Project Usages</strong> dialog, which opens after you select <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Options</strong> &gt; <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Project Usages</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c96cd29-ea23-4a9e-bf7b-50616043e920"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650067214</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="30ed6cd7-6bf5-46d1-b935-201e84674f5f" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=209257443 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Large projects are built from smaller parts. From the perspective of the project using elements from another project, we refer to the other project as a*usage* or *used project*. To learn how to use projects and manage them, see the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Large projects are built from smaller parts. From the perspective of the project using elements from another project, we refer to the other project as a<em> usage</em> or <em>used project</em>. To learn how to use projects and manage them, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ac87287b-9310-4f69-838e-f7da71e93f84" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141763 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00704: Managing regions

- page_id: `189141763`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141763/Managing+regions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:29.785+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['create-region', 'managing-regions', 'remove-region', 'insert-state', 'hide-region']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#createregionTo create a region

Do one of the following:

- In the Containment tree, right-click the State, select Create Element , and then click Region .
- On the diagram, right-click the State, then select the Add New Region .
- On the diagram, select the State symbol and click the **Create Region**button [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav3124c17bcd90e77e6ec5a12cec700032.png'] .
- In the State Specification window, click the Inner Elements property group, then click Create and select Region . Specify the region and click Close or Back .

To remove a region

Do one of the following:

- On the diagram, right-click the State and select Remove Region .
- In the State Specification window, click the Inner Elements property group. Then click the region and click the Delete button.

To insert a State to the region quickly

- In the Containment tree or on the diagram, select the State, and drag it to the State's region.

To display or hide the region name

1. On the diagram, right-click the State and select Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, in the right-top corner, click to expand the menu, and select the Expert mode.
3. Select or clear the Show Region Name check box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

## PAGE 00705: Managing server projects

- page_id: `189143484`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143484/Managing+server+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:53.431+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage your server projects, as well as locally saved server projects (known as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Offline modeling' space='']), in the **Manage Projects** dialog.

Currently open project is automatically selected in the**Manage Projects**dialog, making it easier to findit in the project list.

To open the **Manage Projects**dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting a collaboration session' space='']
2. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Projects .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use the search tab to quickly filter used projects by name.

In the **Manage Projects** dialog, you can:

- For server projects:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening projects from Teamwork Cloud' space='']
  - Rename a server project
  - Remove a server project
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cloning projects' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reviewing historical versions of the project' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing categories' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Branching projects' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Password-protected projects' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing Version Properties' space='']
- For offline server projects:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Offline modeling' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Offline modeling' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143830 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00706: Managing Teamwork Cloud users

- page_id: `189143830`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143830/Managing+Teamwork+Cloud+users
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:58.436+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Managing Teamwork Cloud users includes:

- Creating and modifying user accounts
- Creating roles and assigning them to the users
- Assigning the users to projects

These tasks can be performed only in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Users application' space='TWCloud2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources application' space='TWCloud2024xR2'] of Teamwork Cloud Admin.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Managing Teamwork Cloud users includes:</p><ul><li>Creating and modifying user accounts</li><li>Creating roles and assigning them to the users</li><li>Assigning the users to projects</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>These tasks can be performed only in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR2" ri:content-title="Users application" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR2" ri:content-title="Resources application" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Roles Application]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of Teamwork Cloud Admin.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140004 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00707: Managing unresolved references

- page_id: `189140004`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140004/Managing+unresolved+references
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:41.303+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1525838642

INLINE

1525838654

INLINE

1526101509

INLINE

**On this page**

3

1525838644

INLINE

An unresolved reference means that your project contains an element that refers to some used project element that has become inaccessible. This can happen because the project element is no longer shared, it was removed, it was moved to a non-shared location, etc. To maintain your model integrity and project composition when the element is inaccessible, the modeling tool recovers the inaccessible elements to maintain the references that are indicated in the project as unresolved.

##### Running composition integrity analysis

If recovered elements are detected, a summary of validation errors is shown in the status line. You can run the composition integrity analysis to find and examine the usages of the recovered elements in your project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A summary of validation errors in the status line.

To run the composition integrity analysis

1. Do either: The **Validation Results** panel showing the recovered elements usages in the project opens in a separate tab. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - composition integrity analysis In the **Active Validation Results** panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is REF or NRAPU. From its shortcut menu, select **Run Composition Integrity Analysis**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - composition integrity analysis in Model BrowserIn the Model Browser, right-click an el ement that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu, select Validation > Recovered Element > Run Composition Integrity Analysis . [ATTACHMENT filename='composition_integrity_analysis_from_model_browser.png']
  - On the diagram pane, select the element shape that refers to the recovered element. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_toolbar_recovered_elements_found.png'] > Run Composition Integrity Analysis . [ATTACHMENT filename='managing_recovered_element_from_smart_manipulator.png']

##### Identifying validation errors

These are the abbreviations of common validation errors and their explanations:

- REF : The element is a recovered element. This element is referenced by another element in the project.
- SRE : T he symbol usage of the recovered element. Diagrams may have more than one recovered element displayed as a symbol. Each symbol of a recovered element is listed as a SRE error.
- URE : The element referencing the recovered element.

##### Handling recovered elements

It is recommended that you eliminate recovered elements from your model, using one of the following methods:

- Eliminate the causes of the element recovery . This is the case when recovered elements still exist. Identify the cause of the element recovery and eliminate it by, for example, sharing the package that contains the recovered element, moving the element back from a non-shared location to the shared one, etc.
- Restore recovered elements . This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist but can be restored from the bits of information the modeling tool still has. The restored element has only ID, name, and type.
- Manage the unresolved references that refer to the appropriate recovered elements . This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist and cannot be restored.

The following links outline methods of managing unresolved references using specific commands:

1525838641

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='']

1574919148

INLINE

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the *[ATTACHMENT filename='Project Decomposition Description.mdzip']*****sample model that uses the following projects: *[ATTACHMENT filename='Analysis and Design.mdzip'],**[ATTACHMENT filename='Code Level.mdzip'],* and***[ATTACHMENT filename='domain.mdzip'] .*

Alternatively, you can open the model from*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\project decomposition .*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8999599-ce35-4192-b517-c159670b4a4f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1525838642</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="50d0eddc-2ded-4352-b308-46cd92e529d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1525838654</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc4dd7f6-6ae9-43e3-a8b7-7d8500eab3a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1526101509</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25583595-53e3-416b-8960-7039d3e69ee9"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15e8eb2a-a96d-4710-97a2-91f0d4fc5fb7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1525838644</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An unresolved reference means that your project contains an element that refers to some used project element that has become inaccessible. This can happen because the project element is no longer shared, it was removed, it was moved to a non-shared location, etc. To maintain your model integrity and project composition when the element is inaccessible, the modeling tool recovers the inaccessible elements to maintain the references that are indicated in the project as unresolved. </p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e00884b9-365d-434b-a913-af4eb6566b0c">Running composition integrity analysis</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e00884b9-365d-434b-a913-af4eb6566b0c">If recovered elements are detected, a summary of validation errors is shown in the status line. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="875bc6a5-380e-4043-b3ec-918f7730bcd9">You can run the composition integrity analysis to find and examine the usages of the recovered elements in your project. </ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e00884b9-365d-434b-a913-af4eb6566b0c"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="summary_of_validation_errors.png" /></ac:image></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e00884b9-365d-434b-a913-af4eb6566b0c">A summary of validation errors in the status line.</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3a399a58-9b19-4f0b-8096-59670d00fc6e">run the composition integrity analysis</ac:inline-comment-marker> </p><hr /><ol><li>Do either:<ul><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="884a511e-7619-498d-bc47-38ed0b1c8ebc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">composition integrity analysis</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="807629af-a196-47ad-a720-42bfd53ed283">In the <strong>Active Validation Results</strong> panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is REF or NRAPU. From its shortcut menu, select <strong>Run Composition Integrity Analysis</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="active_validation_results_panel.png" /></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="807629af-a196-47ad-a720-42bfd53ed283"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05f5463d-e141-4ebe-bef5-909a24d0d14c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">composition integrity analysis in Model Browser</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>In the Model Browser, right-click an el</ac:inline-comment-marker>ement that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu, select <strong>Validation</strong> &gt; <strong>Recovered Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Run Composition Integrity Analysis</strong>.<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="composition_integrity_analysis_from_model_browser.png" /></ac:image></li><li>On the diagram pane, select the element shape that refers to the recovered element. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="smart_manipulator_toolbar_recovered_elements_found.png" /></ac:image> &gt; <strong>Run Composition Integrity Analysis</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="managing_recovered_element_from_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p>The <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel showing the recovered elements usages in the project opens in a separate tab.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_results_panel.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Identifying validation errors</h3><p>These are the abbreviations of common validation errors and their explanations:</p><ul><li><strong>REF</strong>: The element is a recovered element. This element is referenced by another element in the project. </li><li><strong>SRE</strong>: T<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">he symbol usage of the recovered element.</span> Diagrams may have more than one recovered element displayed as a symbol. Each symbol of a recovered element is listed as a SRE error. </li><li><strong>URE</strong>: <span>The element referencing the recovered element.</span> </li></ul><h3>Handling recovered elements</h3><p>It is recommended that you eliminate recovered elements from your model, using one of the following methods:</p><ul><li><strong>Eliminate the causes of the element recovery</strong>. This is the case when recovered elements still exist. Identify the cause of the element recovery and eliminate it by, for example, sharing the package that contains the recovered element, moving the element back from a non-shared location to the shared one, etc.</li><li><strong>Restore recovered elements</strong>. This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist but can be restored from the bits of information the modeling tool still has. The restored element has only ID, name, and type.</li><li><strong>Manage the unresolved references that refer to the appropriate recovered elements</strong>. This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist and cannot be restored.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The following links outline methods of managing unresolved references using specific commands:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f337638d-9112-4a03-a02f-4096fcec762c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7bcd508a-c037-4b60-a337-4a6105d77277"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1525838641</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5f0c88fd-99de-47dd-b15f-185f160c696a">Related pages</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3384e73c-16b5-40d0-8cb8-e5b036cc6dd1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1574919148</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures on this page is the <em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Project Decomposition Description.mdzip" /></ac:link></em><strong> </strong>sample model that uses the following projects: <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Analysis and Design.mdzip" /></ac:link>, </em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Code Level.mdzip" /></ac:link>,</em> and<em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="domain.mdzip" /></ac:link> .</em></p><p>Alternatively, you can o<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">pen the model from </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\product features\project decomposition .</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139994 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00708: Managing used projects

- page_id: `189139994`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139994/Managing+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:40.033+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning
- labels: ['used-projects']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305472217

305472219

305472218

You can manage usages of an open local project in the**Used Projects**dialog. To learn how to manage Teamwork Cloud project usages, go to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space=''].

To specify project usage options

1. Do one of the following: • From the Options menu, select Project Usages . • Right-click a used project in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] and, from its shortcut menu, select Project Usages > Options . The Used Projects dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='used_projects_dialog.png']
2. Do one or both of the following:
  - Specify the following project usage options: Dialog element nameDescription**Accessibility****Read-only**- the project is loaded for reading only and cannot be modified in the main project. Elements of the used project can be used and referenced in the main project.**Read-write**- the project can be edited directly in the main project. Elements of this used project can be used, referenced, and modified in the main project.**Load Mode****Always load** (default) - the selected used project is always loaded when the main project is opened.**Autoload** - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The modeling tool monitors user activities in the project and loads the particular used project on demand by the project.**Autoload with prompt** - this mode is similar to the **Autoload** mode, except that the modeling tool asks the user for a confirmation before loading the used project.**Manual load** - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The model integrity is not broken, as all required elements of the used project exist as simplified versions (that is, loaded as proxies) of the elements used in the project. This load mode is recommended for all used projects that are stable or rarely modified. 
Reload the main project after changing the load mode of the selected used project.**Use Index**Select this check box to load indexed elements from the used project that is not loaded. This option is available only if the used project is not loaded. Using indexed elements in the project increases the performance.**Packages**this is the list of all shared packages of a used project and paths for mounting these packages in the main project.
  - Use the following commands: CommandDescription**Use Project**Click to select projects for usage in the open project and specify the usage options. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='']** wizard opens.**Remove** > **Remove with References**Click to remove the selected used project from the project. After removal, all the elements of the used project disappear from the Containment tree, and all the references to these elements are cleared from other places in the model.**Remove** > **Remove, keep references**Click to remove the selected used project and keep model references across it in the model. There are two cases when you may need to remove the used project this way:**Case #1**: You may need to replace a used project with the equivalent project carrying the same elements (but with slightly different content) and retaining all the references.In this case, removing the initially used project, but keeping references creates an automated project usage that temporarily carries the model level references. This automated usage is invalid, since it is unconfirmed. You can confirm it by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages' space=''].**Case #2**: You may need to refactor projects that are created with the modeling tool versions earlier than 17.0.3 and remove excessive project usages. These versions of the modeling tool have only user-defined project usages. Because of this, a project usually had direct user defined usages to indirectly used projects besides the indirect usages through directly used projects. So both “project → directly used project → indirectly used project” and “project → indirectly used project” usage paths were present. In this case it may be advantageous to remove the surplus usage “project → indirectly used project” by clicking the Remove, keep References button.**Import**Click to import the contents of the selected used project to the main project. The Import button is available if the selected used project:Is not a MagicDraw native projectIs locked (valid for server projects only)Is not locked by another user (valid for server projects only)The same used project can be imported only once. After the import, all elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner 
structure of the used project is included in the project as a package. The imported project is not deleted from the file system.Standard and system profiles cannot be imported.**Reload**Click to reload the selected used project in the main project. This button is available only when the selected used project is loaded.**Unload**Click to unload the selected used project from the main project leaving only used elements loaded. It is recommended to unload rarely used projects in order to reduce memory usage while working with large projects.This button is available when a selected used project is loaded.**Open Project**Click to open the selected used project for editing.**Options**Click to open the **Project Options** dialog to manage styles and element display settings of the selected used project. It allows users to change element styles in the used project even without opening it.
3. Click**OK**.

305472216

**Related pages**
