To learn how to manage Teamwork Cloud project usages, go to </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></p><p>To specify project usage options</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br />• From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong>.<br />• Right-click a used project in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3f8fd666-ee51-49d8-bfb1-b04a3433325b"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </ac:inline-comment-marker> and, from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Options</strong>.<br />The <strong>Used Projects</strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Used Projects dialog" ac:alt="Used Projects dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="used_projects_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bf61027b-a39a-45ad-8754-b4d516a9a13c">Do one or both of the following:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ul><li><p>Specify the following project usage options:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Dialog element name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Accessibility</strong></td><td><ul><li><strong>Read-only </strong>- the project is loaded for reading only and cannot be modified in the main project. Elements of the used project can be used and referenced in the main project.</li><li><strong>Read-write </strong>- the project can be edited directly in the main project. Elements of this used project can be used, referenced, and modified in the main project.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Load Mode</strong></td><td><ul><li><p><strong>Always load</strong> (default) - the selected used project is always loaded when the main project is opened.</p></li><li><p><strong>Autoload</strong> - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The modeling tool monitors user activities in the project and loads the particular used project on demand by the project.</p></li><li><p><strong>Autoload with prompt</strong> - this mode is similar to the <strong>Autoload</strong> mode, except that the modeling tool asks the user for a confirmation before loading the used project.</p></li><li><p><strong>Manual load</strong> - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The model integrity is not broken, as all required elements of the used project exist as simplified versions (that is, loaded as proxies) of the elements used in the project. This load mode is recommended for all used projects that are stable or rarely modified.<br />Reload the main project after changing the load mode of the selected used project.</p></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Use Index</strong></td><td>Select this check box to load indexed elements from the used project that is not loaded. This option is available only if the used project is not loaded. Using indexed elements in the project increases the performance.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Packages</strong></td><td>this is the list of all shared packages of a used project and paths for mounting these packages in the main project.</td></tr></tbody></table></li></ul><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"> </span></p><ul><li><p>Use the following commands:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Command</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Use Project</strong></td><td><p>Click to select projects for usage in the open project and specify the usage options. The <strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong> wizard opens.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Remove</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove with References</strong></td><td><p>Click to remove the selected used project from the project. After removal, all the elements of the used project disappear from the Containment tree, and all the references to these elements are cleared from other places in the model.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Remove</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove, keep references</strong></td><td><p>Click to remove the selected used project and keep model references across it in the model. There are two cases when you may need to remove the used project this way:</p><p><strong>Case #1</strong>: You may need to replace a used project with the equivalent project carrying the same elements (but with slightly different content) and retaining all the references.</p><p>In this case, removing the initially used project, but keeping references creates an automated project usage that temporarily carries the model level references. This automated usage is invalid, since it is unconfirmed. You can confirm it by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[replacing the initially used project with another project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Case #2</strong>: You may need to refactor projects that are created with the modeling tool versions earlier than 17.0.3 and remove excessive project usages. These versions of the modeling tool have only user-defined project usages. Because of this, a project usually had direct user defined usages to indirectly used projects besides the indirect usages through directly used projects. So both “project → directly used project → indirectly used project” and “project → indirectly used project” usage paths were present. In this case it may be advantageous to remove the surplus usage “project → indirectly used project” by clicking the Remove, keep References button.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Import</strong></td><td><p>Click to import the contents of the selected used project to the main project. The Import button is available if the selected used project:</p><ul><li>Is not a MagicDraw native project</li><li>Is locked (valid for server projects only)</li><li>Is not locked by another user (valid for server projects only)</li></ul><p>The same used project can be imported only once. After the import, all elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner<br />structure of the used project is included in the project as a package. The imported project is not deleted from the file system.</p><p>Standard and system profiles cannot be imported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Reload</strong></td><td><p>Click to reload the selected used project in the main project. This button is available only when the selected used project is loaded.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Unload</strong></td><td><p>Click to unload the selected used project from the main project leaving only used elements loaded. It is recommended to unload rarely used projects in order to reduce memory usage while working with large projects.</p><p>This button is available when a selected used project is loaded.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Open Project</strong></td><td>Click to open the selected used project for editing.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Options</strong></td><td><p>Click to open the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog to manage styles and element display settings of the selected used project. It allows users to change element styles in the used project even without opening it.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></li></ul></li><li><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Click </span><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"> </span><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">OK</strong><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">.</span></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd0c8cc6-7361-4349-a16f-e9ca82ca5c9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305472216</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f4647c59-65ba-4a55-ae28-170f4669199b" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143268 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00709: Mandatory Tagged Value

- page_id: `189143268`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143268/Mandatory+Tagged+Value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:56.342+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

LackOfTagVal

**Description**

This validation rule checks that if the stereotype's tag definition multiplicity is 1, only one value to a slot is allowed.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Element

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure the tag has exactly 1 value.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *Car*stereotype contains a tag definition *gearTransmission*with the multiplicity set to one. The class *Sedan*with the applied stereotype *Car*does not have the *gearTransmission*tag specified with a value, which causes an error. 
The error is resolved by specifying the *gearTransmission*tag with a value.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>LackOfTagVal</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks that if the stereotype's <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e14238d-efa6-4400-84e7-8fcaa3e3a05b">tag definition</ac:inline-comment-marker> multiplicity is 1, only one value to a slot is allowed.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Element</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, make sure the tag has exactly 1 value.<strong> </strong></li></ul><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="96d08a8d-61b6-4fb5-9ed4-dd630b18b829">Example</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Mandatory Tagged Value.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <em>Car </em>stereotype contains a tag definition <em>gearTransmission </em>with the multiplicity set to one. The class <em>Sedan </em>with the applied stereotype <em>Car </em>does not have the <em>gearTransmission </em>tag specified with a value, which causes an error.<br />The error is resolved by specifying the <em>gearTransmission </em>tag with a value.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141545 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00710: Manifestations

- page_id: `189141545`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141545/Manifestations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:28.349+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1058526979

1058526981

1058526980

An artifact embodies or manifests a number of model elements. It owns the manifestations, each representing the utilization of a packageable element.

To create the manifestations, simply draw the Manifestation link from an artifact to a component.

To display the manifested artifacts on the component shape

- On the diagram pane, select the component shape, click the Compartments button, and from the list, select Artifacts .
- In the component Symbol Properties dialog, cancel the selection of the Suppress Artifacts check box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1058527124

**Related pages**

- Model Elements
- Component diagram
- Specification Window
- Artifact
- Compartment

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72de48ea-04ff-4c42-b49f-d48c2a190821"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1058526979</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4af85558-0fa6-4cfc-962c-c73d4205308e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1058526981</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="525018e6-c9fe-4c12-aa94-748358241404"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1058526980</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An artifact embodies or manifests a number of model elements. It owns the manifestations, each representing the utilization of a packageable element.</p><p>To create the manifestations, simply draw the Manifestation link from an artifact to a component.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display the manifested artifacts on the component shape</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the component shape, click the Compartments button, and from the list, select <strong>Artifacts</strong>.</li><li>In the component <strong>Symbol Properties </strong>dialog, cancel the selection of the <strong>Suppress Artifacts</strong> check box.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="manifestation.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a790420-28ce-4670-a9b5-66ce00246c2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1058527124</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Model+elements">Model Elements</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Component+diagram">Component diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Specification+window">Specification Window</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CBMP2024xR2/Artifacts">Artifact</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Compartments">Compartment</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140390 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00711: Manipulations in the relation map

- page_id: `189140390`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140390/Manipulations+in+the+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:56.216+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-suppress-expand', 'relation-map-zoom', 'relation-map-hide', 'relation-map-remove', 'relation-map-restore-refresh', 'relation-map-validate', 'relation-map-decompose', 'relation-map-create-element']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manipulate and analyze your relation map in the following ways:

- / branches
- Move /
- / / /
- / an element
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

suppressTo suppress branches

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='suppress_button.png'] near the Node.

expandTo expand branches

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_button.png'] near the Node.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

move_structureTo move the relation map structure

- Click on an empty place in the Relation Map and drag it. The whole structure will be moved appropriately.

move_nodeTo move the selected Node

- Click a Node and drag it. The selected Node will be moved appropriately.

zoom_inTo zoom in the relation map structure

Do one of the following:

- Hold down Ctrl while scrolling up.
- Click the Zoom In button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_in.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad +.

zoom_outTo zoom out the relation map structure

Do one of the following:

- Hold down Ctrl while scrolling down.
- Click the Zoom Out button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_out.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad -.

fitTo fit in window

Do one of the following:

- Click the Fit in Window button [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_in_window.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+W.

zoom_1_1To zoom 1:1

- Click the Zoom 1:1 button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_1_1.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad /.

hideTo hide an existing element in the diagram

- Press Delete.

removeTo remove an existing element from the model

- Press Ctrl+D.

restoreTo restore the layout of the manually suppressed/expanded branches, moved, removed and hidden symbols

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the View toolbar. If the**Preserve manually suppressed/expanded branches and hidden elements** option in the **Environment Options** dialog > **Diagrams** > **Relation Map**, is selected, the layout of moved elements is restored.

invalidTo start the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='']of the relation map

- Click the Validate Diagram button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the toolbar of that diagram, table, matrix, or map. The diagram is validated against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored.

containment_treeTo select an element in the Containment tree

- Select the element and click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_in_containment_tree.png'] on the Navigation toolbar.
- On the selected element shortcut menu, click Select in Containment Tree .

decomposeTo decompose an element

1. Select an element.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the selected element shortcut menu, select Create Related Element .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_element_button.png'] or press Insert (or [ATTACHMENT filename='command-button.jpg'] + I).
3. Select element type and relationship. If selected element type exists in the model, select an appropriate element from the list to represent it in the relation map. >]]> 
The element is decomposed.

openTo open an existing element list for the unnamed element

1. Select unnamed element.
2. Do one of the following: - Click the left mouse button. - Press Space. An existing element list opens, if such elements already exist in the model.

same_typeTo create element of the same type

1. Type element name.
2. Press Ctrl+Enter while in the name edit mode.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142218 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00712: Manual numbering

- page_id: `189142218`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142218/Manual+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:03.802+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Generic numbering mechanism > Working with generic numbering mechanism
- labels: ['how-to-update-the-last-id']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Using the manual numbering, you can:

- Set the ID for one or more elements.
- Increase, decrease, or change a number for an element.
- Add a prefix for the element number.
- Change a numbering format.
- Update the last ID.

To number the element

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the element list on the right side of the dialog, select an element you want to number.
3. Click Create .

To change the element’s numbering symbol

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Click the Edit button.
  - If you need to change just the last number of the element ID, click **Edit**. The **Edit ID** dialog opens. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] You can enter only the number, if the selected element’s numbering value is numerical. 
You can enter only a new literal symbol, if the selected element’s numbering value is a literal symbol.
  - If you need to change the whole element ID (including the prefix), click Set Custom ID . The Custom ID dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Custom_ID_dialog.png']
3. Enter a new numbering symbol.
4. Click **OK**.

To remove the element’s number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element from which you want to remove the number and click the Remove button.
3. You can select either to remove the number only from the selected element or to remove numbers from all elements owned by the selected elements. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Remove button. Only the selected element’s number is removed.
  - Click the Remove Recursively button. Numbers are removed from the selected element and all elements owned by the selected element.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To increase the element number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element which number you want to increase.
3. Click the Increase button. The element number is increased by one.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To decrease the element number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and select Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element which number you want to decrease.
3. Click the **Decrease** button. The element number is decreased by one. The **Decrease** button is available, when the selected element’s number is not the first in the list.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To renumber the elements

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Click Details to extent the dialog with the renumbering commands.
3. You can select either to renumber only elements listed in the element list or to renumber elements listed in the element list including their owning elements. Do one of the following:
  - Click the **Renumber** button. All the elements in the elements list are renumbered successively.
  - Click the **Renumber Recursively** button. All the elements and elements owned by the selected element are renumbered successively.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

If the **Renumber from Initial Value** is selected, the whole element number changes. Otherwise just the number of the selected level changes.

To add a prefix to the element number

1. On the element owner’s shortcut menu, select Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the Prefix column, click the appropriate cell (according to the used numbering property and numbering scheme).
3. Type the prefix. It can be any symbol.
4. Click OK .

The following example illustrates how numbers with prefixes and without prefixes are depicted on the element shape.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change a separator in the element number

1. Open the element owner’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the Separator column, click the appropriate cell (according to the used numbering property and numbering scheme).
3. Type the separator symbol. A new separator will override the earlier predefined separator.
4. Click OK .

The following example illustrates the number separator changing from “.” to “/”.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change a numbering scheme

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the appropriate Numbered property row, click the Numbering Scheme cell to open the list of the defined numbering schemes.
3. Select the desired numbering scheme in that list.
4. Click OK .

When you change the numbering scheme for the selected element, the new numbering scheme is applied to all elements owned by the selected element.

The following example illustrates how numbers are depicted on the element shape after changing the numbering scheme from numerical to literal.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To update the last ID

1. Right-click an element numbered in the sequence, whose last ID you want to update.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Element Numbering . The appropriate dialog opens.
3. Click Details and then click the Update Last ID button.
4. Wait while the message informs you that the last ID is set to the particular value. This value is the highest ID in the sequence.
5. Click OK to close the Element Numbering dialog.

As a result, all subsequently created elements are numbered continuing from the the highest ID. This is especially useful after you delete one or more elements from the end of the numbered sequence. In such cases, the last ID remains higher than it is expected to be, causing a numbering gap in the sequence. Updating the last ID prevents the emergence of that gap.

However, if a numbering gap is not at the end of the sequence, it remains even after the update of the last ID.

For better understanding the functionality, let's study a few cases.

1. If you have a sequence from 1 to 5, and delete elements with ID = 4 and ID = 5, the ID of a subsequently created element by default is 6. But if you update the last ID in advance, the one of the subsequently created element is 4 as it should be.
2. If you have a sequence from 1 to 3 and from 5 to 6, the last ID after the update is 6. As you can see, ID = 4 is skipped, because it isn't the highest ID in the sequence; the highest ID is 6.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="50536a63-5525-4885-b49b-9c50e8268fc2"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Using the manual numbering, you can:</p><ul><li>Set the ID for one or more elements.</li><li>Increase, decrease, or change a number for an element.</li><li>Add a prefix for the element number.</li><li>Change a numbering format.</li><li>Update the last ID.</li></ul><p> </p><p>To number the element</p><hr /><ol><li>On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click <strong>Element Numbering</strong>. The <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the element list on the right side of the dialog, select an element you want to number.</li><li>Click <strong>Create</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>To change the element’s numbering symbol</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click <strong>Element Numbering</strong>. The <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Click the <strong>Edit</strong> button. <br /><br /><ul><li><p>If you need to change just the last number of the element ID, click <strong>Edit</strong>. The <strong>Edit ID</strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Edit ID dialog" ac:alt="Edit ID dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit_ID_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ed643c1-4e3d-4d10-a1d4-c5842f282cbe"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can enter only the number, if the selected element’s numbering value is numerical.</span><br /><span>You can enter only a new literal symbol, if the selected element’s numbering value is a literal symbol.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>If you need to change the whole element ID (including the prefix), click <strong>Set Custom ID</strong>. The <strong>Custom ID </strong>dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Set Custom ID dialog" ac:alt="Set Custom ID dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Custom_ID_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p>Enter a new numbering symbol.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the element’s number</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click <strong>Element Numbering</strong>. The <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the element from which you want to remove the number and click the <strong>Remove</strong> button.</li><li>You can select either to remove the number only from the selected element or to remove numbers from all elements owned by the selected elements. Do one of the following:<br /><br /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Remove</strong> button. Only the selected element’s number is removed.</li><li>Click the<strong> Remove Recursively</strong> button. Numbers are removed from the selected element and all elements owned by the selected element.<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>To increase the element number</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click <strong>Element Numbering</strong>. The <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the element which number you want to increase.</li><li>Click the <strong>Increase</strong> button. The element number is increased by one.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To decrease the element number</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and select<strong> Element Numbering</strong>. The <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the element which number you want to decrease.</li><li><p>Click the <strong>Decrease</strong> button. The element number is decreased by one.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26c158ef-3cc9-4f73-a091-38f688cfb98f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Decrease</strong> button is available, when the selected element’s number is not the first in the list.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To renumber the elements</p><hr /><ol><li>On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click <strong>Element Numbering</strong>. The <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Click <strong>Details</strong> to extent the dialog with the renumbering commands.</li><li>You can select either to renumber only elements listed in the element list or to renumber elements listed in the element list including their owning elements. Do one of the following:<br /><br /><ul><li><p>Click the <strong>Renumber</strong> button. All the elements in the elements list are renumbered successively.</p></li><li><p>Click the <strong>Renumber Recursively</strong> button. All the elements and elements owned by the selected element are renumbered successively.<br /><br /></p></li></ul></li><li><p> Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0965d28c-77f8-44e0-8cbb-3d78d8980eec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Renumber from Initial Value</strong> is selected, the whole element number changes. Otherwise just the number of the selected level changes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To add a prefix to the element number</p><hr /><ol><li>On the element owner’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Element Numbering</strong>. The <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Prefix</strong> column, click the appropriate cell (according to the used numbering property and numbering scheme). </li><li>Type the prefix. It can be any symbol.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The following example illustrates how numbers with prefixes and without prefixes are depicted on the element shape.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Numbers with and without prefix." ac:alt="Numbers with and without prefix."><ri:attachment ri:filename="numbering_prefix_added.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To change a separator in the element number</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the element owner’s shortcut menu and click <strong>Element Numbering</strong>. The <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Separator</strong> column, click the appropriate cell (according to the used numbering property and numbering scheme).</li><li><p>Type the separator symbol.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57275d3f-a147-48ab-bf1e-51beceaf3ef9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A new separator will override the earlier predefined separator.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The following example illustrates the number separator changing from “.” to “/”.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Numbering separators" ac:alt="Numbering separators"><ri:attachment ri:filename="numbering_separator_changed.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To change a numbering scheme</p><hr /><ol><li>On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click <strong>Element Numbering</strong>. The <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the appropriate <strong>Numbered property</strong> row, click the <strong>Numbering Scheme</strong> cell to open the list of the defined numbering schemes.</li><li>Select the desired numbering scheme in that list.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cd0af7a-fe96-4c56-ae2a-7c2d82782575"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you change the numbering scheme for the selected element, the new numbering scheme is applied to all elements owned by the selected element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>The following example illustrates how numbers are depicted on the element shape after changing the numbering scheme from numerical to literal.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Changing numbering scheme" ac:alt="Changing numbering scheme"><ri:attachment ri:filename="numbering_sheme_change.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To update the last ID</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click an element numbered in the sequence, whose last ID you want to update.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Element Numbering</strong>. The appropriate dialog opens.</li><li>Click <strong>Details</strong> and then click the <strong>Update Last ID</strong> button.</li><li>Wait while the message informs you that the last ID is set to the particular value. This value is the highest ID in the sequence.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog.</li></ol><p>As a result, all subsequently created elements are numbered continuing from the the highest ID. This is especially useful after you delete one or more elements from the end of the numbered sequence. In such cases, the last ID remains higher than it is expected to be, causing a numbering gap in the sequence. Updating the last ID prevents the emergence of that gap.</p><p>However, if a numbering gap is not at the end of the sequence, it remains even after the update of the last ID.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="427d4f2d-0f1c-413c-9754-2fb488b37435"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For better understanding the functionality, let's study a few cases.</p><ol><li>If you have a sequence from 1 to 5, and delete elements with ID = 4 and ID = 5, the ID of a subsequently created element by default is 6. But if you update the last ID in advance, the one of the subsequently created element is 4 as it should be.</li><li>If you have a sequence from 1 to 3 and from 5 to 6, the last ID after the update is 6. As you can see, ID = 4 is skipped, because it isn't the highest ID in the sequence; the highest ID is 6.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=198377493 space=MD2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00713: Manually configuring properties file

- page_id: `198377493`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/198377493/Manually+configuring+properties+file
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-11-18T07:05:40.391+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Used Projects Auto Updater Plugin
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains how to correctly configure a Used Projects Auto Update Plugin properties file.

To configure the properties file for updating used projects

- In the <modeling tool installation directory>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.twcusageupdater directory, open the sample.properties file (or any generated properties file) through a wizard.
- Specify the properties values listed below in the table (or leave the default values if they do not need to be changed).

| Properties | Description |
| --- | --- |
| twc.server | TWCloud server hostname or IP (with port if it is not default) |
| twc.user.name | TWCloud username |
| twc.user.password | TWCloud user hashed password (the only way to get a hashed password is to generate it through first) |
| twc.project | TWCloud main project URI* |
| twc.project.password | Project hashed password if there is password protection (the only way to get a hashed password is to generate it through first) |
| twc.used.projects | List of used projects URI (with hashed passwords if project is password protected. Pattern <URI>,<URI>#<password>,<URI>.The only way to get a hashed password is to generate it through first) |
| twc.use.ssl | Use SSL flag |
| twc.personal.access.token | The personal access token generated using the My account application in the Teamwork Cloud web UI. To learn more, see . |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140251 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00715: Mapping ports on a Part type change

- page_id: `189140251`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140251/Mapping+ports+on+a+Part+type+change
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:48.625+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams > Composite Structure diagram
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

On a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space=''] type change, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] of the original Part type are automatically replaced with ports of a new type. Connectors are reconnected accordingly too.

Ports are mapped automatically if particular rules are met. Otherwise, the **Port Mapping** dialog appears.

##### Rules of automatic port mapping

Ports are mapped automatically:

- When the following properties of the Port of the original Part type are the same as the properties of the Port of the new part type:
  - Port type and name
  - Port type (note that only one Port of the new Part type has the same type as Port(s) of the original Part type)
  - Port name (valid if the type of the original Port is not specified)
- Ports of the original Part type and the Port of the new Part type has the same [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space=''] .
- When the new Part type is subtype of the original Part type (inherited ports are mapped automatically with ports which they are inherited).
- When the ports of a new type redefines ports of original Part type.

##### The Port Mapping dialog

The **Port Mapping** dialog appears on changing a Part type when ports of the type cannot be mapped automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the elements of the **Port Mapping** dialog described in the following table.

| Element number | Element | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 |  | Lists the type of the Part from which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type. |
| 2 |  | Lists the type of the Part to which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type. |
| 3 |  | Displays lines between the mapped ports. |
| 4 |  | Creates a new port in the To list. In the From list, click a port and then drag it to the "Drag port(s) here to create a new port" box. The port for the new type will be created and mapped. |

To map ports in the **Port Mapping** dialog

1. Select a Port in the From box.
2. Drag the selected Port onto a Port in the **To** box. The ports are mapped and the line between them is displayed. You can also select multiple number of ports in the **From** box and drag onto a port in the **To** box. The selected ports will be mapped with the Port.

To create a new Port in the **To** box

1. Select a Port in the From box.
2. Drag the selected Port onto the following box: "*Drag**port(s)**here**to**create**a**new**port*". The new Port is created in the **To** box and ports are mapped. You can also select multiple number of ports in the **From** box and drag onto the box: "*Drag port(s) here to create a new port*". The selected number of ports will be created.

To un-map ports in the **Port Mapping** dialog

1. Select the line between the mapped ports.
2. Press the Delete key. The line is deleted and ports unmapped.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>On a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /></ac:link> type change, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the original Part type are automatically replaced with ports of a new type. Connectors are reconnected accordingly too.</p><p>Ports are mapped automatically if particular rules are met. Otherwise, the <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog appears.</p><h3>Rules of automatic port mapping</h3><p>Ports are mapped automatically:</p><ul><li>When the following properties of the Port of the original Part type are the same as the properties of the Port of the new part type:<ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Port type and name</strong></li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Port </strong><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">type</strong> (note that only one Port of the new Part type has the same type as Port(s) of the original Part type) </li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Port name</strong> (valid if the type of the original Port is <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">not</strong> specified) </li></ul></li><li>Ports of the original Part type and the Port of the new Part type has the same <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interface" /></ac:link>. </li><li>When the new Part type is subtype of the original Part type (inherited ports are mapped automatically with ports which they are inherited).</li><li>When the ports of a new type redefines ports of original Part type. </li></ul><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>The Port Mapping dialog</h3><p>The <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog appears on changing a Part type when ports of the type cannot be mapped automatically. </p><p><ac:image ac:title="Port Mapping dialog" ac:alt="Port Mapping dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_mapping.png" /></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></p><p>See the elements of the <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog described in the following table.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Element number</th><th>Element</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>1</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_mapping3.png" /></ac:image></td><td>Lists the type of the Part from which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type.</td></tr><tr><td>2</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_mapping4.png" /></ac:image></td><td>Lists the type of the Part to which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type.</td></tr><tr><td>3</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_mapping1.png" /></ac:image></td><td>Displays lines between the mapped ports.</td></tr><tr><td>4</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_mapping2.png" /></ac:image></td><td>Creates a new port in the <strong>To</strong> list. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />In the <strong>From</strong> list, click a port and then drag it to the &quot;Drag port(s) here to create a new port&quot; box. The port for the new type will be created and mapped.</td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><p>To map ports in the <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Port in the <strong>From</strong> box.</li><li><p>Drag the selected Port onto a Port in the <strong>To</strong> box. The ports are mapped and the line between them is displayed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2018e296-0847-4d0a-8481-8d55c293ae7f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also select multiple number of ports in the <strong>From</strong> box and drag onto a port in the <strong>To</strong> box. The selected ports will be mapped with the Port. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>To create a new Port in the <strong>To</strong> box</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Port in the <strong>From</strong> box.</li><li><p>Drag the selected Port onto the following box: &quot;<em>Drag</em><em>port(s)</em><em>here</em><em>to</em><em>create</em><em>a</em><em>new</em><em>port</em>&quot;. The new Port is created in the <strong>To</strong> box and ports are mapped.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc09ca29-20ea-4daa-b80c-d08fa53fe280"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also select multiple number of ports in the <strong>From</strong> box and drag onto the box: &quot;<em>Drag port(s) here to create a new port</em>&quot;. The selected number of ports will be created. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>To un-map ports in the <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the line between the mapped ports.</li><li>Press the <strong>Delete</strong> key. The line is deleted and ports unmapped. </li></ol><p> </p><p style="text-align: center;" /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145598 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00716: Mapping to UML Rules

- page_id: `189145598`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142307 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00717: Marking elements as auxiliary

- page_id: `189142307`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142307/Marking+elements+as+auxiliary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:09.303+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639285959

639285961

639285960

An auxiliary element is an element needed in the model but not necessarily visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']. Usually some profiles are marked as auxiliary and are not displayed by default in the model. You can mark the element in your model as an auxiliary as well.

To mark an element as auxiliary

1. Select the element in the Model Browser.
2. In the Properties tab, select the Tags tab.
3. Set the treatAsAuxiliaryInOwningProject tag value to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting the tag value.

The element marked as auxiliary is visible in the Model Browser if the option **Show Auxiliary Resources**is selected in the Options.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Options drop-down list.

You can specify that all auxiliary resources are shown in the model by default by specifying in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**of your tool.

To show auxiliary resources by default in your model

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment .
2. In the search box, type auxiliary . All auxiliary resources related properties appears in the options list.
3. Set the Show Auxiliary Resources property value to true .

639285958

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139693 space=MD2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00718: Memory allocation

- page_id: `189139693`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139693/Memory+allocation
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-25T13:47:46.999+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Improving performance
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Low memory notification

1427463063

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Low memory notification

1427463065

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Low memory notification

1427463064

If you receive an error message saying your modeling tool is out of memory, the application does not have enough memory to operate. You can change the amount of allocated memory directly in the error message dialog or by changing modeling tool properties.

#### TIP: Memory allocation concept

Memory allocation concept

Memory allocation for the product is the same as the Java heap size. To increase the amount of memory allocated for the product, simply increase the Java heap size.

##### Recommended amount of allocated memory

The amount of allocated memory (or Java heap size) should be set to less than the available physical RAM on your computer. To calculate the approximate amount of memory you should allocate, subtract the amount of memory taken by any other processes that will run concurrently with your modeling tool from the total amount of RAM in your computer. Otherwise, your JVM process will likely swap, and that will slow down the application.

##### Changing the amount of allocated memory

You can set the amount of memory allocated for your modeling tool either in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog, in the modeling tool properties file, or in the **Environment Variables** dialog on your machine.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the **Environment Options** dialog

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. Expand the General options group on the left side of the dialog, and select the Memory Settings category.
3. In the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, change the value of the Maximum Heap Size option. You should only change the value of the Thread Stack Size option if a *java.lang.StackOverflowError*error occurs.

#### TIP: Low memory notification

Low memory notification

While working on the project, you can get the *Low memory* notification. This notification is triggered by the mechanism which tracks whether the allocated amount of memory is enough to work with your currently opened projects. Click *Open Environment Options*to open the **Environment options** dialog where you can adjust memory settings.

To enable/disable notifications about low memory

1. Go to Environment Options > Display > Notify about low memory option

- 
  - Set to true to show notifications about low memory.
  - Set to false to disable notifications about low memory. Also, t his notification can be disabled, by clicking Do not show again option in the Low memory notification.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the modeling tool properties file

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the modeling tool properties file.
2. In the **JAVA_ARGS** line, change the value of the **-Xmx** property. For example, change the **-Xmx800M** to **-Xmx1066M**.
3. Save and close the file.
4. Restart your modeling tool.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the **Environment Variables** dialog on your machine

1. Go to Control Panel > System and Security > System > Advanced system settings. The System Properties dialog opens.
2. In the Advanced tab, click Environment Variables . The Environment Variables dialog opens.
3. In the System variables section, do one of the following:
  1. If the _JAVA_OPTIONS variable is not in the variables list, click New . New System Variable dialog opens.
  2. If the **_JAVA_OPTIONS** variable is in the variables list, select it and click **Edit**.**Edit System Variable**dialog opens.
4. Set **Variable name** to **_JAVA_OPTIONS** and **Variable value** to the desired memory amount, e.g. **-Xmx1066M**. Click **OK**. Changing options via the **Environment Variables** dialog overrides specified options for all Java applications.

1427463062

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Improving performance' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Background Task Manager' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation period' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146003 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00719: Memory and Disk Space Requirements for Large Models

- page_id: `189146003`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189146003/Memory+and+Disk+Space+Requirements+for+Large+Models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:42:09.447+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Guidelines for Working with Large Models
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Memory

The memory allocation for the modeling tool by project size:

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Allocated Memory 2 |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Earlier versions | 2021x and later 1 |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 7GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 11GB |

The amount of required memory proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.

1the project elements count is different due to the profiling data storing metamodel optimized in 2021x (decrease by 40-80% in SysML and UAF projects).

2these memory requirements are indicative and may vary by project specifics.

The operations that require more memory (1, 2, and 3.5 million of 2021x project elements respectively):

- UPDM migration to UAF - 10GB, 10GB, 36GB
- Converting between UAF, DoDAF2, DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, NAF4 frameworks - 8GB, 9GB, 13GB
- Project Merge - 11GB, 13GB, 21GB 3

3strongly depends on the amount of detected changes between merged versions (in this case, the specified required memory is for merging projects with 1.5k changes in total).

##### Disk Space

The recommended disk space for installation and configuration files is 3GB or more depending on used plugins, project type (local or server), and project size. In addition to this, large server projects require extra disk space for configuration files:

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Disk Space |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 19.0 | 2021x |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 10GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 30GB |

The amount of required disk space proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.

**Note:** Element History and Content History features usage requires significantly more disk space due to intensive caching at the client side.

Depending on the feature usage scenario (e.g., retrieving content history from old/distant project versions multiple times), the disk space might increase by up to 100GB.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

